regulation_name: FDA UDI System + GUDID — Class III Mandatory
key_requirements: UDI mandatory for Class III: UDI-DI and UDI-PI on device label and packaging; GUDID (Global UDI Database) submission of device identification data; direct marking of implant body where feasible (laser etching); accredited issuing agency (GS1, HIBCC, ICCBBA); compliance dates for Class III met
notes: Class III UDI compliance required since 2015; GUDID public database; direct marking of implantable required unless exemption documented; coordinate with EU UDI for harmonisation

regulation_name: FDA QSR/QMSR — Quality System Regulation 21 CFR 820
regulation_ref: 21 CFR Part 820 (QSR) / QMSR (effective Feb 2026, aligned ISO 13485)
enforcement_body: FDA CDRH + ORA (Office of Regulatory Affairs)
key_requirements: Design controls; CAPA; production and process controls; records requirements; complaint files; management controls; FDA inspection readiness; Class III manufacturers subject to frequent FDA GMP inspections; QMSR 2024 update aligns with ISO 13485:2016; MDSAP accepted in lieu of FDA inspection
notes: QMSR effective Feb 2026 replaces QSR; ISO 13485-aligned; MDSAP audit accepted by FDA for inspection substitute; Class III dental implant manufacturers = high FDA inspection priority

regulation_name: MAUDE + MDR — Adverse Event Reporting 21 CFR 803
key_requirements: Mandatory MDR reporting: death, serious injury, malfunction; manufacturer 30-day reports to FDA; 5-day reports for certain events requiring immediate action; MAUDE public database; annual baseline reports; implant failures (fracture, osseointegration failure, infection) = reportable malfunctions; MDR file maintenance
notes: MAUDE database public; dental implant adverse events extensively documented; fracture, failure to osseointegrate, peri-implantitis = reportable; vigilance data feeds FDA PMA post-approval studies

regulation_ref: California Health and Safety Code Section 25249.5 et seq. (Prop 65)
key_requirements: Warning if device exposes California consumers to listed chemicals above NSRL/MADL; dental implant alloys: vanadium (in Ti-6Al-4V), nickel (in some alloys), chromium; titanium not listed; assessment of alloy composition; reformulation or clear and reasonable warning; Prop 65 website disclosure
notes: Prop 65: pure titanium implants generally safe; Ti-6Al-4V may trigger vanadium; evaluate alloy composition against OEHHA listed chemicals list

regulation_name: NY Education Law Art. 133 — Dentist Scope (Implant Placement)
regulation_ref: NY Education Law Article 133 (Dentistry) Section 6601 et seq.
enforcement_body: NY State Education Department + Office of the Professions
key_requirements: Dental implant surgical placement and restoration restricted to licensed dentists or oral/maxillofacial surgeons; NY scope of practice rules; continuing education requirements for implant dentistry; not a device registration but affects commercial strategy and liability; NY malpractice insurance requirements
notes: Scope of practice = professional licensing; affects who can commercially place dental implants in NY; relevant for distributor/training programme design

regulation_name: Health Canada — Class IV Medical Device Licence
regulation_ref: Medical Devices Regulations SOR/98-282 (Part 2 Class IV)
key_requirements: Class IV MDL (most stringent in Canada) mandatory for dental implants; Safety and Effectiveness Information (SEI) dossier; ISO 13485 QMS certificate; Canadian Authorised Representative; MDSAP certificate accepted (preferred); MDL is product-specific; post-approval obligations; annual report; problem reporting to Health Canada
notes: Canada Class IV = highest risk category; MDSAP certificate strongly preferred by Health Canada; MDL renewable; SEI format follows Health Canada guidance

regulation_name: MDSAP — Medical Device Single Audit Program
regulation_ref: MDSAP Audit Approach Document (MDSAP AU P0002)
enforcement_body: MDSAP Council: Health Canada, FDA, TGA, ANVISA, PMDA/MHLW
key_requirements: Single QMS audit covering requirements of Health Canada, FDA, TGA, ANVISA, PMDA; MDSAP certificate accepted by all participating regulators; reduces multiple country audits to one; annual surveillance + 3-year recertification; dental implant Class III manufacturers = primary MDSAP beneficiaries; ISO 13485 basis
notes: MDSAP: Canada (mandatory preferred), FDA (accepted lieu inspection), TGA (conditional acceptance), ANVISA (mandatory), PMDA (accepted); major efficiency for Class III manufacturers

regulation_name: COFEPRIS — Registro Sanitario DM Clase III (Implante Dental)
regulation_ref: Reglamento de Insumos para la Salud (RIS) + NOM-137-SSA1-2008 + NOM-241-SSA1-2012
enforcement_body: COFEPRIS (Comision Federal para la Proteccion contra Riesgos Sanitarios)
key_requirements: Registro Sanitario mandatory for Class III implants; dossier: technical specifications, safety data, clinical evidence, Spanish labeling; Mexican Authorized Agent (Agente Tecnico); GMP certificate; CE/FDA facilitates approval; COFEPRIS inspection possible; 5-year registration renewal; Spanish-language IFU
notes: Mexico: NOM-137 for dental devices; NOM-241 for GMP; CE/FDA expedites COFEPRIS review; Class III = full COFEPRIS review; 6-12 month typical timeline

regulation_name: ANVISA RDC 751/2022 — Class III Medical Device Registration
key_requirements: Mandatory ANVISA registration (Registro) for Class III implantable devices; technical dossier per RDC 751; clinical performance data; Brazilian Authorised Representative (empresa com CNPJ); MDSAP accepted as GMP evidence; Portuguese-language labeling; UDI Brazil alignment; post-market vigilance reporting; 5-year renewal
notes: Brazil: Class III = Registro (not Cadastro); MDSAP now mandatory for GMP evidence; ANVISA RDC 751 unified DM regulation; Portuguese IFU required

regulation_ref: RDC No. 16/2013 (Boas Praticas de Fabricacao BPFM)
key_requirements: Brazilian GMP (BPFM) certification for medical device manufacturers; ANVISA GMP inspection or MDSAP certificate; Class III manufacturers priority for ANVISA inspection; GMP certificate linked to product registration; MDSAP now accepted as primary GMP evidence by ANVISA for foreign manufacturers
notes: MDSAP preferred by ANVISA post-2019; ANVISA may still conduct inspection; BPFM aligns with ISO 13485; key for Class III dental implant market access in Brazil

regulation_name: ANMAT — Disposicion 2318/02 + Class III Implant Registration
enforcement_body: ANMAT (Administracion Nacional de Medicamentos, Alimentos y Tecnologia Medica)
key_requirements: ANMAT inscription in Registro de Establecimientos y Productos; Class III: full technical dossier; CE/FDA approval facilitates ANMAT recognition; Agente Tecnico in Argentina; Spanish labeling; GMP certificate; ANMAT technical evaluation; 5-year renewal; vigilance reporting to ANMAT
notes: Argentina: ANMAT recognises CE mark for Class III; local agent mandatory; peso/import restrictions; ANMAT Disposicion 2318 = reference framework for implants

regulation_name: ISP Chile — Registro DM Clase III (Decreto Supremo 825)
regulation_ref: Decreto Supremo No. 825/1998 + Resoluciones ISP Circular 5/2022
enforcement_body: ISP (Instituto de Salud Publica de Chile)
key_requirements: ISP product registration mandatory for Class III implants; technical dossier; CE/FDA/TGA approval recognised as evidence; Chilean Authorised Representative; Spanish labeling; ISP technical evaluation; 5-year registration; post-market vigilance to ISP; ISP inspection of importers
notes: Chile: ISP registration 4-8 months; CE/FDA strongly valued; Class III = full ISP review; growing private dental implant market

regulation_name: INVIMA — Registro Sanitario DM Clase IIb/III
enforcement_body: INVIMA (Instituto Nacional de Vigilancia de Medicamentos y Alimentos)
key_requirements: INVIMA Registro Sanitario mandatory for Class IIb/III dental implants; full technical dossier; CE/FDA/ANMAT acceptance case by case; Colombian Authorised Representative; Spanish labeling; GMP evidence (ISO 13485); INVIMA technical committee review; post-market vigilance; 10-year registration
notes: Colombia: INVIMA Clase III = full review; acceptance of international approvals varies; local QC testing may be required; 10-year registration longer than most LATAM countries

regulation_name: MERCOSUR GMC Res. 40/00 — Harmonised Medical Device Rules
regulation_ref: GMC/RES No. 40/00 (Reglamento Tecnico MERCOSUR sobre Dispositivos Medicos)
enforcement_body: MERCOSUR GMC + ANVISA / ANMAT / INFARMED / INAME
key_requirements: MERCOSUR technical regulation harmonising medical device requirements across member states (Brazil, Argentina, Uruguay, Paraguay); Class III devices: aligned technical requirements; facilitates documentation reuse across MERCOSUR; mutual recognition in development; national registration still required in each country
notes: MERCOSUR res. reduces duplicative technical work across BR/AR/UY/PY; dental implant dossier can be harmonised; national registration still required separately

regulation_name: NMPA — Class III Medical Device Registration (Implant Dentaire)
regulation_ref: Medical Device Supervision and Administration Regulations (State Council Order No. 739, 2021)
key_requirements: NMPA Class III registration (most stringent); clinical evaluation: China-specific clinical trials often required; technical dossier per NMPA guidance; Chinese Authorised Agent (domestic legal entity); NMPA GMP inspection of foreign manufacturing; GB/YY standard compliance; 5-year registration; UDI China mandatory
notes: China: local clinical data usually required for Class III dental implants; NMPA review 2-4 years; Chinese manufacturing GB/YY standards; local agent = domestic company with NMPA filing

regulation_name: CMDE — Technical Review for Dental Implant Systems (China)
regulation_ref: CMDE Technical Review Guidance for Dental Implant Systems (2022)
enforcement_body: CMDE (Center for Medical Device Evaluation / NMPA)
key_requirements: CMDE conducts technical review of Class III dossier; specific CMDE guidance for dental implant systems: implant body + abutment + crown; osseointegration performance data; fatigue testing per ISO 14801/YY/T 0300; surface characterisation; material analysis; clinical data from Chinese subjects; CMDE pre-submission consultation recommended
notes: CMDE issued 2022 guidance for dental implant systems; pre-submission meeting critical; CMDE review 12-18 months; Chinese patients data often required

regulation_name: Order 739 (2021) — China Medical Device Administration Reform
regulation_ref: State Council Order No. 739 (2021) + NMPA Implementation Rules
key_requirements: Comprehensive overhaul of Chinese MDR; stricter post-market obligations; real-world data requirements; annual report mandatory; UDI (YY/T 1752) mandatory for Class III; adverse event reporting strengthened; cross-border e-commerce rules; enhanced risk classification enforcement
notes: Order 739 significantly increased compliance burden; Class III post-market obligations include annual reports, real-world data; UDI China mandatory and different from EU/US UDI

regulation_name: PMDA — Class IV Highly Controlled Medical Device (Shonin)
regulation_ref: Pharmaceutical and Medical Devices Act (PMD Act) + Cabinet Order
enforcement_body: PMDA (Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency) + MHLW
key_requirements: Class IV (highest risk in Japan) Marketing Approval (Shonin) mandatory; PMDA clinical review; GCP-compliant clinical data; Japan-specific clinical data often required; QMS audit (JPAL); PMDA pre-submission consultation recommended; approval timeline 12-24 months; MAH required
notes: Japan: dental implants = Class IV; J-pilot programme for accelerated review; PMDA consultation essential before submission; MAH must be Japanese entity

regulation_name: PMD Act — Marketing Authorisation Holder (MAH) Requirement
regulation_ref: PMD Act Chapter IV + MHLW Ordinance No. 169/2004
enforcement_body: MHLW (Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare)
key_requirements: Foreign manufacturer cannot hold Japanese Marketing Approval; must appoint Japanese MAH (Marketing Authorisation Holder); MAH legally responsible for all post-market obligations; In-country Caretaker (ICC) for foreign manufacturer; MAH signs QMS audit; product liability per Japanese PL Law; MAH change = complex transfer process
notes: MAH = legal manufacturer in Japan; due diligence on MAH selection critical; contract manufacturing/distribution agreement must specify responsibilities clearly

regulation_ref: MHLW Ministerial Ordinance No. 169/2004 (QMS Ordinance) + PMDA MDSAP Guidance
key_requirements: QMS Ordinance aligned with ISO 13485:2016; MDSAP audit accepted by Japan MHLW for foreign manufacturers; RCB or PMDA QMS conformity assessment; design development documentation per Japanese format; UDI per PMDA guidance; manufacturing site inspection
notes: Japan accepts MDSAP; QMS Ordinance = ISO 13485 aligned; MDSAP audit covers Japan requirements; significant efficiency for Class III manufacturers targeting JP market

regulation_name: MFDS — Class IV Medical Device Registration (Korea)
regulation_ref: Medical Device Act (Act No. 18610/2021) + MFDS Notifications
enforcement_body: MFDS (Ministry of Food and Drug Safety)
key_requirements: Class IV registration (highest risk in Korea): MFDS technical review of full dossier; clinical performance evidence; KGMP certificate; Korean Authorised Representative; Korean labeling; MFDS standard tests compliance; UDI registration in MFDS UDI DB; post-market surveillance; MFDS inspection of manufacturing sites
notes: Korea: dental implants Class IV in revised Medical Device Act 2021; Korea is major dental implant manufacturing country; KGMP mandatory; strong domestic standards (KS M ISO series)

regulation_ref: Medical Device Act Art. 22 + MFDS KGMP Standards (ISO 13485 aligned)
key_requirements: KGMP certification mandatory for Class IV device manufacturers (foreign + domestic); MFDS inspection of manufacturing site; KGMP aligned with ISO 13485:2016; MDSAP partially accepted; KGMP certificate issued by MFDS after inspection; annual KGMP surveillance; KGMP certificate required before product registration final approval
notes: KGMP: foreign manufacturer MFDS inspection required for Class IV; MDSAP not fully substituting KGMP in Korea; dedicated Korean inspection often required

regulation_name: HSA — Class D Medical Device Registration (Highest Risk)
regulation_ref: Health Products Act (Cap. 122D) + Health Products (Medical Devices) Regulations 2010
key_requirements: Class D registration (highest risk in Singapore): HSA full product dossier review; clinical performance data; ISO 13485 + GMP evidence; Singapore Authorised Representative; ASEAN AMDD harmonised format; HSA UDI alignment; post-market surveillance; HSA may request additional data; HSA reference agency reliance (FDA, EMA, TGA)
notes: Singapore HSA Class D = EU Class III equivalent; dental implants = Class D; HSA reliance on FDA/EMA/TGA speeds review; strong regulatory environment

regulation_name: MDA Malaysia — Class D Medical Device (Act 737/2012)
regulation_ref: Medical Device Act 2012 (Act 737) + Medical Device Regulations 2012
key_requirements: Class D registration mandatory for dental implants; product registration Form MD-7; establishment licence; Malaysian Authorised Representative; GMP evidence (ISO 13485 or MDSAP); clinical performance data; Bahasa Malaysia labeling (or English with BM translation); ASEAN AMDD aligned; post-market vigilance
notes: Malaysia MDA Class D = highest risk; ASEAN harmonisation aids multi-ASEAN strategy; dental implants = Class D per risk classification; MDA digitising registration process

regulation_name: Thai FDA — Class IV Medical Device Registration
regulation_ref: Medical Device Act B.E. 2562 (2019) + Thai FDA Notifications
enforcement_body: Thai FDA (Food and Drug Administration Thailand)
key_requirements: Class IV registration (highest risk in Thailand): Thai FDA full dossier review; clinical evidence; Thai Authorised Importer/Representative; Thai labeling (Thai + English); GMP evidence; Thai FDA inspection possible; ASEAN AMDD format considered; post-market vigilance; timeline 6-18 months
notes: Thailand: new Medical Device Act 2019 (B.E. 2562) replaced 1987 Act; dental implants = Class IV; Thai FDA registration mandatory; growing Thai private dental market

regulation_name: DAV Vietnam — Class D Medical Device Registration
regulation_ref: Decree No. 98/2021/ND-CP (Quy dinh ve quan ly trang thiet bi y te)
enforcement_body: DAV (Drug Administration of Vietnam) under MoH
key_requirements: Class D registration mandatory for dental implants (highest risk); full technical dossier; clinical evidence; Vietnamese Authorised Representative; Vietnamese labeling; DAV technical review; GMP certificate (ISO 13485); ASEAN AMDD alignment; post-market obligations; 5-year registration; timeline 6-12 months
notes: Vietnam Decree 98/2021 = current framework; Class D dental implants require DAV full review; registration mandatory before import/distribution; Vietnamese labeling required

regulation_name: BPOM/Kemenkes Indonesia — Class D Medical Device
enforcement_body: BPOM (Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan) + Kemenkes (Kementerian Kesehatan)
key_requirements: Class D registration for implantable dental devices; BPOM product registration via e-registration; technical dossier; Indonesian Authorised Agent; Indonesian labeling; GMP evidence; Kemenkes HTA involvement for public procurement; post-market vigilance; halal certification consideration for Indonesian market
notes: Indonesia: dual BPOM/Kemenkes oversight; Class D = highest risk; halal certification may be required for Islamic finance/government hospital procurement; ASEAN AMDD partially implemented

regulation_name: FDA Philippines — Class C Medical Device Certificate of Registration
enforcement_body: FDA Philippines (Food and Drug Administration CDRR)
key_requirements: Class C Certificate of Product Registration (CPR) mandatory for dental implants; technical dossier (ASEAN AMDD format); clinical evidence; Philippine Authorised Distributor; GMP evidence; Filipino labeling requirements; FDA-PH inspection possible; post-market vigilance; ASEAN AMDD aligned; 5-year CPR
notes: Philippines: Class C = second highest risk for dental implants; CPR mandatory; FDA-PH increasingly aligned with ASEAN AMDD; growing Philippine dental implant market

regulation_name: CDSCO — Medical Device Rules 2017 Class D Registration
enforcement_body: CDSCO (Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation) + DCGI
key_requirements: Class D registration (highest risk in India): Import Licence Form MD-15 for foreign devices; Manufacturing Licence Form MD-9 for domestic; clinical investigation data; technical dossier; testing at NABL-accredited laboratory; Indian Authorised Agent; CDSCO GMP inspection; labeling per MDR 2017; SUGAM portal
notes: India: dental implants notified as Class D (MDR 2017); CDSCO SUGAM portal digitalising process; local testing in NABL labs required; CDSCO review 6-18 months; significant growth market

regulation_name: CDSCO Form MD-15 (Import Licence) + Form MD-9 (Manufacturing)
enforcement_body: CDSCO + DCGI (Drug Controller General of India)
key_requirements: Form MD-15: Import Licence for Class D foreign device importers; product and site-specific; CDSCO evaluation and approval; Form MD-9: Manufacturing Licence for domestic Class D production; both require Indian Authorised Agent; site inspection by CDSCO; 3-year licence renewal; SUGAM e-portal
notes: MD-15 is per-product per-site; separate licence for each manufacturing site; renewal every 3 years; SUGAM portal mandatory for submissions; CDSCO DVDMS database

regulation_name: TGA — Class III ARTG Registration + Conformity Assessment
regulation_ref: Therapeutic Goods Act 1989 + Therapeutic Goods (Medical Devices) Regulations 2002
key_requirements: Class III registration in ARTG (Australian Register of Therapeutic Goods) mandatory; Australian Conformity Assessment Body (CAB) or TGA-performed conformity assessment; full technical dossier; clinical evidence; Australian Sponsor mandatory; ARTG entry public; MDSAP accepted by TGA; UDI alignment; vigilance reporting; annual ARTG renewal
notes: Australia TGA Class III = EU Class III equivalent; MDSAP accepted (preferred); Australian Sponsor mandatory; ARTG entry = public database; TGA CABs for Class III conformity assessment

regulation_name: Medsafe — WAND + Class III Implantable Medical Device
regulation_ref: Medicines Act 1981 + Medical Devices Safety Act 2023 (transitional)
enforcement_body: Medsafe (New Zealand Medicines and Medical Devices Safety Authority)
key_requirements: Class III device registration in WAND (Web Assisted Notification of Devices) mandatory; NZ Agent required; evidence of conformity (CE/FDA/TGA accepted as primary evidence); clinical data; English labeling; Medsafe vigilance reporting; Medsafe may request additional data for Class III implantables; WAND public database
notes: NZ: WAND registration required; Medsafe relies heavily on overseas assessments (CE/TGA/FDA); Medical Devices Safety Act 2023 transitional; NZ Agent mandatory

regulation_name: AMDD — ASEAN Medical Device Directive (Dental Implants)
regulation_ref: ASEAN Medical Device Directive (AMDD) 2015 + AHWP Guidance
key_requirements: ASEAN harmonised framework; risk classification of dental implants = highest class (D/IV/C depending on country); common technical dossier format (ASEAN CSDT); clinical evidence guidance harmonised; AMDD facilitates multi-country registration strategy in ASEAN; non-binding directive implemented via national law
notes: AMDD harmonisation eases multi-ASEAN strategy; all ASEAN members implement nationally; dental implants = highest risk class in all ASEAN systems; CSDT format saves duplicative work

regulation_name: SFDA Saudi + UAE MOH/DOH/DHA — Class C/D Implant Registration
regulation_ref: SFDA MDS-G005 (Saudi) + UAE Cabinet Decision No. 7/2017 + GCC Drug Regulatory Authority
enforcement_body: SFDA (Saudi) + UAE MoH/DHA/DOH/SCH + Kuwait MOH + Qatar MOH + Bahrain NHRA + Oman MOH
key_requirements: Separate national registrations required in each GCC country; Saudi SFDA Class C/D: full technical dossier; CE/FDA valued; UAE: multiple UAE authorities (DHA Dubai, DOH Abu Dhabi, SCH Sharjah); local agent mandatory; Arabic labeling required; GMP certificate; no unified GCC medical device registration
notes: GCC: no unified MD registration system; Saudi SFDA most developed; CE mark valued; Arabic IFU required; growing dental implant market in Saudi and UAE

regulation_name: AMAR (Israel MoH) — Class III/IV Medical Device Registration
regulation_ref: Medical Device Act 5772-2012 + AMAR Guidelines for Medical Devices
key_requirements: AMAR registration mandatory for Class III implants; technical dossier; CE or FDA approval as primary evidence; Israeli Authorised Representative; Hebrew labeling requirements; AMAR may conduct independent scientific review for Class III; post-market vigilance; IMDRF harmonisation efforts; 5-year registration
notes: Israel AMAR: Class III dental implants = full review; CE mark = strong basis; increasingly independent AMAR review; Israeli AR mandatory; Hebrew IFU required

regulation_name: EDA Egypt — Class C/D Medical Device Registration
regulation_ref: Law 151/2019 (Pharmaceutical Affairs and Medical Devices) + EDA MD regulations
key_requirements: EDA product registration mandatory for Class C/D implants; technical dossier; CE/FDA approval valued; Egyptian Authorised Agent; Arabic labeling; EDA technical review; GMP certificate; clinical evidence; post-market vigilance reporting; timeline 6-24 months
notes: Egypt EDA reorganised 2021 (formerly CAPA); Class C/D dental implants; CE mark facilitates but full dossier required; Arabic IFU; Egypt growing medical device market

regulation_name: DMP Maroc — Homologation DM Classe III
regulation_ref: Loi 17-04 portant Code du Medicament et de la Pharmacie + Circulaires DMP
enforcement_body: DMP (Direction du Medicament et de la Pharmacie) / Ministere de la Sante Maroc
key_requirements: Homologation DMP obligatoire pour DM Classe III; dossier technique (format MDR UE accepte); CE/FDA comme preuve principale; mandataire local marocain; etiquetage francais/arabe; DMP evaluation technique; delai 6-18 mois; reforme reglementaire DM en cours au Maroc
notes: Maroc: DMP aligne progressivement MDR UE; CE mark = argument principal; reglementation DM en reforme depuis 2020; mandataire local requis

regulation_name: SAHPRA — Class C/D Medical Device Registration
regulation_ref: Medical Devices and IVD Act 2021 + SAHPRA Medical Device Registration Regulations
enforcement_body: SAHPRA (South African Health Products Regulatory Authority)
key_requirements: SAHPRA Class C/D registration mandatory for dental implants; technical dossier; conformity assessment evidence (CE/FDA valued); South African Authorised Representative; English labeling; SAHPRA technical review; GMP evidence; vigilance reporting; SAHPRA reliance on international assessments promoted; 5-year registration
notes: SAHPRA: transformation underway; Class C/D = dental implants; CE/FDA = strong basis for reliance; SAHPRA aims to reduce backlog; annual post-market reporting required

regulation_name: NAFDAC — Medical Device Registration Class III (Nigeria)
regulation_ref: NAFDAC Medical Devices and Packaged Water Regulations 2018
enforcement_body: NAFDAC (National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control)
key_requirements: NAFDAC registration mandatory for imported Class III implants; technical dossier; CE/FDA/SAHPRA approval valued; Nigerian Authorised Agent (locally registered company); English labeling; NAFDAC evaluation; GMP certificate; vigilance reporting; e-registration portal (NAFDAC e-submission); 5-year renewal
notes: Nigeria: NAFDAC Class III registration; CE/FDA = key facilitators; local agent mandatory; growing private healthcare and dental implant market

regulation_name: PPB Kenya — Class C/D Medical Device Registration
regulation_ref: Pharmacy and Poisons Act Cap. 244 + PPB Medical Device Registration Guidelines 2019
key_requirements: PPB registration mandatory for Class C/D high-risk implants; technical dossier; CE/FDA approval as primary evidence; Kenyan Authorised Agent; English labeling; PPB technical review; GMP evidence; adverse event reporting to PPB; EAC East African Community harmonisation consideration; 5-year registration
notes: Kenya PPB: growing regulatory capacity; CE mark = strong basis; EAC harmonisation emerging; PPB medical device guidelines 2019 improved clarity

regulation_ref: Medical Device Administrative Control System (MDACS) under Pharmacy and Poisons Ordinance Cap. 138
enforcement_body: Medical Device Division (MDD) Department of Health Hong Kong
key_requirements: MDACS listing for Class III/IV dental implants; evidence of regulatory approval (CE/FDA/PMDA/TGA); Product Owner registration; technical summary submission; vigilance reporting to MDD; HK transitioning toward mandatory registration system; MDACS listing practically required for public hospital procurement
notes: HK: MDACS currently voluntary but mandatory system proposed (Medical Device Ordinance Bill); Class III/IV dental implants; CE/FDA = sufficient basis; HK public hospitals require MDACS listing

regulation_name: TFDA Taiwan — Class III Medical Device Registration (Highest Risk)
regulation_ref: Medical Devices Act 2020 (Act No. 11810) + TFDA Enforcement Rules
key_requirements: Class III registration (highest risk in Taiwan) mandatory; TFDA full product dossier review; clinical performance data (Taiwan or international); Taiwan Authorised Agent (domestic company); QMS certificate (ISO 13485); TFDA GMP inspection of foreign sites; Chinese labeling; UDI Taiwan alignment; post-market surveillance
notes: Taiwan TFDA Class III = most stringent; dental implants; CE/FDA valued but Taiwan-specific TFDA requirements apply; GMP inspection of foreign manufacturers for Class III

regulation_name: ISO 13485:2016 — Medical Device Quality Management System
enforcement_body: ISO + national accreditation bodies (COFRAC, UKAS, DAkkS, A2LA, JAS-ANZ, etc.)
key_requirements: QMS for medical devices: design and development controls; risk management integration; production and service controls; complaint handling; CAPA; device history records; supplier qualification; statutory requirements integration; QMS certificate by accredited CB; basis for regulatory QMS globally (MDR, FDA QMSR, Health Canada, TGA, MDSAP)
notes: ISO 13485 = global QMS standard required by all major regulators; Class III dental implant manufacturers must be certified; MDSAP audit covers ISO 13485

regulation_name: ISO 14971:2019 — Risk Management for Medical Devices
key_requirements: Risk management throughout device lifecycle; hazard identification; risk estimation and evaluation; risk control implementation; benefit-risk analysis; residual risk acceptability; production and post-production monitoring; risk management file in technical documentation; dental implant specific: osseointegration failure, fatigue fracture, corrosion, ion release, peri-implantitis
notes: ISO 14971 harmonised under EU MDR; required by FDA, Health Canada, TGA; universal risk management standard; ISO/TR 24971 provides practical guidance

enforcement_body: ISO + Notified Bodies and regulatory authorities
key_requirements: Biological evaluation of implantable devices: ISO 10993-1 evaluation strategy; ISO 10993-5 cytotoxicity; ISO 10993-6 implantation; ISO 10993-3 genotoxicity/carcinogenicity; ISO 10993-18 chemical characterisation; ISO 10993-12 sample preparation; ISO 10993-23 irritation testing; biocompatibility report for Class III dental implants mandatory
notes: All ISO 10993 series tests required for Class III implantable; dental materials (Ti, ZrO2, PEEK) well characterised but specific alloy formulations need individual assessment; genotoxicity + carcinogenicity mandatory

regulation_name: ISO 22794 + ISO 14801 — Dental Implant System Standards
key_requirements: ISO 22794: dental implant system design and performance; ISO 14801: dynamic fatigue testing of endosseous dental implants (mandatory performance test); surface characterisation; corrosion resistance; dimensional requirements; material properties for titanium grades; CMDE and NMPA reference these standards; referenced in EU MDR harmonised standards list
notes: ISO 14801 fatigue test = critical performance test for dental implants worldwide; ISO 22794 provides system-level requirements; both referenced by CMDE (China) and MDR harmonised standards

key_requirements: Codex Alimentarius covers food standards, food safety and quality; dental implants are medical devices and do not fall within Codex scope; no Codex regulation applicable to implantable medical devices
notes: Not applicable: dental implants = medical device, not food or food contact material; Codex has no remit over medical devices

regulation_ref: World Anti-Doping Code 2021 + WADA Prohibited List
key_requirements: WADA Prohibited List governs substances and methods prohibited in sport; dental implants are passive implantable devices and do not contain prohibited substances; WADA not applicable to dental implant compliance; no therapeutic use exemption required
notes: Not applicable: dental implant = passive device; no prohibited substance; no WADA doping concern; no TUE required

enforcement_body: UNECE Working Party 29 (World Forum for Harmonization of Vehicle Regulations)
key_requirements: UNECE WP.29 governs vehicle regulations and automotive standards; dental implants are implantable medical devices and fall entirely outside UNECE WP.29 scope; no UNECE regulation applicable to dental implants
notes: Not applicable: UNECE WP.29 = automotive/vehicle; dental implant = medical device; completely separate regulatory domains

key_requirements: OECD Guidelines cover chemicals testing (TG series), good laboratory practice, pharmaceutical policy; dental implant Class III medical devices are not subject to OECD binding regulations; OECD TG series may inform ISO 10993 biocompatibility methodology but are not directly applicable as regulatory requirement for dental implants
notes: Not applicable as binding regulation; OECD TG series may provide methodological guidance referenced in ISO 10993 but are not regulatory requirements for dental implants

regulation_name: Directive on VOC in decorative paints and vehicle refinishing products
key_requirements: Limite COV par catégorie produit (phase II : peintures murales intérieures max 30 g/L solv. / 75 g/L eau); étiquetage teneur max COV
notes: Régulation fondatrice EU pour les peintures déco; limites phase II en vigueur depuis 2010; révision prévue dans cadre Chemicals Strategy

regulation_name: REACH - Registration Evaluation Authorisation Restriction Chemicals
key_requirements: Restriction substances dangereuses (Annexe XVII); SVHC Candidate List notification; obligation DACS; SDS obligatoire
notes: Plomb, chrome VI, isothiazolinones et phtalates soumis à restrictions; ECHA met à jour Candidate List bi-annuellement

key_requirements: Classification et étiquetage harmonisés; pictogrammes SGH; FDS section 3; notification ECHA via C&L Inventory
notes: Applicable aux mélanges peintures; solvants et biocides conservateurs impactent la classification

regulation_name: EU Ecolabel for paints and varnishes
key_requirements: Critères COV stricts; absence substances CMR cat 1&2; teneur matières recyclées; performance minimale tests ISO; redevance annuelle
notes: Label volontaire mais exigé par marchés publics verts; critères révisés régulièrement; prochaine révision annoncée 2025

key_requirements: Autorisation substance active biocide (isothiazolinone MIT/BIT) dans conservateurs peintures; PT6 (conservateurs en pot) et PT7 (conservateurs film)
notes: MIT (methylisothiazolinone) quasi interdit en peintures intérieures UE depuis 2017; BIT soumis à évaluation en cours

regulation_name: Décret relatif à l'étiquetage des produits de construction et de revêtement de sol sur les émissions de polluants volatils
regulation_ref: Décret n° 2011-321 du 23 mars 2011 + Arrêté du 19 avril 2011
key_requirements: Classes d'émission A+/A/B/C pour 10 composés COV intérieurs (formaldéhyde TVOC acétaldéhyde…); test EN 16516 ou ISO 16000-6/9/11; étiquette obligatoire
notes: Obligatoire pour peintures vendues en France depuis 2012; parmi les réglementations indoor air les plus strictes au monde; A+ requis pour marchés publics

regulation_name: Règlement Général à la Construction (accessoires COV)
regulation_ref: Code de la Construction et de l'Habitation + Loi Grenelle II
key_requirements: Exigences qualité air intérieur dans bâtiments neufs; compatibilité avec décret 2011-321
notes: Exigence complémentaire au décret 2011-321 dans cadre rénovation énergétique et qualité air intérieur bâtiment

regulation_name: AgBB Scheme - Committee for Health-related Evaluation of Building Products
key_requirements: Tests émissions COV selon ISO 16000 après 3 et 28 jours; limites TVOC et CMR; requis pour déclarations environnementales produits
notes: Référence allemande pour indoor air; requis pour label GEV-EMICODE et accès marchés construction neuve DE

key_requirements: Faibles émissions COV; sans plastifiants sans biocides nocifs; test selon ISO 16000; critères R&D
notes: Label volontaire très reconnu sur le marché allemand; exigé pour marchés publics fédéraux; révision régulière des seuils

regulation_name: Decreto Presidente della Repubblica - produits de construction émissions
key_requirements: Transposition Directive 2004/42/EC; réglementation nationale émissions COV produits de construction; obligations étiquetage
notes: Italie a adopté des exigences spécifiques indoor air en complément de la directive EU

regulation_name: Real Decreto limitación de COV en pinturas
key_requirements: Transposition Directive 2004/42/EC; limites COV phase II; étiquetage teneur COV
notes: Espagne a transposé fidèlement la directive EU; contrôles via MITECO et CC.AA.

regulation_ref: Besluit organische oplosmiddelen verf voor professioneel gebruik (2000 & amdt)
key_requirements: Limites d'émission solvants organiques; obligations substitution; contrôles inspections
notes: Réglementation néerlandaise pro-active sur solvants; complète la directive EU 2004/42

regulation_ref: UK Paints Directive (retained) SI 2012/1715 as amended post-Brexit
key_requirements: Limites COV équivalentes Directive 2004/42/EC; étiquetage en anglais; conformité post-Brexit
notes: UK a retenu la directive EU via retained EU law post-Brexit; UK REACH parallèle à EU REACH mais liste substances diverge

regulation_ref: UK REACH (Retained EU Law REACH SI 2019/758 as amended)
key_requirements: Enregistrement substances >1t/an; restrictions analogues EU REACH Annex XVII; SVHC UK
notes: Depuis jan 2021 UK REACH indépendant; substances UK SVHC peuvent diverger de EU Candidate List

regulation_ref: UK CLP (Retained EU Law CLP Regulation 2008/1272 as amended for GB)
key_requirements: Classification étiquetage emballage chimiques; conformité avec GHS au Royaume-Uni
notes: Labels peintures UK doivent respecter UK CLP séparément de EU CLP depuis 2021

key_requirements: Alignement dynamique sur EU REACH/CLP via MRA bilatéral; restriction COV peintures parallèle à Directive 2004/42; SDS obligatoire
notes: Suisse aligne régulièrement son droit chimique sur EU; divergences possibles sur biocides (LPBio)

regulation_name: Ordonnance sur la protection de l'air (OPair)
key_requirements: Limites émissions COV solvants organiques en Suisse; valeurs COV plus strictes qu'EU dans certaines catégories
notes: Suisse peut avoir des valeurs limites COV plus restrictives que EU 2004/42 pour certaines catégories

key_requirements: Limites COV peintures; étiquetage obligatoire; conformité EEA
notes: Norvège membre EEA; applique transposition équivalente à la directive EU via espace économique européen

key_requirements: Application REACH/CLP via accord EEA; restrictions substances; SVHC notification
notes: REACH s'applique en Norvège via l'accord EEA; ECHA autorité compétente pour enregistrements

regulation_name: Technical Regulation EAEU on safety of paints and coatings
key_requirements: Déclaration de conformité obligatoire; limites substances dangereuses; exigences étiquetage; tests acccrédités EAEU
notes: Obligatoire pour mise sur marché Russie; certification par organisme accrédité; liste substances harmonisée EAEU

regulation_name: Technical Regulation EAEU on safety of paints and coatings
key_requirements: Certification EAC obligatoire; limites métaux lourds et substances toxiques; étiquetage en langues EAEU; test labs accrédités
notes: Couvre Russie + Kazakhstan + Biélorussie + Arménie + Kirghizistan; marquage EAC requis sur emballage

regulation_name: Boya ve Cila Yönetmeliği (Paint and Varnish Regulation)
key_requirements: Limites COV équivalentes EU Directive 2004/42; étiquetage en turc; harmonisation avec UE dans cadre douanes
notes: Turquie align sur réglementation EU via union douanière; application par le ministère de l'Environnement

regulation_name: State Sanitary Norms for paint and varnish materials
enforcement_body: State Service of Ukraine for Food Safety (FSQS)
key_requirements: Normes sanitaires peintures; limites substances dangereuses; obligations marquage en ukrainien
notes: Réglementation en transition post-2022; approximation des normes EU dans cadre accord d'association; certification UKRSEPRO

regulation_name: EPA Architectural Coatings Rule - AIM Rule
regulation_ref: 40 CFR Part 59 Subpart D (National Volatile Organic Compound Emission Standards)
key_requirements: Limites COV g/L par catégorie (murs intérieurs flat ≤50 g/L; non-flat ≤150 g/L); non-compliant product cannot be sold; date compliance
notes: Règle fédérale US; plus stricte pour certaines catégories depuis 1998 / amdt 2008; Etats peuvent aller plus loin (CARB)

regulation_name: TSCA - Toxic Substances Control Act Section 6
key_requirements: Restrictions sur substances chimiques dans peintures (plomb, chrome VI); exigences reporting; obligations inventaire TSCA
notes: EPA peut réglementer substances dans peintures via TSCA Section 6; solvants aromatiques sous surveillance

key_requirements: Interdiction plomb >90 ppm (0.009%) dans peintures résidentielles; tests obligatoires; certification tierce partie
notes: Loi Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act 2008 (CPSIA); seuil réduit de 600 à 90 ppm en 2011; contrôles aléatoires CPSC

regulation_name: Federal Hazardous Substances Act - FHSA labeling
key_requirements: Étiquetage produits dangereux; avertissements obligations; signal words (DANGER/WARNING/CAUTION); directions premier secours
notes: S'applique aux peintures contenant solvants inflammables ou substances corrosives/toxiques

regulation_name: CARB Suggested Control Measure + SCAQMD Rule 1113
key_requirements: Limites COV plus strictes (flat ≤50 g/L; non-flat ≤100 g/L); liste compounds exempt révisée; restrictions HAP; certains comtés plus stricts encore
notes: California leader mondial; CARB SCM révisé régulièrement; SCAQMD Rule 1113 spécifique Sud Californie; Prop 65 requiert avertissements lead/formaldéhyde

regulation_ref: California Health and Safety Code Section 25249.5 et seq.
key_requirements: Avertissements cancérigènes/reprotoxiques si seuils NSRL/MADL dépassés; substances listées (plomb, TiO2 inhaled, formaldéhyde, PGME-P)
notes: Safe Harbor warnings requis si plomb >0.5 µg/day exposure ou formaldéhyde >40 µg/day; mises à jour liste fréquentes

regulation_name: New York DEC Architectural Coatings VOC Rule
enforcement_body: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (DEC)
key_requirements: Limites COV architectural coatings alignées sur OTC Model Rule; vente NY doit respecter ces limites
notes: NY member of OTC (Ozone Transport Commission); adopte limites COV modèles; moins strict que CA mais plus que fédéral EPA

regulation_name: Volatile Organic Compound Concentration Limits for Architectural Coatings Regulations
key_requirements: Limites COV par catégorie (interior flat ≤50 g/L; non-flat ≤150 g/L); obligations étiquetage et tenue de registres
notes: Harmonisation avec EPA AIM rule; révision 2018 a aligné certaines catégories; bilingual labeling (EN/FR) requis

key_requirements: Limites plomb <90 ppm; cadmium baryum chrome VI; peintures résidentielles et jouets
notes: Exigence parallèle à CPSC américaine; ECCC et Health Canada autorités compétentes

key_requirements: Spécifications techniques peintures intérieures; tests performance; limites substances dangereuses; étiquetage espagnol
notes: NOM vs NMX: NMX volontaire mais généralement exigée pour certification ANCE / accès marchés publics

regulation_name: Ley Federal de Protección al Consumidor + NOM étiquetage chimique
key_requirements: Étiquetage produits chimiques dangereux; fiches de données sécurité en espagnol; obligations information consommateur
notes: NOM-018-STPS-2015 système d'harmonisation de classification étiquetage chimiques (SGA/GHS Mexique)

regulation_name: INMETRO - Portaria sobre compostos orgânicos voláteis em tintas
key_requirements: Limites COV tintas imobiliárias; certificação compulsória compostos perigosos; labs accrédités
notes: Brésil a institué certification compulsoire via INMETRO pour certains types de peintures

key_requirements: Spécifications techniques tintas imobiliárias; classes performance; tests applicabilité
notes: Norme technique brésilienne de référence pour peintures immobilières; alignée sur pratiques ASTM/ISO

regulation_name: IBAMA - controle de substâncias perigosas em tintas
regulation_ref: Lei 6938/81 + Resolução CONAMA + Instrução Normativa IBAMA
key_requirements: Contrôle substances chimiques dangereuses; restrictions pigments à base plomb/mercure/cadmium; importation chimiques
notes: IBAMA contrôle l'importation et l'usage de substances dangereuses; interface avec cadastro técnico federal

key_requirements: Restrictions substances peintures dangereuses; plomb <90 ppm peintures résidentielles; certification INTI
notes: Argentine a aligné sur standard CPSC pour plomb dans peintures résidentielles; IRAM normes techniques de référence

regulation_name: DS 47/92 - Ordenanza General de Urbanismo y Construcciones + NCh
key_requirements: Exigences peintures dans construction; normes techniques chilennnes pour revêtements; limites substances dangereuses
notes: Chili en cours d'adoption standards plus proches OCDE; NCh normes volontaires souvent exigées sur marchés

regulation_name: MAVDT + Normas Técnicas Colombianas NTC peintures
key_requirements: Réglementation substances dangereuses peintures; limites plomb; NTC normes techniques pour tintas arquitectónicas
notes: Colombie progressivement aligne sur standards CPSC/EPA; MADS autorité substances chimiques

regulation_ref: Resolución GMC n°56/92 + AME (Acuerdo Marco Ambiental Mercosur)
key_requirements: Harmonisation normative peintures dans espace Mercosur; reconnaissance croisée normes ABNT/IRAM/UNIT; travaux en cours
notes: Harmonisation partielle; chaque Etat membre conserve réglementations nationales propres

regulation_name: Norme nationale peintures intérieures - substances nocives
regulation_ref: GB 18581-2020 (Harmful Substances Limits for Solvent-borne Coatings)
key_requirements: Limites COV peintures solvants (max 550 g/L); benzène <0.3%; méthanol <0.3%; TVOC mesure obligatoire
notes: GB 18581 révisé 2020 renforcé; applicable aussi aux peintures professionnelles; contrôle par CQC et labs agréés

regulation_name: Norme nationale peintures murales intérieures à l'eau
regulation_ref: GB 18582-2020 (Harmful Substances Limits for Architectural Coating)
key_requirements: Limites COV peintures eau intérieures (max 80 g/L); formaldéhyde <50 mg/kg; alcylphénol éthoxylés; APE limités
notes: GB 18582 révisé 2020 avec renforcement limites formaldéhyde et introduction limite APE; test méthodes GB/T

regulation_name: GB/T 23984 + GB 24410 - Peintures intérieures à faibles émissions
key_requirements: Labels basse émission; critères optionnels pour certification China Environmental Label (Chine-ENV) et ten-ring label
notes: Ten-ring label (中国环境标志) = écolabel chinois; critères GB/T 23984 base pour évaluation indoor air; souvent exigé marchés publics CN

regulation_name: Air Pollution Control Ordinance + VOC Regulation
regulation_ref: Air Pollution Control (Volatile Organic Compounds) Regulation (Cap. 311L)
key_requirements: Limites COV architectural coatings; registration obligatoire; teneur VOC label; contrôles marché
notes: HK a adopté réglementation VOC spécifique; produits avec COV >légal ne peuvent être vendus; inspections EPD

regulation_name: Air Pollution Control Act + EPA VOC regulations
regulation_ref: Air Pollution Control Act (APCA) + EPA notification rules
key_requirements: Limites COV peintures architecturales; notification préalable; étiquetage conformité; contrôles inspection
notes: Taiwan EPA applique réglementation VOC pour peintures architecturales; en cours de renforcement alignement sur standards internationaux

regulation_name: Building Standard Law - Indoor Air Quality
regulation_ref: Building Standard Law (建築基準法) Article 28-2 + Notification 1113 + 1114
enforcement_body: Ministry of Land Infrastructure Transport and Tourism (MLIT)
key_requirements: Réglementation émissions formaldéhyde et chlorpyrifos bâtiments; matériaux classés F☆☆☆☆ à F☆; F☆☆☆☆ requis usage illimité
notes: Système étoiles Fstar: F☆☆☆☆ = niveau le plus bas émission; peintures doivent être classifiées; CARIEA test protocol

regulation_name: JIS K 5663 - Peintures émulsions pour construction
key_requirements: Spécifications techniques peintures émulsion pour bâtiments; tests performance; classification formaldéhyde
notes: Norme JIS de référence pour peintures émulsion intérieures; souvent requise pour projets construction japonais

regulation_ref: Act on Confirmation of Release Amount of Specific Chemical Substances (Law No.86/1999 amdt 2022)
key_requirements: Déclaration rejets substances chimiques PRTR; substances listées dans peintures (toluène xylène éthylbenzène etc.)
notes: Obligation déclaration pour fabricants peintures utilisant >1t substances PRTR listées

regulation_name: Indoor Air Quality Control Act (다중이용시설 등의 실내공기질 관리법)
regulation_ref: Act on Indoor Air Quality Control in Public Use Facilities + Enforcement Decree
key_requirements: Limites émissions polluants intérieurs (formaldéhyde TVOC benzène toluène…); matériaux bâtiment testés; certification obligatoire
notes: MOE Korea publie liste matériaux conformes; peintures doivent obtenir certification; tests labs accrédités KAS

regulation_ref: Ecolabel Certification Criteria for Interior Paints (KE-102)
enforcement_body: Korea Environmental Industry and Technology Institute (KEITI)
key_requirements: Critères écolabel coréen peintures intérieures; limites TVOC formaldéhyde; absence substances SVHC Korea; performance tests
notes: Label volontaire mais exigé pour Green Public Procurement Korea (녹색제품); critères révisés périodiquement

regulation_name: Singapore Green Label Scheme (SGLS) for paints
regulation_ref: Green Label standard for paints - Singapore Environment Council (SEC)
key_requirements: Critères faibles émissions COV; absence substances dangereuses; test selon ISO 16000; audit annuel certification
notes: Label volontaire très reconnu à Singapour; exigé par Green Mark scheme BCA pour bâtiments; révision périodique critères

regulation_name: Singapore NEA - Environmental Protection and Management Act chemicals
regulation_ref: Environmental Protection and Management Act (EPMA) + Regulations
key_requirements: Contrôle substances chimiques dangereuses; enregistrement chimiques industriels; obligations importateurs
notes: NEA régule substances chimiques dangereuses; importateurs peintures doivent notifier pour certaines substances

regulation_ref: SIRIM Eco-label criteria for paints - MS 1550
key_requirements: Critères écolabel malaisien pour peintures; faibles émissions; limites substances dangereuses; certification SIRIM
notes: Label volontaire mais progressivement exigé pour projets gouvernementaux GreenRE certified buildings

regulation_name: Environmental Quality Act - Schedule of Scheduled Wastes
key_requirements: Gestion déchets peintures (SW304); obligations traitement; interdiction rejet environnement
notes: Peintures et solvants usagés = déchets programmés; obligation manifeste de transfert; pertinent pour fabricants et distributeurs

regulation_name: Thai Industrial Standards TIS 2737-2559 (2016) - peintures intérieures à l'eau
key_requirements: Spécifications peintures émulsion intérieures; performance; limites substances dangereuses; certification TISI obligatoire
notes: TISI certification obligatoire pour peintures architecturales en Thaïlande; contrôles douane et marché

regulation_ref: Notification PCD + Enhancement and Conservation of National Environmental Quality Act
key_requirements: Contrôle émissions COV produits de peinture; limitations activités peinture industrielle; surveillance qualité air
notes: PCD Thaïlande développe réglementation spécifique VOC peintures; en alignement progressif avec standards ASEAN

regulation_ref: QCVN 16:2019/BXD (National Technical Regulation on Construction Products)
key_requirements: Conformité produits de construction dont peintures; test par labs désignés; déclaration conformité CR; limite substances nocives
notes: QCVN 16:2019 remplace QCVN 16:2014; certification obligatoire pour importation et distribution

regulation_name: Décret sur substances chimiques dangereuses dans peintures
key_requirements: Déclaration substances chimiques dangereuses; permis importation; inventaire substances; obligations notification
notes: Decree 113 implémente Chemical Law Vietnam; peintures avec solvants dangereux soumis à déclaration et permis

key_requirements: Spécifications peintures murales émulsion; tests performance; limites plomb <90 ppm; certification SNI obligatoire
notes: SNI 3564 certification obligatoire via LS Pro accrédité; contrôles KLHK pour substances dangereuses environnement

regulation_name: KLHK - Peraturan VOC et B3 substances dangereuses
enforcement_body: Kementerian LHK (Ministry of Environment and Forestry)
key_requirements: Réglementation substances berbahaya beracun (B3) dont solvants; obligations gestion; limites émissions industrielles
notes: B3 = substances dangereuses réglementées; fabricants peintures solvantées doivent gérer déchets B3

enforcement_body: Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) / DTI-BPS
key_requirements: Standards techniques peintures Philippines; certification DTI-PS mark; limites plomb <90 ppm
notes: DTI gère certification PS mark pour peintures; alignement progressif sur standards ASTM/ISO

regulation_name: Toxic Substances and Hazardous Waste Act
key_requirements: Contrôle substances toxiques dans produits; Chemical Control Orders pour plomb cadmium; obligations importateurs
notes: RA 6969 base légale pour restriction substances dans peintures; DENR peut émettre CCO sur substances spécifiques

key_requirements: Spécifications peintures émulsion intérieures; tests performances; limites plomb; certification BIS obligatoire pour certaines catégories
notes: Certification BIS obligatoire pour peintures sous Quality Control Orders (QCO) MoC&I; liste QCO élargie depuis 2023

regulation_name: CPCB VOC guidelines for architectural coatings
key_requirements: Lignes directrices limites COV peintures architecturales; émissions solvants industries peintures
notes: CPCB a émis guidelines non-contraignantes sur VOC peintures; évolution vers réglementation obligatoire en cours

enforcement_body: Department of Industry Science Resources and Energy
key_requirements: Schéma approbation peintures gouvernement australien; critères performance + environnementaux; SVHC restrictions
notes: APAS obligatoire pour marchés publics fédéraux; spécifications incluent limites COV et substances SVHC; NICNAS/AICIS contrôle substances nouvelles

regulation_name: AICIS - Australian Industrial Chemicals Introduction Scheme
key_requirements: Enregistrement substances chimiques industrielles; categorisation introduction; obligations importateurs fabricants; SVHC assessment
notes: AICIS remplace NICNAS depuis 1er juillet 2020; substances nouvelles dans peintures doivent être évaluées/catégorisées

regulation_name: National Environment Protection (Air Toxics) Measure + VOC
regulation_ref: NEPM (Air Toxics) 2004 + state EPAs VOC provisions
enforcement_body: State/Territory EPAs (EPA Vic NSW QLD etc.)
key_requirements: Limites émissions COV industrielles; objectifs qualité air ambiant pour BTX (benzène toluène xylène); réglementation états
notes: Chaque état australien peut avoir réglementation VOC propre pour fabricants; Victoria et NSW les plus stricts

regulation_ref: Environmental Choice NZ standard EN14 (paints and coatings)
key_requirements: Critères écolabel néo-zélandais peintures; faibles COV; sans biocides nocifs; performance tests; certification annuelle
notes: Label volontaire reconnu; exigé pour Green Star NZ building certification; critères alignés sur global GEN network

regulation_name: Hazardous Substances and New Organisms Act (HSNO)
key_requirements: Classification substances dangereuses; approbation groupes peintures; contrôles étiquetage; Emergency Response Codes
notes: HSNO régule solvants dans peintures; groupes d'approbation pour peintures alkydes et produits solventés

regulation_ref: ASEAN Chemical Industries Council (ACIC) MoU + ASEAN Chemicals Control System
key_requirements: Harmonisation progressivement des classifications chimiques; échange information substances dangereuses; GHS adoption
notes: Cadre coopération non-contraignant; chaque Etat ASEAN conserve réglementations nationales; harmonisation GHS en cours

key_requirements: Limites substances dangereuses (plomb <90 ppm); spécifications techniques peintures; certification SASO obligatoire; conformité UAE ESMA
notes: Arabie Saoudite: SASO certification + SALEEM scheme; UAE: ESMA Green Building réglementations renforcées; Qatar/Kuwait alignés GSO

regulation_name: UAE ESMA Green Building regulations for paints
regulation_ref: UAE Federal Law + Emirates Authority for Standardization (ESMA) standards
enforcement_body: ESMA / UAE Ministry of Climate Change
key_requirements: Certification produits peintures éco-responsables dans bâtiments UAE; limites COV pour Estidama Pearl Rating System
notes: Estidama Pearl (UAE) et LEED ME requièrent peintures faibles COV; ESMA élabore standards spécifiques

regulation_name: Israeli Standard SI 1923 - Water-based paints VOC
key_requirements: Limites COV peintures à base eau; classification émissions; étiquetage obligatoire; tests selon méthodes SII/ISO
notes: Norme israélienne spécifique aux peintures eau intérieures; s'aligne sur Directive EU 2004/42 et standards EU

regulation_ref: ES 4007 (Egyptian Standard for paints and varnishes)
enforcement_body: Egyptian Organization for Standards and Quality (EOS)
key_requirements: Spécifications techniques peintures; tests performance; limites plomb et substances dangereuses; homologation EOS
notes: Certification EOS/GOEIC requise pour importation; Egypte progressivement renforce exigences chimiques

key_requirements: Spécifications peintures bâtiment; tests performances; limites substances; marquage et emballage
notes: IMANOR gère normes marocaines; certification obligatoire progressivement étendue; Maroc alignement normes EU via accord association

regulation_ref: SANS 1881 (paint and coatings) + SABS method tests
key_requirements: Spécifications peintures pour bâtiments; limites substances dangereuses (plomb Cr VI); tests performance; certification SABS
notes: SABS Mark certification volontaire mais souvent exigée marchés publics ZA; limites plomb 90 ppm alignées CPSC

regulation_name: South Africa Chemical Control + NEMA regulations
regulation_ref: National Environmental Management Act 1998 + Chemical Control under NEMA
key_requirements: Contrôle substances chimiques dangereuses; obligations notification importateurs; gestion déchets peintures
notes: DFFE (ex DEAT) administre Chemical Control; peintures avec solvants soumises à déclarations spécifiques

regulation_name: SON NIS 100 - Nigerian standard for paints
key_requirements: Spécifications peintures émulsion; limites plomb <90 ppm; certification SON obligatoire; contrôles marché
notes: SON certification obligatoire au Nigéria; contrôles douaniers renforcés depuis 2019; NAFDAC peut intervenir pour substances toxiques

regulation_ref: KS 24:2019 (Kenya Standard for paints) + KEBS PVoC scheme
key_requirements: Pre-export verification of conformity (PVoC); certificat d'inspection requis avant embarquement; limites substances
notes: Kenya PVoC obligatoire pour peintures importées; inspection avant embarquement par agents agréés (SGS/Intertek/Bureau Veritas)

regulation_name: ISO 11890 - Paints and varnishes - determination of VOC content
enforcement_body: ISO Technical Committee TC 35 (Paints and varnishes)
key_requirements: Méthodes de mesure teneur COV peintures; Part 1 méthode calcul; Part 2 chromatographie gazeuse; référence mondiale
notes: Référence normative pour toutes les réglementations VOC mondiales; adoptée par EU US AU et autres dans leurs tests

regulation_name: ISO 16000 - Indoor air quality - emissions from building products
key_requirements: Méthodes mesure émissions COV produits construction; chambre 1m³ test; référence pour tests indoor air
notes: ISO 16000 = méthode test de référence pour labels indoor air (EU FR DE KR SG etc.); adoptée internationalement

regulation_name: OECD Test Guidelines for VOC - Chemical Testing
regulation_ref: OECD TG 114 + OECD VOC definition and guidelines
key_requirements: Définition substances COV pour réglementation; lignes directrices test produits chimiques; échange données entre pays membres
notes: OECD définition COV (point ébullition <250°C) adoptée par EU et autres pays OCDE dans leurs réglementations

regulation_name: Codex Alimentarius - non applicable peintures déco
key_requirements: Non applicable aux peintures décoratives intérieures (secteur agroalimentaire uniquement)
notes: Codex Alimentarius ne couvre pas les peintures; pas de régulation applicable

key_requirements: Non applicable aux peintures décoratives intérieures (concerne substances dopage sport)
notes: WADA ne couvre pas les peintures; pas de régulation applicable

regulation_name: UNECE - non applicable peintures déco intérieure
enforcement_body: UNECE World Forum for Harmonization of Vehicle Regulations
key_requirements: Non applicable aux peintures décoratives intérieures résidentielles (WP.29 = réglementation véhicules)
notes: UNECE WP.29 couvre peintures de carrosserie véhicules et non peintures décoratives intérieures résidentielles

regulation_name: Regulation on placing on market and use of feed
key_requirements: Labelling (species particulars indicative analytical constituents directions for use), permitted materials, claims restrictions
notes: Core EU pet food marketing regulation

key_requirements: Classification of by-products (Cat 1/2/3), approved processing methods, traceability, import conditions
notes: Governs animal-derived ingredients in pet food

key_requirements: Authorisation of additives (preservatives vitamins minerals), labelling of additives, maximum levels
notes: Applies to all additives used in pet food

regulation_name: Regulation on official controls in feed and food
key_requirements: Audit and inspection framework, import border control posts, notification obligations, corrective actions
notes: Superseded Regulation 882/2004 for controls

key_requirements: HACCP for feed business operators, hygiene requirements for facilities, registration/approval
notes: Applies to manufacturers and importers

key_requirements: Electronic health certificates, CHED-D for feed of animal origin, traceability records
notes: Mandatory for intra-EU and import movements

key_requirements: Permitted feed materials list, names and descriptions, labelling of materials
notes: Reference list for ingredients declaration

regulation_name: Code rural et de la pêche maritime — alimentation animale + contrôles DGAL
key_requirements: National transposition of EU feed rules, DGAL inspection plans, operator registration (SIGAL database)
notes: France enforces EU rules via DGAL; annual inspection plan

regulation_name: EU Wine Sector Single CMO — Authorised Oenological Practices
enforcement_body: EC DG AGRI + national wine authorities
key_requirements: Delegated regulation defining permitted oenological practices for EU wines; authorised additives (sulfur dioxide max levels - 150-200 mg/L red, 200-250 mg/L white); sweetening rules; enrichment limits per wine zone; deacidification permitted in northern zones only; lees ageing rules; permitted analytical and sensory characteristics
notes: Annex IA/IB list of permitted treatments; Mevushal wine for kosher market separate ; PIWI varieties (disease resistant) authorised by 2021/2117

regulation_name: EU Geographical Indications Reform — New GI Regulation 2024
key_requirements: New unified EU GI regulation for wine, spirits, agricultural products effective 2024; consolidates and simplifies GI protection; strengthens enforcement against online infringement (search engines, marketplaces); new EUIPO competence for GI examination; sustainability dimension mandatory; producer groups powers expanded; ex officio protection by member states
notes: Applies from 13 May 2024; replaces 1308/2013 GI provisions for wine; EUIPO now manages examination phase; GI Champagne, Bordeaux, Rioja, Chianti benefit from stronger online protection

regulation_name: Decret Champagne — Cahier des charges AOC
regulation_ref: Decret du 23 octobre 2010 modifie (AOC Champagne) + reglements specifiques
enforcement_body: INAO + Comite Interprofessionnel du Vin de Champagne (CIVC)
key_requirements: Cahier des charges AOC Champagne: zone delimitee (Marne, Aube, Aisne); cepages autorises (Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, Pinot Meunier + 4 autorises minoritaires); rendement maximum 10400 kg/ha; pressurage 102L/160kg; vieillissement minimum 15 mois sur lattes (millesime 36 mois); seconde fermentation en bouteille obligatoire (methode traditionnelle); deuxieme etiquette code producteur (NM/RM/CM/SR) obligatoire
notes: Champagne = AOC la plus protegee au monde; CIVC actions juridiques systematiques contre usurpations (Champagne pour fromage, parfum, etc.); production max 320M bouteilles/an; nouveaux cepages resistants Voltis autorises 2021

regulation_name: Code de la consommation — Etiquetage allergenes vins (sulfites)
regulation_ref: Code de la consommation art. R412-12 + arrete 2 oct. 2024
key_requirements: Mention 'contient des sulfites' ou 'contient du dioxyde de soufre' obligatoire si teneur SO2 superieure a 10 mg/L; transposition directe FIC; obligation etiquetage en francais; mention allergenes oenologie (oeuf, lait, isinglass) si residus detectables; arrete 2024 precise modalites e-label francais pour ingredients/nutrition
notes: Mention sulfites quasi systematique (>99% vins commerciaux); collage oeuf/caseine doit etre declare; alternative vegan emerge (bentonite, PVPP); arrete 2024 valide QR code conforme art. 9 FIC

key_requirements: Pradikatswein hierarchy by must weight (Oechsle): Kabinett (70-85 Oe), Spaetlese (76-95), Auslese (83-105), Beerenauslese (110-128), Eiswein (110-128, harvested frozen), Trockenbeerenauslese (150-154); each level different in viticulture region; no chaptalisation permitted for Pradikatswein; manual harvest mandatory for higher categories; AP-Nr testing required
notes: Pradikatswein = premium German wine category; Eiswein increasingly rare due to climate change (mild winters); TBA = world's most expensive sweet wines; Pradikat panel tasting subjective but rigorous

key_requirements: VDP private four-tier vineyard classification: VDP.Gutswein (estate wine), VDP.Ortswein (village), VDP.Erste Lage (Premier Cru), VDP.Grosse Lage (Grand Cru = Grosses Gewachs/GG); voluntary but market-leading; GG dry wine from Grosse Lage minimum quality requirements; only Riesling/Spaetburgunder/Weissburgunder/Grauburgunder/Lemberger/Silvaner/Chardonnay permitted by region; yield restrictions 50-60 hl/ha for GG
notes: VDP not legally required but commercial benchmark; ca 200 estates members; GG launch first Sunday of September; some regions debate VDP overlap with regulatory GG concept introduced 2020 amendment

regulation_ref: DM 14 luglio 2020 (modifica Disciplinare Prosecco DOC) + Consorzio Tutela Prosecco DOC
enforcement_body: Consorzio di Tutela Prosecco DOC + MIPAAF + ICQRF
key_requirements: Prosecco DOC: area produzione 9 provincie Veneto + Friuli-Venezia Giulia; Glera minimum 85% (autorises Verdiso, Bianchetta, Perera, Glera lunga, Chardonnay, Pinot Bianco/Grigio/Nero max 15%); rendement max 18 t/ha; titolo alcoolometrico min 9% (10.5% Spumante); fermentazione Charmat-Martinotti (autoclave); pas de methode traditionnelle pour DOC; lotto e annata facoltativi; ICQRF random sampling
notes: Prosecco DOC = approx 600M bottles/an = plus grosse appellation mondiale; conflit avec Croatia 'Prosek' (vin doux dalmate) reconnaissance UE 2024; sous-zone Asolo et Conegliano-Valdobbiadene = DOCG separes; bouchon a vis autorise 2018

regulation_name: Sistema fascette di Stato — Sigillo DOCG
enforcement_body: Poligrafico e Zecca dello Stato + Consorzi DOCG + ICQRF
key_requirements: Sigilo neck label statale obligatorio per ogni bottiglia DOCG; numerazione sequenziale; emessa Poligrafico Zecca; tracciamento elettronico vinificazione → imbottigliamento → vendita; consorzio richiede fascette in numero proporzionale a volume dichiarato; controllo anti-frode; counterfeit fascette = reato penale; costo per bottiglia variabile (1-5 centesimi); fascette diverse per colore secondo DOCG
notes: Fascetta DOCG = elemento distintivo italiano unico al mondo; Brunello, Barolo, Amarone, Chianti Classico tutti con fascetta; falsificazione storica e ora prevenuta da hologramma e QR code; introduzione fascetta digitale (blockchain) in studio 2025

regulation_name: Reglamento DOCa Rioja — Pliego de condiciones
regulation_ref: Orden APA/415/2004 + modificaciones + pliego DOCa Rioja
key_requirements: Rioja DOCa: zona produccion La Rioja + Alava + Navarra; tres subzonas Rioja Alta, Rioja Alavesa, Rioja Oriental; Tempranillo principal (variedad tinta autorizada 90%); 14 variedades autorizadas; rendimiento maximo 6500 kg/ha tintos; titulo alcoholico min 11% (10.5% blancos); categorias envejecimiento: Generico, Crianza (24 meses min, 12 en barrica), Reserva (36/12), Gran Reserva (60/24); etiqueta contraetiqueta numerada del Consejo obligatoria
notes: Rioja = primera DOCa de Espana (1991); volumen approx 280M bottiglie/an; contraetiqueta numerata Consejo critica per traceabilita; recente categoria Vino de Pueblo (village wine) e Vinedo Singular (single vineyard) introducidas 2017

regulation_ref: Real Decreto 1338/2011 + RD 738/2015 + RD 1363/2018 (Cava de Paraje Calificado)
key_requirements: Cava DO: vino espumoso metodo tradicional; zona productora Cataluña + 7 otras CCAA; Macabeo, Xarel-lo, Parellada (variedades tradicionales); Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, Subirat autorizadas; segunda fermentacion en botella obligatoria; minimo 9 meses sobre lias (15 Reserva, 30 Gran Reserva); Cava de Paraje Calificado = nueva categoria premium (vinedo unico, minimo 36 meses sobre lias, manual harvest)
notes: Cava DO denominacion mas grande Cataluna; conflict con Penedes/Corpinnat (algunos productores premium salieron 2019); Cava de Paraje Calificado = top tier (12 paraje aprobados); methode champenoise nominacion prohibida (Sentencia TJUE Champagne v Cava)

key_requirements: Nederland: wijnetikettering moet voldoen aan FIC 1169/2011 en NL Warenwet; verplichte vermelding in Nederlands of meertalig (incl. NL); allergenenvermelding (sulfieten >10 mg/L); voedingswaarde en ingredientenlijst per dec 2023; e-label QR code toegestaan voor ingredienten; minimum lettergrootte 1.2mm x-height; NVWA marktcontroles; etikettering taal eis (NL of EU-erkend)
notes: NVWA controles risicogebaseerd; Nederlandse wijnimporteurs aansprakelijk voor labelconformiteit; e-label QR adoptie hoog in NL markt; Dutch wine production minor (Limburg, Gelderland) maar grawing

regulation_name: Verkeerswet alcoholbeleid — Verlichte handhaving online verkoop
regulation_ref: Wet op de Accijns + Drank- en Horecawet 2013 + Wijziging 2021 (online aankoop)
key_requirements: Verzwaring controles online alcoholverkoop sinds 2021: leeftijdsverificatie via betrouwbaar systeem (iDIN, paspoort scan); twee-momentcheck (bestelling + bezorging); bezorger moet leeftijd controleren; verkoper aansprakelijk; boetes tot EUR 4.500; online retailers (bijv Gall&Gall, supermarkten) compliant; minimum leeftijd 18 jaar voor alle alcohol; geen onderscheid wijn/bier/sterk meer (sinds 2014)
notes: Online wijnverkoop sterke groei; NL = belangrijke EU e-commerce hub wijn; leeftijdsverificatie risico-gebaseerd; gemeenten extra regels mogelijk (sluitingstijden, dichtheid)

regulation_name: UK Wine Standards Branch — Wine Standards Inspectorate
regulation_ref: UK Wine Regulations 2011 (SI 2011/2936) + Food Safety Act 1990
enforcement_body: Food Standards Agency (FSA) + Wine Standards Branch + Trading Standards
key_requirements: FSA Wine Standards Branch (WSB) - dedicated wine compliance inspectorate; pre-Brexit was EU-aligned, now UK retained law; mandatory wine sampling and analytical testing; vine register (UK English & Welsh wine producers); analytical conformity (ABV declared accurate within 0.5%); contamination testing; cellar inspections; certified UK PDO/PGI verification; export certificate (VI-1 replaced with simplified UK form for some markets)
notes: WSB inspects approx 800 UK wine businesses annually; English sparkling wine industry boom (250+ vineyards); UK PDO/PGI scheme: 6 PDOs + 5 PGIs registered post-Brexit; VI-1 form abolished for EU imports 2021

key_requirements: AWRS = mandatory registration for all businesses wholesaling alcohol in UK (including wine); excluded: producers selling own products only; Fit and Proper test (financial soundness, anti-fraud); URN (Unique Reference Number) assigned; URN must be communicated to trade buyers; failure to register = criminal offence; AML compliance integrated; spot checks; revocation possible for non-compliance
notes: AWRS introduced 2017 to combat alcohol duty fraud (estimated GBP 1bn/year pre-AWRS); over 8000 registered businesses; URN must appear on invoices; non-registered wholesalers cannot sell to retailers/restaurants/bars legally

regulation_name: Ordonnance sur les denrees alimentaires et les objets usuels — vin
regulation_ref: ODAlOUs (RS 817.02) + Ordonnance DFI sur les boissons alcooliques (RS 817.022.110)
key_requirements: Suisse OSAV regule etiquetage vin: mentions obligatoires en allemand/francais/italien (3 langues nationales selon canton ventes); ABV avec tolerance ±0.5%; lot, contenance, ABV, identite fabricant/importateur, allergenes (sulfites >10 mg/L); origine geographique; conformite FIC pour ingredients/nutrition depuis dec 2023; conditions stockage; pas d'allegation sante
notes: Suisse non-UE/EEE mais ODAlOUs harmonise sur reglement UE 1169/2011; etiquetage trilingue souvent en pratique; OSAV controles cantonaux; importateur suisse responsable conformite

regulation_name: Loi sur l'agriculture — Importation vin contingents tarifaires
regulation_ref: LAgr (RS 910.1) art. 21 + Ordonnance importation vin RS 916.140
key_requirements: Contingent tarifaire annuel d'importation vin: 1.7 million hectolitres total (vin blanc + rouge + mousseux + non-conditionne); allocation par sub-quotas selon historique importateurs; au-dela contingent tarif douanier eleve (CHF 50-60/100kg); accords bilateraux UE-CH facilitent acces (origines preferentielles); regle EUR.1 ou declaration sur facture; AFD perçoit droits + TVA 8.1% (taux normal)
notes: Suisse marche protege production locale (15M bouteilles/an); importation 200M bouteilles/an majoritairement France, Italie, Espagne; contingent partiellement disponible aux nouveaux importateurs (10% annuel); negociations UE-CH continues

regulation_name: Forskrift om alkoholholdig drikk — Etikettering Norge
key_requirements: Norvege EEE: etikettering vin harmonisee avec reglement UE 1169/2011 (FIC); mention obligatoires en norvegien ou langue scandinave comprehensible; ABV, contenance, allergenes (sulfitter), lot, origine; conformite FIC ingredients/nutrition dec 2023; Vinmonopolet listing requires conformity check; pre-market notification possible; pictogramme grossesse non obligatoire (volontaire); mention 'inneholder sulfitt' courante
notes: Norvege EEE applique acquis UE alimentation/etiquetage; norsk language requirement pas strict; etiquetage trilingue (NO/SE/DK) souvent utilise pour marche scandinave; Vinmonopolet preselection avant listing commercial

regulation_name: Alkoholavgift Norge — Excise duty wine high rate
regulation_ref: Stortingsvedtak om saeravgift alkoholholdige drikkevarer (annual) + Saeravgiftsforskriften
key_requirements: Norvege: excise tres elevee sur vin = approx NOK 569/litre alcool pur (= approx EUR 50/litre); pour vin 12% ABV bouteille 75cl = approx NOK 51 (EUR 4.50) just d'excise; +VAT 25%; pre-market notification import obligatoire; etiquette excise obligatoire (banderole); paiement excise au passage frontiere ou warehouse approuve; revue annuelle Stortinget; tres haute pression fiscale = un des plus chers vins UE
notes: Norvege = excise alcool plus haute Europe; vin 75cl couramment NOK 200+ (EUR 18+) prix detail; Vinmonopolet capture marge; cross-border shopping vers Suede courant; restrictions duty-free 1.5L vin pour residents

regulation_name: EGAIS — Unified State Automated Information System for alcohol
regulation_ref: Federal Law 171-FZ + Government Decree 1459 + Order Rosalkogol 397
key_requirements: EGAIS = mandatory electronic tracking system for alcohol in Russia; every wine bottle has unique excise stamp (FSM) with EGAIS code; bottling, wholesale, retail all integrated; QR/PDF417 codes scanned at each stage; retailers must scan and validate every bottle; consumer can scan and verify authenticity; foreign producers exporting to Russia must integrate EGAIS via Russian importer; introduced 2016 wholesale, 2017 retail, full deployment ongoing for restaurants/bars
notes: EGAIS = world's most comprehensive alcohol track-and-trace; mandatory for all wine in Russian retail; counterfeit drastically reduced; foreign brands must work with EGAIS-registered importer; mobile app 'Antikontrafakt Alko' for consumers; integration adds 3-5% cost

regulation_name: Russian Federal Wine Law 468-FZ — Domestic wine protection
regulation_ref: Federal Law 468-FZ of 27 December 2019 (Vinogradarstvo i vinodelie)
enforcement_body: Ministry of Agriculture RF + RAR + Rospotrebnadzor
key_requirements: Russian wine industry development law: defines Russian wine (only from Russian grapes); ZHGU (Protected GIs of Russia) for Krasnodar, Crimea (Russian-administered), Stavropol; minimum 85% Russian grapes for 'Vino Rossii'; bulk wine imports for blending banned; quality categories; subsidy support; mandatory labelling Russian-language; foreign wine clearly labelled origin (imported); restaurants quota Russian wine in wine list; on-premise sales restrictions
notes: 468-FZ = major reform protecting domestic Russian wine; impact on imports significant; sanctions 2022+ accelerated import substitution; Krasnodar wine production growing rapidly; ZHGU 'Kuban Region', 'Crimea Wine' (status disputed internationally)

regulation_name: Turkish Alcohol Tax Reform — OTV Wine Rates
regulation_ref: Special Consumption Tax Law No. 4760 (OTV) + annual presidential decrees on rates
enforcement_body: Ministry of Treasury and Finance + Revenue Administration
key_requirements: Special Consumption Tax (OTV/SCT) on wine in Turkey reformed regularly; current rate approx 63.30 TRY/litre (2024) for wine + 20% ad valorem on retail; combined effective tax can exceed 80% retail price; presidential decrees adjust rates 2x/year minimum; small producer reduced rates not available; specific OTV rate higher than wine vs other alcohol per litre; municipal additional taxes possible; import wine = OTV + customs + VAT
notes: Turkey OTV vin = lourdes taxes politiques; importations vin europeennes deviennent prohibitives; production locale (Kavaklidere, Doluca, Sevilen) prend marche; appellations turques d'origine (Bornova Misketi, Capalca, Tokat) reglementees

regulation_name: Yonetmelik Alkollu Iceceklerin Etiketlemesi — Wine labelling regulation Turkey
regulation_ref: Turk Gida Kodeksi + Yonetmelik No. 28867 (Alkollu icecekler etiketleme)
enforcement_body: Tarim ve Orman Bakanligi + Yerli Halk Saglik Kurumlari
key_requirements: Turkish wine labelling: mandatory Turkish language; product name, ABV (% hacim), volume, manufacturer, country of origin (mensei), batch number, expiry/best before; mandatory health warnings in Turkish ('Alkollu icecek tuketimi saglik icin zararlidir'); pregnancy pictogram mandatory; sulfite declaration if >10 mg/L; no health claims permitted; advertising prohibited on packaging; vintage permitted but regulated
notes: Turkey health warning mandatory since 2013 with pictogram; legitimacy of Turkish wine industry contested politically; export to Turkey complex due to changing tax/labelling rules; Bayrampasa = bonded warehouse zone for imports

regulation_name: Ukraine GI Wine Reform — EU Alignment under DCFTA
