regulation_ref: Zakon Ukrainy Pro heohrafichni zaznachennya No. 752-XIV + AA/DCFTA Annex
enforcement_body: Derzhprodspozyvsluzh + Ministry of Economic Development + Patent Office
key_requirements: Ukraine GI system aligning with EU under AA/DCFTA: registered Ukrainian wine GIs include 'Bessarabia', 'Beresan', 'Schody', 'Inkerman' (status complex post-Crimea); EU GI (Champagne, Bordeaux, Chianti, Rioja) protected in Ukraine; reciprocal protection Ukrainian wines in EU; technical specifications mandatory; producer groups (consortia) recognised; phase-in for Crimean Sparkling Wines transition completed
notes: Ukraine progresses EU integration despite war; Champagne brand finally restored in Ukrainian law post-DCFTA (was generic name); Crimean wine industry lost (Inkerman, Massandra) - now in Russia-controlled area; Carpathian wine producers gaining recognition

regulation_name: Ukraine Excise Stamps on Wine — Tax Compliance
regulation_ref: Tax Code of Ukraine art. 226 + Cabinet Resolution on excise stamps
enforcement_body: State Tax Service of Ukraine + State Fiscal Service
key_requirements: Ukrainian excise stamps (aktsyznyi marky) mandatory on all wine bottles sold in Ukraine; specific format with serial number, holographic protection, QR code; excise rate wine still: UAH 8.42/litre (2024); sparkling: UAH 12.05/litre; importers acquire stamps from State Tax Service; foreign producers send stamps to Ukrainian bottler/importer; counterfeit major problem historically; e-excise system in deployment
notes: Ukraine excise stamps essential pour anti-fraude; war 2022+ disrupts supply chain (Sumy, Kharkiv printing plants); border crossings simplified for humanitarian wine donations; stamp counterfeiting reduced post-EGAIS-style reforms

regulation_name: TR TS 022/2011 — Food labelling technical regulation (wine)
regulation_ref: Technical Regulation Customs Union TR TS 022/2011 (food labelling)
key_requirements: EAEU food labelling regulation applicable to wine: product name in Russian + member state language; net volume, ABV, country of origin, manufacturer/importer details, ingredient list, allergens, lot, storage conditions, expiry; nutrition values voluntary for alcoholic beverages; mandatory warning for pregnant women + drivers + minors; mandatory for all EAEU markets; Eurasian Conformity Mark (EAC) on label
notes: TR TS 022/2011 applies in parallel with TR TS 047/2018 alcohol regulation; EAC mark mandatory; non-conformity = withdrawal from market; manufacturer or first importer responsible

regulation_name: EAEU Common Excise Policy on Wine — Inter-state agreement
regulation_ref: EAEU Treaty + Excise harmonisation Decision EEC 35
enforcement_body: Eurasian Economic Commission + national tax authorities
key_requirements: EAEU members harmonising excise on wine but national rates remain divergent: Russia approx 21 RUB/L wine; Belarus approx 1.5 BYN/L; Kazakhstan approx 80 KZT/L; Armenia approx 380 AMD/L; Kyrgyzstan approx 30 KGS/L; movement between EAEU duty-suspended with excise payment at destination; common customs tariff for third-country wine imports
notes: EAEU excise harmonisation ongoing but slow; arbitrage opportunities between members; Russia largest wine market in bloc; Armenian wine industry growing (Areni grape)

regulation_name: Trade Agreements Act + Wine Equity Compliance
regulation_ref: Trade Agreements Act 1979 + USMCA wine annex
key_requirements: US wine trade obligations under WTO + bilateral agreements; USMCA includes wine annex with rules of origin (substantial transformation = fermentation); EU-US Wine Agreement 2006 (mutual recognition of certain practices); Champagne/Burgundy/Chablis grandfather clause for pre-2006 US semi-generic uses (controversial); reciprocal certification reduced); MIP-21 wine export programs; FDA Foreign Supplier Verification for imported wine
notes: EU-US Wine Agreement preserves some US 'semi-generic' uses (Champagne California, Chablis) limited; modern brands cannot use; Constellation Brands etc grandfather clauses; reciprocal Wines Australia, Wine New Zealand programs

regulation_name: FDA Bioterrorism Act — Wine Facility Registration
regulation_ref: Public Health Security and Bioterrorism Preparedness Act 2002 + 21 CFR 1.227
key_requirements: Foreign wine producers exporting to US must register with FDA (Food Facility Registration); biennial renewal; US Agent designation required; Prior Notice for each shipment via FDA Prior Notice System Interface (PNSI); arrives 8 hours before for sea, 4 hours land/air; failure to prior notice = shipment refused at port; integrated with TTB import processes; FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP) for importer due diligence
notes: FDA registration separate from TTB COLA process; both required; FDA registration renewal Oct 31 - Dec 31 even-numbered years; failure to renew = automatic deactivation; US Agent must be available 24/7 for FDA

regulation_ref: California Health and Safety Code 25249.5 et seq. + OEHHA regulations
enforcement_body: Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment (OEHHA) + Attorney General + private enforcers
key_requirements: California Prop 65: alcoholic beverages listed as carcinogen (ethanol) since 1988; mandatory warning label OR point-of-sale sign for wine sold in California: 'WARNING: Drinking distilled spirits, beer, coolers, wine and other alcoholic beverages may increase cancer risk, and, during pregnancy, can cause birth defects'; new 2018 short-form warning permitted; bottler/distributor responsible; private enforcer lawsuits common (Center for Environmental Health, Mateel Environmental); recent Acrylamide settlement framework
notes: Prop 65 ALCOHOL WARNING required since 1988 - older than federal SG warning; private enforcement creates litigation risk (>$200k typical settlements); 2024 OEHHA revising warning format - cancer risk specifically; California ABC also requires posting in retail establishments

regulation_name: California ABC Department — Three-Tier System Enforcement
regulation_ref: California Business and Professions Code Division 9 (Alcoholic Beverage Control Act)
enforcement_body: California Department of Alcoholic Beverage Control (ABC)
key_requirements: California strict 3-tier system: producer-wholesaler-retailer separation; wineries can hold Type 02 (Winegrower) license permitting direct shipping (up to 36 cases/household/year); on-sale (Type 41/47) and off-sale (Type 20/21) licenses; tied-house violations prohibited (gift, promotion, exclusive deals); ABC investigations frequent; minor decoy operations; happy hour rules restrictions on free alcohol; sales tax + excise tax + use tax compliance
notes: California = largest US wine market and producer; ABC enforcement aggressive; tied-house violations carry $5000+ fines and license suspension; alcoholic beverage permits prized (numerical limits per county); recent reforms allow more on-trade promotion

regulation_name: New York State Liquor Authority (SLA) — Licensing wine
regulation_ref: New York Alcoholic Beverage Control Law + SLA regulations
key_requirements: NY SLA licenses all alcohol commerce in New York; wine wholesaler license required; retail wine license (off-premises liquor store separate from groceries); wine in grocery stores currently NOT permitted (long-standing political debate); on-premises wine/beer license restaurants; direct shipping permit for out-of-state wineries (since 2005); SLA approves wine labels for sale in NY (state-level approval in addition to federal COLA); New York Wine Industry Association membership common for compliance
notes: NY = restrictive 3-tier state; wine NOT sold in supermarkets/groceries (only beer); standalone wine/liquor stores only; political pressure to liberalize ongoing; NY Common Council bills pending; direct-to-consumer shipping legal post-Granholm v Heald

regulation_name: New York Excise Tax + Bottle Bill on Wine
regulation_ref: NY Tax Law Article 18 + NY Environmental Conservation Law 27-1003 (Returnable Container Act)
enforcement_body: NY Department of Taxation and Finance + NY DEC
key_requirements: NY State wine excise tax: still wine USD 0.30/gallon; sparkling wine USD 0.30/gallon (rate parity); NYC additional USD 0.12/gallon; deposit law (Bottle Bill) NOT currently applying to wine (only beer/soda/water); 2024 expansion proposed to include wine - pending legislation; recycling content mandates emerging; sales tax 8.875% NYC, varies upstate; restaurant alcohol sales subject to sales tax
notes: NY Bottle Bill expansion to wine = 2025 hot topic; 5 cent deposit per container if expanded; significant compliance impact for wine industry; current wine excise lower than spirits NY rate; restaurant compliance critical

regulation_name: Canada Food and Drug Regulations — Wine (Division 2)
regulation_ref: Food and Drug Regulations CRC c870 Division 2 (Alcoholic Beverages)
enforcement_body: Health Canada + CFIA (Canadian Food Inspection Agency)
key_requirements: Canada FDR Division 2 governs alcoholic beverages composition + labelling; wine defined; standardised analytical limits; sulfite declaration mandatory if >10 mg/L; bilingual French/English labelling mandatory; metric units; nutrition labelling exemption for alcoholic beverages (currently); allergen labelling (eggs, milk, fish - if used as fining); BPA-free closures recommended; CFIA enforcement under SFCA (Safe Food for Canadians Act 2019)
notes: Canada bilingual labelling = mandatory French/English with equal prominence; wine nutrition declaration optional in Canada (unlike EU 2023); CFIA + provincial liquor boards both enforce; recent SFCA modernisation 2019 effective for wine

regulation_ref: Provincial Liquor Acts (Ontario LLBC, Quebec LRDIQ, BC LDA) + each board's listing regulations
enforcement_body: LCBO (Ontario), SAQ (Quebec), BCLDB (BC), AGLC (Alberta), other provincial boards
key_requirements: Canada wine retail dominated by provincial monopolies (with private exceptions): LCBO Ontario; SAQ Quebec; BCLDB British Columbia; NSLC Nova Scotia; NLC Newfoundland; private retail in Alberta (since 1993) + Saskatchewan (since 2017); each board: listing tender process (LCBO Vintages program); wholesale-retail markups 100%+ typical; quality standards inspection; mandatory product registration; bilingual labelling Quebec stricter
notes: Canada provincial liquor boards = largest wine buyers in world; LCBO purchases USD 6bn+ wine annually; complex listing process per province; producer must navigate 13 separate provincial regimes; recent Federal Court case Comeau v R 2018 upheld provincial monopoly authority

key_requirements: NOM-142 Mexico: etiquetado obligatorio bebidas alcoholicas espanol; nombre comercial, contenido alcoholico (%Vol), contenido neto, lote, fecha consumo preferente o caducidad, denominacion (vino, vino espumoso, etc.); leyenda preventiva 'EL ABUSO EN EL CONSUMO DE ESTE PRODUCTO ES NOCIVO PARA LA SALUD' obligatoria; pictograma 'PROHIBIDA SU VENTA A MENORES DE 18 ANOS'; declaracion sulfitos si >10 mg/L; importadores requieren registro COFEPRIS
notes: NOM-142 actualizada 2014; vinos importados deben re-etiquetar con leyenda Mexico; COFEPRIS revisa muestras pre-mercado; PROFECO controla informacion al consumidor; pictograma menores de 18 distintivo Mexico

regulation_name: IEPS — Impuesto Especial sobre Produccion y Servicios (vino)
regulation_ref: Ley del IEPS art. 2 + Reglas miscelaneas SAT
key_requirements: IEPS Mexico sobre vino: tasa 26.5% sobre precio bebidas alcoholicas hasta 14 grados ABV (vino esta); +16% IVA standard; aplicacion ad valorem complica cumplimiento; importadores deben presentar pedimento aduanal con IEPS calculado; impresion folio fiscal en marbete obligatorio; SAT controles aduaneros e internos; etiquetado fiscal (marbete) obligatorio en cuello bottela; reglas misceloneas SAT actualizadas anualmente
notes: Mexico IEPS bebidas alcoholicas alta carga fiscal; marbete fiscal obligatorio (~costo 8-12 pesos); contrabando vino impactado por marbete; importadores certificados via SAT padron sectorial necesario

regulation_name: Brazilian Wine Law 7.678/1988 — Federal wine framework
regulation_ref: Lei Federal n. 7.678/1988 + Decreto 8.198/2014 (Regulamento) + Instrucao Normativa MAPA
enforcement_body: Ministerio da Agricultura, Pecuaria e Abastecimento (MAPA) + IPI/Receita Federal
key_requirements: Lei 7.678/1988 marco regulatorio vinho Brasil; classifica: vinho de mesa, vinho fino, vinho frizante, espumante; vinho deve ter minimo 8.6% ABV; uvas Vitis vinifera ou hibridas autorizadas; rotulagem em portugues obrigatoria; analise fisico-quimica laboratorio credenciado; certificado de origem para exportacao; cadastro vinicola MAPA obrigatorio; etiqueta deve indicar producao/embalagem/distribuicao; rastreabilidade obrigatoria
notes: Brasil = maior produtor vinho America Sul (regiao Serra Gaucha); Lei 7.678 modernizada 2014; cadastro vinicola MAPA controla mercado; vinho mesa (cv Isabella) majoritario producao local mas vinho fino crescendo (Vale dos Vinhedos D.O. registrada 2012)

key_requirements: Brasil IPI (Imposto Produtos Industrializados) sobre vinho: aliquota 10-12% segundo tipo; +ICMS estadual (varia 18-25%); +PIS/COFINS; carga fiscal total significativa; rotulagem ANVISA RDC 259/2002 (alimentos pre-embalados): obrigatorio portugues, contendo, lote, validade, advertencia gravidez; advertencia 'EVITE O CONSUMO EXCESSIVO DE ALCOOL' obrigatorio destacado; vendas menores 18 proibidas; importadores devem ter registro Receita + ANVISA
notes: Brasil IPI vinho importado deve ser pago antes de liberacao aduana; selo Receita Federal pode ser exigido; ANVISA controlos pos-mercado; etiqueta gravidez pictograma; INMETRO controla unidades de medida

regulation_ref: Ley 14.878/1959 (Ley Nacional de Vinos) + decretos reglamentarios INV
enforcement_body: Instituto Nacional de Vitivinicultura (INV) + AFIP + Ministerio de Agricultura
key_requirements: INV controla integralmente sector vinicola argentino; vinedos deben estar inscriptos en registro INV; certificacion analitica obligatoria pre-comercializacion; sello de garantia INV en envase; clasificacion: Vinos Finos, Vinos de Mesa, Vinos Espumosos; denominaciones reconocidas (DOC Mendoza, Lujan de Cuyo, San Rafael); analisis fisico-quimicos por INV o laboratorios autorizados; estampillas fiscales para mercado interno
notes: INV = autoridad central vitivinicultura Argentina; segunda mayor productor vino Sudamerica; cuna del Malbec mundial; controles INV estrictos; exportaciones vinico significativos (>200M USD/ano)

regulation_name: Argentine Wine Export Regulation - Certificate of Origin
enforcement_body: INV + Secretaria de Comercio Exterior + Aduana Argentina
key_requirements: Exportacion vino Argentina: certificado de origen INV obligatorio; analisis quimico-fisico previo embarque; certificado fitosanitario SENASA; declaracion exportacion AFIP; etiquetado destino segun pais importador; reembolsos por exportacion (reintegros); restricciones cambiarias BCRA para retorno divisas exportacion; documentacion electronica via Maria (sistema aduanero)
notes: Argentina exportadora importante; mercados EU, US, China; reintegros exportacion variables segun politica economica; cepos cambiarios impactan competitividad; INV emite Certificado Inspeccion en menos 5 dias habiles

regulation_ref: Ley 18.455 de 1985 (fija normas vinos y alcoholes) + Decreto 78/1986 (Reglamento)
enforcement_body: Servicio Agricola y Ganadero (SAG) + Servicio Salud + Aduana
key_requirements: Chile Ley 18.455 marco regulatorio: definiciones vino y alcoholes; clasificacion (vino, mosto, vino con denominacion de origen); zonas vitivinicolas reconocidas (Valle del Maipo, Casablanca, Colchagua, Maule, etc.); D.O. categorias: 'Vino con D.O.', 'Vino Premium', 'Vino Reserva', 'Vino Gran Reserva'; SAG controla calidad y origen; analisis precomercializacion; rotulacion castellano obligatorio; certificado SAG exportacion
notes: Chile = 4to mayor exportador vino mundial (despues IT, ES, FR); SAG sistema riguroso certificacion; D.O. Chile incluye 14 valles principales; reciprocidad reconocimiento D.O. UE-Chile vigente; sector altamente concentrado (Concha y Toro, Vina San Pedro, Santa Rita)

regulation_name: SAG - Resolucion N1.187/2016 Certificacion Vinos Exportacion
key_requirements: SAG Resolucion 1.187/2016 establece procedimientos certificacion vino exportacion: analitica obligatoria (SAG o laboratorio autorizado); etiquetado conforme destino; certificado de origen (CO); certificado de calidad (CC); registro electronico TRAZA; trazabilidad desde vinedo hasta bodega; controles permanentes y aleatorios; sancion incluye suspension exportacion
notes: SAG genera 1M+ certificados de exportacion/ano; sistema digital TRAZA mandatorio; certificacion D.O. Chile = pasaporte calidad; CO indispensable para tarifa preferencial acuerdos comerciales; demoras certificacion impactan logistica export

enforcement_body: Instituto Nacional de Vigilancia de Medicamentos y Alimentos (INVIMA) + Ministerio de Salud
key_requirements: Decreto 1686/2012 reglamenta bebidas alcoholicas en Colombia; clasificacion vinos; requisitos sanitarios fabricacion, importacion, distribucion; registro sanitario INVIMA obligatorio pre-mercado (RSA); etiquetado en espanol; advertencia salud obligatoria; contenido alcoholico declarado tolerancia ±0.5%; analisis laboratorio acreditado; importadores deben estar registrados; aprobacion de etiqueta separada del RSA
notes: Colombia INVIMA registro sanitario vino obligatorio (RSA) - vigencia 10 anos; codigo de barras y trazabilidad; controles aleatorios mercado; PROFECO equivalente DGCCRF para informacion consumidor; expedicion RSA puede demorar 6-12 meses

regulation_ref: Estatuto Tributario art. 207 + Leyes departamentales + Sentencia C-486
enforcement_body: Direccion Impuestos y Aduanas Nacionales (DIAN) + Secretarias Hacienda departamentales
key_requirements: Colombia: ICUI (Impuesto al Consumo de Vinos y Espumosos) recaudado por departamentos; tasa especifica + ad valorem variable por departamento; vinos hasta 15% ABV: tarifa fija COP por grado de alcohol/litro; estampilla departamental obligatoria en envase para circulacion legal en cada departamento; rentas departamentales (licores) financian salud y educacion; importacion: + arancel + IVA 19%; productor o importador responsable de pago
notes: Colombia rentas departamentales pueden duplicar precio vino; estampilla diferente por departamento complica distribucion; Antioquia, Cundinamarca, Valle del Cauca mayores mercados; importacion paralela ilegal pero presente

regulation_ref: Resolucion GMC N 45/96 - Reglamento Vitivinicola del MERCOSUR + actualizaciones
enforcement_body: Grupo Mercado Comun + Comites tecnicos MERCOSUR
key_requirements: MERCOSUR resolucion 45/96 armoniza definiciones vino entre AR, BR, PY, UY (CL miembro asociado); definiciones comunes (vino, vino especial, espumoso, frizante); analisis fisico-quimicos armonizados; etiquetado bilingue portugues/espanol facilitado; reconocimiento mutuo certificaciones; circulacion intra-MERCOSUR facilitada; arancel externo comun (AEC) para terceros paises sobre vinos importados
notes: MERCOSUR wine harmonization facilita circulacion intra-bloque; Argentina + Brasil + Chile (associate) + Uruguay = 99% produccion regional; AEC vino aprox 27% pour pays tiers; UE-MERCOSUR FTA finalizado 2024 potencialmente cambia cuotas vino

regulation_name: MERCOSUR Regulatory Approach to Wine Geographic Indications
regulation_ref: Decision CMC N 1/12 - GIs framework MERCOSUR + Argentina-Brazil GIs Agreement
enforcement_body: Grupo Mercado Comun + INV (AR) + MAPA (BR) + INAVI (UY)
key_requirements: MERCOSUR GI cooperation framework: mutual recognition of registered GIs between members; Vale dos Vinhedos (BR), Mendoza/Lujan de Cuyo (AR), Tannat Uruguay GIs progress; EU GIs (Champagne, Bordeaux, Chianti) protected under EU-MERCOSUR FTA Annex II; transition periods for previously-used semi-generic terms; producer organizations recognized cross-border
notes: MERCOSUR GI system embryonic compared to EU; Vale dos Vinhedos = first BR wine GI (2012); GI tourism developing; EU-MERCOSUR FTA when ratified will protect EU GIs in all members

regulation_name: GB 15037-2006 - Chinese National Standard for Wine
enforcement_body: SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) + China Alcoholic Drinks Association
key_requirements: GB 15037-2006 = mandatory national standard for wine in China; defines wine categories (still, sparkling, fortified); production technical requirements; chemical and microbiological limits; analytical methods; sensory evaluation criteria; minimum ABV (7% still, 10% sparkling); pesticide residue MRLs aligned with GB 2763; food additive limits per GB 2760; ALL wine sold in China (imported or domestic) must comply; CIQ inspection at port
notes: GB 15037 binding for all wine in PRC market; revision in progress (2024-2025) to align with international practices; OIV references increasingly used; testing methods mandatory for import compliance

regulation_name: China Wine Import Label Approval — CIQ requirements
regulation_ref: GB 7718-2011 (general food labelling) + Order GACC 248/249 (food safety + facility registration)
key_requirements: Imported wine to China: Chinese label (back label) mandatory in simplified Chinese; original label preserved + Chinese label added; mandatory elements: product name (Chinese), origin country, ABV, volume, ingredients (including sulfites), allergens, producer/importer details, production date, shelf life, lot number; GACC food facility registration of producer mandatory (effective 2022); CIQ inspection certificate; importer record (Customs filing)
notes: GACC 248/249 (effective 1 Jan 2022) MAJOR reform requiring producer facility registration with China Customs; failure to register = import refusal; Chinese label essential pre-import; importers handle Chinese label printing in bonded warehouses; CIQ random sampling at port

regulation_name: Hong Kong Wine Tax Abolition + Free Port Status
regulation_ref: Dutiable Commodities Ordinance Cap. 109 + 2008 budget abolition of wine tax
enforcement_body: Hong Kong Customs and Excise + FEHD
key_requirements: Hong Kong abolished wine duty in 2008 (from 80% to 0%); created Asia's premier wine trading hub; no excise on still or sparkling wine (zero duty); only ABV >30% spirits taxed; no import licence required for wine; no quotas; declaration via Customs (Trade Declaration) and food import notification (FEHD); wine auctions flourishing (Sotheby's, Christie's HK); fine wine storage Asia hub
notes: Hong Kong = world's largest fine wine auction market post-2008; mainland China premium wine often routes via HK; CEPA 0% tariff to mainland China since 2024 - boosts re-export; FTAs reciprocal benefits

regulation_name: Hong Kong Wine Labelling under FBO Regulation
regulation_ref: Food and Drugs (Composition and Labelling) Regulation Cap 132W + FEHD guidance
enforcement_body: Food and Environmental Hygiene Department (FEHD) + Centre for Food Safety
key_requirements: HK food labelling regulation applies to wine; mandatory English + Chinese (traditional) labelling; product name, list of ingredients, net quantity, durability indication, name and address of manufacturer/distributor, country of origin, allergen declaration (sulfites if >10 mg/L); nutrition labelling exempt for alcoholic beverages; small package exemption (<300 cm2); pre-packed wine must comply at point of sale
notes: HK bilingual labelling EN+TC mandatory; importers add Chinese sticker label compliant; allergen sulfites typically declared; CFS market surveillance includes wine; small format wines (single serve 187ml) eligible for simplified labelling

regulation_name: Taiwan Tobacco and Alcohol Administration Act
regulation_ref: Tobacco and Alcohol Administration Act + Tobacco and Alcohol Tax Act + Ministry of Finance regulations
enforcement_body: Ministry of Finance Taiwan + Customs Administration
key_requirements: Taiwan wine governed by MOF Tobacco and Alcohol Administration Act; importer/manufacturer licence required; alcohol tax on wine: NTD 7/litre for wine; bonded warehouse system; advertising restrictions in print, broadcast, internet; minimum age 18 sale; mandatory health warnings; pre-market product registration; nutritional labelling not required; importer responsible for compliance with Customs Administration
notes: Taiwan wine tax modest compared to spirits NTD 185/L pure alcohol; MOF Tobacco-Alcohol Administration central authority; importer registration mandatory; tax marks not required (paid at import)

regulation_name: Taiwan Food Safety and Sanitation Act — Wine Application
regulation_ref: Act Governing Food Safety and Sanitation art. 22 + TFDA standards
key_requirements: Taiwan wine labelling under FSSA: Traditional Chinese mandatory; product name, ingredients, net weight, manufacturer/distributor, country of origin, expiration date, lot, manufacturing date; mandatory health warning 'Drink in moderation; drinking and driving is dangerous'; pregnant women warning; ABV declaration; sulfite declaration if used; pre-market notification not required but importer must hold FDA Food Importer license
notes: TFDA aligned with Codex on wine labelling; Traditional Chinese characters mandatory (not simplified); Taiwan = significant premium wine market; health warning regulatory and standardized; minor compliance issues vs Mainland China

regulation_name: JAS Standard for Wine — Japanese Wine Definition
regulation_ref: JAS Law No. 175/1950 + JAS Standard for Domestic Wine 2018 + Liquor Tax Act
enforcement_body: Ministry of Finance Japan + NTA (National Tax Agency) + MAFF
key_requirements: Japan: 'Japanese Wine' (Nihon Wine) protected designation since 2018; must use 100% domestic grapes (Yamanashi, Nagano, Hokkaido etc.); blended wine (foreign + domestic) cannot use 'Japanese Wine' designation; Liquor Tax Act categorisation: wine = 'kajitsu-shu' (fruit wine); tax JPY 80/L wine; mandatory Japanese labelling; alcohol tax stamps not required (paid at production); manufacturer license required
notes: Japan Wine designation = strict 100% domestic grape rule since 2018 (transition period ended); booming domestic wine industry; Koshu grape protected designation; sparkling wine domestic market growing

regulation_name: Japan Wine Labelling — Manufactured by Liquor Tax Act 1953
regulation_ref: Liquor Tax Act 1953 (Sake-zei Ho) + Act on Liquor Business Association art. 86 (labelling) + NTA labelling rules
enforcement_body: National Tax Agency (NTA) + Consumer Affairs Agency (CAA)
key_requirements: Japanese wine labelling rules NTA + CAA: Japanese language mandatory; product name (vinos/wine), ABV, volume, manufacturer name/address, country of origin (import), production area for domestic; mandatory '20 saimi minimum age' (drinking age 20 - lowered to 18 for some adult products but alcohol stays 20); pregnancy warning recommended; sulfite declaration mandatory if used; manufacturer name in JP; certification mark not required
notes: Japan = 5th largest wine import market; legal drinking age 20 (one of highest globally); pregnancy warning voluntary but adopted widely; designation 'Nihon Wine' premium positioning; Yamanashi Koshu protected

regulation_name: Korea Liquor Tax Act + Food Sanitation Act — Wine
regulation_ref: Liquor Tax Act + Food Sanitation Act + Ministry of Food and Drug Safety regulations
enforcement_body: MFDS (Ministry of Food and Drug Safety) + Korea Customs + National Tax Service
key_requirements: Korea wine regulated under Liquor Tax Act (taxation) + Food Sanitation Act (safety/labelling); wine = kwail-ju (fruit liquor); excise tax 30% + education tax 10% of liquor tax; importer license required; MFDS food import notification mandatory pre-import; quality and safety standards (pesticide residues, contaminants); Korean Hangul labelling mandatory; pre-market notification via Korea Importer Single Window (UNI-PASS)
notes: Korea wine import market growing rapidly; FTA EU-Korea 2011 reduced wine tariffs to 0%; FTA US-Korea (KORUS) same; FTA Chile-Korea earliest; importer must hold license + MFDS notification + customs declaration

regulation_name: Korea Wine Labelling Standards under Liquor Tax Act
regulation_ref: Liquor Tax Act art. 8 + MFDS Notification 2024-XX + Liquor Labelling Standards
enforcement_body: MFDS + Korea Customs + Korea Fair Trade Commission
key_requirements: Korean wine labelling: Hangul (Korean script) mandatory; product name, ingredient list, country of origin, manufacturer/importer name, alcohol content (%), volume, manufacturing date, expiry/shelf life, batch number; mandatory warning 'Drinking alcohol can cause cancer including liver cancer' + pregnancy warning + 'No sale to minors under 19'; allergen declaration (sulfites); pesticide residues per MFDS standards
notes: Korea = first country to mandate cancer warning on alcohol labels (since 2016); Korea drinking age = 19; warning text in specific format mandated; Hangul labelling = importer sticker often used; KFDA inspection

regulation_name: Singapore Liquors Control Act + Customs Act
regulation_ref: Liquors Control (Supply and Consumption) Act 2015 + Customs Act + GST Act
enforcement_body: Singapore Customs + Health Sciences Authority + Singapore Food Agency
key_requirements: Singapore: import licence required (Customs); excise duty SGD 88/litre alcohol (wine 12% = SGD 7.92/bottle 75cl); +9% GST; no production locally significant; in-bond movements via PSA terminals; Liquor Control Act restricts retail hours 22:30-07:00; restricted Liquor Control Zones in Little India + Geylang; food labelling requirements per Sale of Food Act (English mandatory); allergen declaration
notes: Singapore = major Asian wine trading hub + tourist consumption; excise duty significant; Liquor Control Zones enforce restrictions; transhipment role for ASEAN; FTA partners benefit reduced tariffs

regulation_name: Sale of Food Act + Food Regulations - Wine Labelling Singapore
regulation_ref: Sale of Food Act + Food Regulations + Food Safety Regulations 2024
key_requirements: SFA labelling for wine: English mandatory; product name (wine), volume, ABV, country of origin, importer/distributor details, lot number, allergen declaration (sulfites if >10 mg/L); use-by/best-before date NOT required for alcoholic beverages; manufacturer/packer details; warning statements voluntary; pictogram pregnancy voluntary; nutrition declaration not required; certification mark not required (Halal certification voluntary)
notes: SFA English mandatory but multilingual permitted; sulfite declaration critical for export to SG; pregnancy pictogram increasingly adopted voluntarily; small format wines (<200ml) simplified labelling

regulation_ref: Food Regulations 1985 (Regulation 360-365 wine) + Food Act 1983 + Customs Act
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health Malaysia + Royal Malaysian Customs Department
key_requirements: Malaysia Food Regulations 1985: wine defined; standardised composition; mandatory labelling Bahasa Malaysia + English; alcohol content disclosure; sulfite declaration mandatory; producer/importer details; lot number; expiry date; halal NOT applicable for wine but mandatory not-halal indication; excise duty MYR 23.70/litre + import duty + sales tax 10%; restricted sale (Muslims prohibited under Sharia)
notes: Malaysia majority-Muslim country - wine market niche (non-Muslims, expatriates, tourists); kedai tuak/wine shops licensed; supermarket sales restricted to certain outlets; bilingual labelling BM/EN; halal certification not applicable to alcohol

regulation_name: Halal Standards — Wine and Alcohol Prohibitions
regulation_ref: Trade Descriptions (Definition of Halal) Order 2011 + JAKIM Halal Manual
enforcement_body: Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (JAKIM) + KPDNHEP
key_requirements: Wine explicitly haram (forbidden) in Malaysia under Islamic law for Muslims; products containing alcohol cannot bear halal certification; wine vinegar permitted (acetic acid >acetic alcohol); cooking wine in food products triggers haram classification; clear separation in retail stores often required; bottle labelling may need 'Mengandungi alkohol' (contains alcohol) warning; sale to Muslims criminal offence in some states under Sharia
notes: Malaysia dual legal system: civil + Sharia; wine sale to Muslims = Sharia offence in 13 states; Selangor + KL caning historically for alcohol consumption by Muslims; non-Muslim consumers permitted but discreetly; on-trade restaurants serving alcohol = licensed only

regulation_name: Thailand Liquor Act 2017 + Excise Tax Act
regulation_ref: Liquor Act 2017 (B.E. 2560) + Excise Tax Act 2017 (B.E. 2560) + ministerial regulations
enforcement_body: Thai Excise Department + FDA Thailand + Ministry of Public Health
key_requirements: Thailand liquor regulation reformed 2017; import + production + sale licensed by Excise Department; excise tax based on ABV + value (ad valorem) - wine specific rate THB 1500/litre alcohol + 10% ad valorem; +VAT 7%; +import duty 60% (significant barrier); advertising restrictions (no logos on broadcast); restricted sale hours 11-14h + 17-24h; no sales to under 20; no sales near schools/religious sites
notes: Thailand wine market growing but heavy taxation; total tax can exceed 100% of CIF value; FTAs ASEAN benefit some; restaurant on-trade common; ASEAN MRA wine permits some intra-bloc benefits

regulation_name: Thai Food Act + Liquor Labelling Standards
regulation_ref: Food Act B.E. 2522 (1979) + FDA Notification on Liquor Labels + Thai Industrial Standards
enforcement_body: Thai Food and Drug Administration + Thai Industrial Standards Institute (TISI)
key_requirements: Thailand wine labelling: Thai language mandatory + may include EN; product name, ingredient list (allergens), volume, ABV, country of origin, importer/manufacturer details, batch, manufacturing/expiry date; mandatory warning 'Drinking alcohol can cause health problems' Thai language; mandatory warning 'Reduces driving ability'; sulfite declaration; FDA Thailand pre-import notification; importer license + label approval
notes: Thailand wine warning labels significant graphic content sometimes required; FDA registration label-by-label time-consuming (6-12 months); excise stamp required physically on bottle for retail

regulation_name: Vietnam Decree 105/2017/ND-CP on Alcohol Production Trading
enforcement_body: Ministry of Industry and Trade (MOIT) + Ministry of Health + Tax Authority
key_requirements: Decree 105/2017 reformed alcohol regulation in Vietnam; production + wholesale + retail licenses; importation license from MOIT; wine excise tax 35% (alcohol products generally); +import duty (FTA partners reduced); +VAT 10%; advertising prohibitions extensive; labelling Vietnamese mandatory; restricted sales hours; minimum age 18; package size restrictions; warning labels mandatory
notes: Vietnam wine market growing with middle class; CPTPP and EVFTA (EU-Vietnam) reduce wine import tariffs; ASEAN FTA tariff reductions; restaurant on-trade significant; bia (beer) dominant alcohol consumption

regulation_name: Vietnamese Standard TCVN — Wine Quality and Labelling
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health Vietnam + Vietnam Food Administration (VFA) + STAMEQ
key_requirements: TCVN 7045:2013 = Vietnamese national standard for wine; composition, sensory, analytical specifications; QCVN 6-3:2010/BYT mandatory health-safety standard for alcoholic beverages; Vietnamese language labelling mandatory; product name, ingredient list, volume, ABV, manufacturer/importer details, manufacturing date, expiry, lot; health warning mandatory; allergen declaration (sulfites); VFA notification pre-import for some products
notes: Vietnam alcohol QCVN binding for safety; TCVN 7045 quality standard; product registration via VFA can take 60-90 days; importer license + product notification required

regulation_name: Indonesia BPOM Regulation on Alcoholic Beverages — Class B (Wine)
regulation_ref: BPOM Regulation 14/2016 + BPOM Reg 27/2017 + Ministry of Trade Regulation 20/2014
enforcement_body: BPOM (Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan) + Ministry of Trade + Customs
key_requirements: Indonesia: alcohol classified Class A (<5% ABV beer), Class B (5-20% wine), Class C (>20% spirits); only limited importers/distributors (NPIK + IT-MB licenses); BPOM food registration mandatory (Registration Number BPOM RI ML); only sold in: hotels, restaurants, bars, duty-free, supermarkets (Class B only in select cities); wine excise IDR 45,000/litre + import duty + 10% VAT; advertising heavily restricted; halal not applicable
notes: Indonesia majority-Muslim - alcohol restrictions tight; only major hotels/restaurants licensed; Bali tourism exception more liberal; product registration BPOM ML number critical; importer must be IT-MB authorized; counterfeit wine major concern

regulation_name: Indonesian Excise Tax + Pita Cukai Stamps
regulation_ref: Customs and Excise Law 1995 + Minister of Finance Regulation 158/PMK.04/2017 on Pita Cukai
enforcement_body: Directorate General of Customs and Excise + Ministry of Finance
key_requirements: Indonesia: cukai (excise) mandatory on all alcoholic beverages; pita cukai (excise stamp) physical sticker on each bottle for retail sale; wine excise IDR 45,000/litre + import duty + PPN 10%; pita cukai obtained from Customs by registered importer; counterfeit stamps criminal; consumer can verify pita authenticity via Customs website; on-trade restaurants must purchase stamped bottles; raids on unstamped alcohol common
notes: Indonesia pita cukai essential for legal sale; counterfeit alcohol kills hundreds annually (methanol contamination from illegal trade); legitimate import expensive due taxes; Bekraf supports legitimate wine market growth

regulation_name: Philippines Republic Act 9711 (FDA Act) + Wine Labelling
regulation_ref: Republic Act 9711 (FDA Act 2009) + FDA Circulars on Food and Alcohol
enforcement_body: Food and Drug Administration Philippines (FDA Philippines) + BIR + BOC
key_requirements: Philippines FDA regulates food including wine; License to Operate (LTO) for importers/distributors mandatory; Certificate of Product Registration (CPR) for each wine SKU; English language mandatory (Filipino optional); mandatory: product name, ingredients including allergens (sulfites if >10 mg/L), net contents, manufacturer/importer details, lot, manufacturing date, expiry; minimum drinking age 18; advertising restrictions; CPR valid 2-5 years renewable
notes: FDA Philippines product registration mandatory pre-import; FDA random sampling at port and market; CPR application process can take 3-6 months; renewal required; License to Operate (LTO) annual; English labelling sufficient (Filipino optional)

regulation_name: Philippines BIR Excise Tax + TRAIN Law 2018
regulation_ref: Tax Reform for Acceleration and Inclusion (TRAIN) Law RA 10963 + Excise Tax provisions
enforcement_body: Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) + Bureau of Customs
key_requirements: TRAIN Law 2018 increased excise tax on wine in Philippines; current rate PHP 50-650/litre depending on price + ABV; ad valorem 22% + specific tax; +12% VAT; import duty (low for ASEAN); annual indexation 4% increase; tax stamps required (BIR strip stamps); BOC enforces import duties + excise at port; smuggling crackdown ongoing; importer/manufacturer must register with BIR + secure tax stamps
notes: Philippines TRAIN Law major excise reform; wine prices significantly increased; tax stamps prevent illegal trade; CHED (alcoholic beverage tax adjustment) annual; ASEAN intra-trade benefits reduced tariff

regulation_name: Indian States Liquor Excise Laws — State-by-State Regulation
regulation_ref: State Excise Acts (UP, MH, KA, TN, WB, DL etc.) + Constitution Schedule 7
enforcement_body: State Excise Departments + State Liquor Boards
key_requirements: India: alcohol = State Subject under Constitution; each state regulates separately; complex 28 states + 8 UT with different rules; some states 'dry' (Gujarat, Bihar, Mizoram, Nagaland, Lakshadweep); excise rates vary dramatically (Delhi vs Karnataka 5x difference); state-specific labels often required; CL (Country Liquor) vs IMFL (Indian Made Foreign Liquor) vs IFL (Imported Foreign Liquor) classifications; wholesale via state corporations (TASMAC TN, BEVCO Kerala etc.)
notes: India = world's 9th largest wine consumer growing rapidly; Maharashtra + Karnataka largest markets; Nashik = 'wine capital'; state monopolies dominate; import licences required state-by-state; dry states = total wine prohibition

regulation_ref: FSS Act 2006 + Food Safety and Standards (Alcoholic Beverages) Regulations 2018
enforcement_body: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) + state food authorities
key_requirements: FSSAI regulates safety/quality of alcoholic beverages including wine; mandatory FSSAI license/registration for manufacturer/importer; product standards (wine min 7% ABV, max 15%; brandy, beer separate); contaminants and pesticide residue limits; mandatory labelling per FSS Labelling Regulations; English + Hindi or regional language as per state; mandatory health warnings; allergen declaration; advertising restrictions; recent grape wine specific standards
notes: FSSAI 2018 first nationwide alcohol regulations (before only excise/state); harmonisation challenge with state laws; importer must register with FSSAI; product testing at accredited labs; FSSAI mandatory label includes manufacturer/importer FSSAI number

regulation_name: Australia New Zealand Food Standards Code — Wine Standard 4.5.1
regulation_ref: Food Standards Code Standard 4.5.1 (Wine production) + Standard 2.7.4 (Wine and wine products)
enforcement_body: Food Standards Australia New Zealand (FSANZ) + state liquor authorities
key_requirements: FSANZ Standard 2.7.4: wine production requirements; mandatory composition standards; Standard 4.5.1: production requirements; ingredient labelling exemption for wine with new mandatory pregnancy warning since 2023; sulfite declaration mandatory; manufacturer/packer + country of origin; ABV mandatory; volume in metric; energy + carb content NOT yet mandatory (under review); food allergen declaration includes fining agents
notes: Australia + NZ joint food standards; pregnancy warning mandatory since 2020 (pictogram + text 'HEALTH WARNING - Any amount of alcohol can harm your baby' + 'PREGNANCY WARNING'); 3 year transition completed 2023; future ingredient/nutrition labelling debate active

regulation_name: Wine Australia Corporation Act + WET Tax Regulation
regulation_ref: Wine Australia Act 2013 + Wine Equalisation Tax Act 1999 + FOI regulations
enforcement_body: Wine Australia + ATO (Australian Taxation Office) + DAFF
key_requirements: Wine Australia = statutory authority funding wine industry levy; mandatory levy on Australian wine production; promotes Australian wine globally; manages export certifications (label integrity scheme); LIP (Label Integrity Programme) verifies vintage, variety, region claims; WET = Wine Equalisation Tax 29% on wholesale value; Producer Rebate scheme; export refund possible; Free Trade Agreements (CPTPP, AANZFTA, CHAFTA, EU FTA negotiating)
notes: Wine Australia LIP critical for export credibility; WET reform 2017 limited rebate eligibility; producer rebate capped AUD 350k/year; small wineries impacted; export refunds available

regulation_name: Wine Act 2003 + Wine Regulations 2006 New Zealand
regulation_ref: Wine Act 2003 + Wine Regulations 2006 + Wine (Specifications) Notice 2006
enforcement_body: Ministry for Primary Industries (MPI) + NZ Customs
key_requirements: NZ Wine Act 2003: comprehensive wine framework; mandatory winemaker registration with MPI; Wine Standards Management Plans (WSMP) required for all licensed winemakers; mandatory record-keeping; export eligibility certification via MPI; analytical testing requirements; labelling per FSANZ + NZ-specific requirements; New Zealand Wine Growers (NZW) is industry body; PGI/GI system developing (Hawke's Bay GI Trust)
notes: NZ wine industry = export-oriented (>70% exported); Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc dominant; MPI WSMP rigorous food safety; GI registration optional but growing (Marlborough, Central Otago, Hawke's Bay registered)

regulation_name: NZ Geographical Indications (Wine and Spirits) Act 2006
regulation_ref: Geographical Indications (Wine and Spirits) Registration Act 2006 + Regulations
enforcement_body: Intellectual Property Office of New Zealand (IPONZ) + MPI
key_requirements: NZ established GI system 2006 specific to wine and spirits; protected GIs include 'Marlborough', 'Central Otago', 'Martinborough', 'Waipara Valley'; minimum 85% grapes from claimed GI; producer applications via IPONZ; GI database public; counterfeit/misleading use enforced; reciprocal protection via FTAs (EU-NZ FTA 2024 mutual GI recognition); generic terms (NZ Wine) not protected
notes: NZ GI Act = world's first GI law specifically for wine; 22 registered NZ wine GIs; mutual recognition expanded under EU-NZ FTA effective 2024; producer organisations can apply for new GIs; geographic blending rules strict

regulation_name: ASEAN MRA on Alcoholic Beverages — Mutual Recognition Arrangement
regulation_ref: ASEAN Sectoral Mutual Recognition Arrangement on Alcoholic Beverages + ASEAN Trade in Goods Agreement (ATIGA)
key_requirements: ASEAN sectoral MRA aims to facilitate trade in alcoholic beverages between ASEAN members; mutual recognition of conformity assessment results; reduces duplication of testing; ASEAN Wine Standard development ongoing; ATIGA reduces intra-ASEAN tariffs to 0-5% generally (wine variable); rules of origin (Form D) for preferential treatment; e-Form D in many countries
notes: ASEAN intra-trade wine grew with ATIGA; Singapore = re-export hub for ASEAN; Vietnam emerging market; Indonesia + Malaysia + Brunei restrict for religious reasons; Thailand + Philippines + Vietnam growing import markets

regulation_name: ASEAN Harmonized Tariff Nomenclature (AHTN) — Wine HS Codes
regulation_ref: AHTN 2022 + ASEAN Single Window + national customs codes
key_requirements: AHTN harmonizes HS codes across ASEAN members at 8-digit level; wine codes: 2204.10 (sparkling wine), 2204.21 (wine in containers < 2L), 2204.22 (2-10L), 2204.29 (>10L); 2204.30 (other grape must); supports ASEAN Single Window for paperless trade; preferential tariff treatment under ATIGA Form D; wine fine wine specialty codes; cross-border movement simplified
notes: AHTN simplifies wine trade documentation; e-Form D operational in major ASEAN members; importer can claim preferential origin if grapes/processing ASEAN; non-ASEAN wine (EU/US/AU/CL) pays MFN rates per member

regulation_name: GCC Standard GSO 9 — Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (Alcohol exception)
regulation_ref: GSO 9/2013 General Requirements for Labelling of Prepackaged Foodstuffs + GCC Standards Organization
key_requirements: GCC Standard GSO 9 governs food labelling including alcoholic beverages where permitted (UAE limited, Bahrain, Oman); Arabic mandatory; English permitted addition; product name, ingredients, volume, ABV, manufacturer/importer details, lot, manufacturing/expiry dates; mandatory health warning per national rules; halal not applicable; for restricted markets (Saudi, Kuwait, Qatar) alcohol prohibited entirely
notes: GCC bloc partial alcohol prohibition: Saudi/Kuwait total ban; UAE limited (license required, expat communities, hotels); Bahrain + Oman permit licensed sales; Qatar very restricted; GSO 9 labelling applies in permitted markets

regulation_name: UAE Federal Decree Law No. 14 of 2020 — Alcohol Liberalization
regulation_ref: UAE Federal Decree Law No. 14/2020 + Dubai Tourism Department regulations
enforcement_body: UAE Federal Authority for Identity, Citizenship, Customs and Port Security + Emirates licensing authorities
key_requirements: UAE 2020 liberalized alcohol consumption: no individual liquor license required (was previously); hotels/restaurants can sell to all (not just non-Muslims) in licensed venues; tourists welcome to consume; minimum age 21 (higher than Western 18); off-trade retail still requires venue license (MMI, African+Eastern in Dubai); excise tax 50% on alcohol since 2019 (in addition to import duty + 5% VAT); Sharjah remains dry emirate (no alcohol)
notes: UAE 2020 reform major liberalization (no individual liquor license required); pre-2020 license was required for Muslims/non-Muslims and proof of religion; Sharjah Emirate maintains alcohol prohibition; excise 50% significant cost; Ramadan reduced licensed hours

regulation_name: Israel Wine Law + Kosher Wine Regulations
regulation_ref: Israeli Standard 1116 (Wine) + Kosher Certification (Chief Rabbinate) + Customs Tariff
enforcement_body: Standards Institution of Israel + Chief Rabbinate of Israel + Israel Tax Authority
key_requirements: Israel Standard 1116 governs wine; mandatory analytical compliance; Hebrew + Arabic + English labelling permitted (Hebrew + one mandatory); ABV declaration; sulfite if >10 mg/L; manufacturer/importer details; mandatory health warning Hebrew; minimum drinking age 18; excise tax on wine; kosher certification not mandatory but commercially essential (95%+ Israeli market is kosher); Mevushal (cooked/pasteurized) sub-category for Shabbat permitted handling
notes: Israel = significant wine producer (>50 wineries); kosher certification = market essential; Mevushal kosher = handled by non-Sabbath observant; Israeli wine industry technological innovation; Golan Heights Winery, Carmel, Barkan major brands

regulation_name: Israeli Customs + Excise Tax Wine — IATC
regulation_ref: Customs Tariff + Excise Duty Order + Israeli Tax Authority Notification
enforcement_body: Israel Customs (within ITA) + Ministry of Health
key_requirements: Israel: import duty on wine variable by origin (EU-Israel FTA = 0%, US FTA = 0%, others MFN); excise tax NIS 5.49/litre wine; +17% VAT; importer registration with Tax Authority; pre-import notification Ministry of Health (kosher status declaration optional); customs valuation declared; FDA-equivalent (Israel Ministry of Health) approves food imports; tariff classification critical
notes: Israel FTAs reduce wine import duties significantly; kosher status often declared at import (commercial necessity); excise modest compared to EU averages; market liberal compared to other Middle East

regulation_name: Egypt Trade Law + Wine Import Regulation
regulation_ref: Trade Law No. 17/1999 + Egyptian Drug Authority regulations + Customs Authority
enforcement_body: Ministry of Trade and Industry + Customs Authority + Ministry of Tourism (for licensing)
key_requirements: Egypt: alcohol import strictly regulated; importer license required; permitted only to licensed importers serving tourist sector + diplomatic; mandatory labelling Arabic; product name, ABV, volume, manufacturer/importer, country of origin; alcohol prohibited Muslims by religious law (not strict legal prohibition but social); domestic production (El Gouna, Beirut Beverages produce limited wine + spirits); Customs Authority controls all imports; high tariffs
notes: Egypt wine market limited - mainly tourism sector (hotels Red Sea + Nile cruises); domestic production modest; import licenses scarce; Coptic Christian market exists; Ramadan alcohol sales restricted further

regulation_ref: Customs Law + Egyptian Customs Tariff + Excise Tax Law
enforcement_body: Egyptian Customs Authority + Ministry of Finance
key_requirements: Egypt high import tariffs on alcohol: wine import duty 600 EGP/litre minimum or 3000% ad valorem (whichever higher) - prohibitive; excise tax additional; +VAT 14%; import only via licensed agents; bonded warehouses for duty-free shops + tourist hotels; smuggling significant; domestic wine modest (Omar Khayyam brand best known); few imported wines available even in upscale hotels
notes: Egypt = world's highest wine import tariffs effectively prohibiting most imports; domestic production fills small market; tourist hotels duty-free for international consumers; black market significant

regulation_name: Morocco Decree 02-1273 — Wine Production and Trade
regulation_ref: Decret n 2-99-89 du 14 fevrier 2000 + Arretes ministeriels + Code des Investissements
enforcement_body: Ministere de l'Agriculture + Ministere de l'Industrie + Direction Generale des Impots
key_requirements: Maroc: production vin reglementee; reservations d'aire et de cepages; AOC marocaines (Coteaux de l'Atlas, Coteaux du Zerhoun, etc.); etiquetage francais + arabe permis; ABV, volume, producteur/importateur, mentions obligatoires; vente alcool reglementee (interdit pour Musulmans theoriquement); detaillants + restaurants licencies; supermarches autorises (Carrefour, Marjane vendent vin); taxe sur boissons alcooliques significative; production locale principalement Meknes + Casablanca
notes: Morocco production vin significative pour pays musulman (8M bottiglie/an); appellations marocaines reconnues; secteur tourisme important; Castel Group + Les Celliers de Meknes producteurs majeurs; Halal certification non applicable mais notation alcohol exigee

regulation_ref: Code Douanes Maroc + Loi des Finances annuelle + Taxe Interieure de Consommation
enforcement_body: Administration Douanes et Impots Indirects (ADII) + DGI
key_requirements: Maroc TIC (Taxe Interieure de Consommation) sur vin = 800 DH/hl approx; +droits douane importation (variable, accords UE-Maroc reduisent); +TVA 20%; ALE UE-Maroc reduit progressivement tarif douanier; production marocaine pas sujette droits douane; importation via importateurs agrees; Free Zones avantage fiscal limite; vente detail licence obligatoire
notes: Maroc TIC vin modere comparativement; ALE UE-Maroc tarifs preferentiels (en cours alignement OMC); ADII controles bonded warehouses; smuggling rare comparativement Egypt/Tunisia

regulation_name: South Africa Liquor Products Act + Wine of Origin Scheme
regulation_ref: Liquor Products Act 60 of 1989 + Regulations + Wine of Origin Scheme
enforcement_body: South African Wine Industry and Systems for Integrity and Sustainability (SAWIS) + Department of Agriculture
key_requirements: South Africa Wine of Origin (WO) Scheme: certifies origin, vintage, variety; minimum 100% origin claim; 85% vintage; 85% variety claim; production areas: Coastal Region (Stellenbosch, Paarl, Franschhoek), Breede River Valley, Olifants River, Cape South Coast, Boberg, etc.; Estate Wine designation (must be 100% from named estate); analytical + sensory evaluation; SAWIS issues certification number on bottle neck; export certification additional
notes: SA WO Scheme = oldest African wine GI system (1973); approx 50% SA wine exported (mainly UK, Germany, NL, Sweden); empowerment requirements for ownership; BEE codes apply to industry

regulation_name: South African Excise Tax + SARS Wine Compliance
regulation_ref: Customs and Excise Act 91 of 1964 + Sugary Beverages Levy (not on wine) + SARS notices
key_requirements: SA wine excise: still wine ZAR 4.40/litre (2024); sparkling wine ZAR 11.50/litre; +15% VAT; +import duty (FTAs reduce - EU-SADC EPA 0% on EU wine); SARS Customs collects at port for imports; bonded warehouses for duty-suspended; small producer concession potentially; export wine - rebate available; lighting goods (small package) special rate
notes: SA wine excise modest compared to EU; SACU customs union with Botswana, Lesotho, Namibia, Eswatini = common tariff; EPA SADC-EU provides 0% on Cape wine to EU; export rebates support competitiveness

regulation_name: Nigeria NAFDAC Regulations on Wine + Alcoholic Beverages
regulation_ref: National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control Act + Pre-packaged Food Labelling Regulations 2019
enforcement_body: NAFDAC + Standards Organisation of Nigeria (SON) + Nigeria Customs Service
key_requirements: Nigeria: NAFDAC product registration mandatory for imported and domestic wine; Pre-packaged Food Labelling Regulations 2019; mandatory English language labelling; product name, ABV, ingredients list including allergens, manufacturer/importer details, country of origin, batch, manufacturing/expiry dates; mandatory health warning English; mandatory NAFDAC registration number on label; minimum age 18; advertising restrictions
notes: Nigeria NAFDAC registration mandatory (NAFDAC No.) - process can take 3-12 months; counterfeit/substandard wine major problem historically; NAFDAC raids common; mandatory registration on label gives consumer confidence; SON also involved in standards

regulation_name: Nigerian Customs Service Tariff + Excise Duty
regulation_ref: Common External Tariff (CET) ECOWAS + Nigeria Federal Inland Revenue Service excise
enforcement_body: Nigeria Customs Service + FIRS + ECOWAS Commission
key_requirements: Nigeria: wine import duty 20% (ECOWAS CET) + excise duty NGN 75/litre + ETLS levy + VAT 7.5%; ECOWAS-FTA provides preference for ECOWAS-produced (very limited wine); FX restrictions on alcohol historically (delisted from official rate 2020 - market rate only); importer must clear via Customs; CET review periodic; smuggling significant via porous borders
notes: Nigeria FX challenges complicate import; wine market growing with affluent class; Lagos + Abuja + Port Harcourt main markets; restaurant + hotel on-trade significant; church/communion wine some demand

regulation_ref: Alcoholic Drinks Control Act No. 4 of 2010 (Mututho Law) + Kenya Bureau of Standards (KEBS)
enforcement_body: NACADA (National Authority for the Campaign Against Alcohol and Drug Abuse) + KEBS + Kenya Revenue Authority
key_requirements: Kenya Mututho Law 2010 - landmark alcohol regulation; manufacturer/importer/distributor/retailer all licensed; minimum drinking age 18; restricted sale hours (16:00-22:00 weekdays, longer weekends); KEBS standards for wine; mandatory labelling English + Kiswahili; health warnings mandatory; mandatory advertising restrictions; mandatory packaging requirements; on-premise restrictions; NACADA enforcement
notes: Kenya Mututho Law major impact 2010+; counterfeit chang'aa (illegal spirits) killed hundreds annually pre-law; legitimate alcohol market grown significantly; KEBS standards mandatory; KRA collects significant revenue from alcohol

regulation_name: Kenya Excise Duty on Wine + Excisable Goods Management System (EGMS)
regulation_ref: Excise Duty Act 2015 + Kenya Revenue Authority Regulations + EGMS
key_requirements: Kenya wine excise: KES 235/litre (2024) + +VAT 16% + import duty (East African Community CET); EGMS = electronic system: physical Excise Stamps on each bottle for retail; KRA-issued stamps with QR code; counterfeit stamps offence; consumers can verify via SMS or KRA app; EGMS mandatory for all excisable goods including wine since 2017; importers + manufacturers register with KRA
notes: Kenya EGMS = pioneer African alcohol track-trace system; stamps on every bottle; KRA mobile app for verification; significantly reduced counterfeit; importer adds 3-5% cost for stamps; bonded warehouses for clearance

regulation_name: Codex General Standard for Wine — CXS 234 + Limits Reference
regulation_ref: Codex Alimentarius CXS 234-1999 (revised) + CXS 192-1995 (food additives) + CXS 193 (contaminants)
key_requirements: Codex CXS 234 = international wine standard reference; definitions; permitted oenological practices (cross-referenced OIV); analytical methods (sulfite, alcohol, methanol); contaminant limits (ochratoxin A, heavy metals); food additive use levels per CXS 192; codex labelling principles applied; voluntary but referenced by WTO TBT/SPS for regulatory purposes; basis for national regulations harmonization
notes: Codex wine standard referenced globally; not directly enforced but adopted by 188 member countries; OIV (International Organisation of Vine and Wine) closer reference for wine specifically; Codex Maximum Residue Limits (pesticides) binding for trade

regulation_name: Codex General Standard for Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (Wine)
regulation_ref: Codex CXS 1-1985 + CXS 146-1985 + GSFA (General Standard for Food Additives)
key_requirements: Codex CXS 1-1985 general labelling principles applicable to wine: name of food, list of ingredients (with exemption for alcoholic beverages historically being modernised), net contents, manufacturer name + address, country of origin, lot identification, date marking; CXS 146 for prepackaged in specific markets; GSFA controls allowable food additives in wine; allergens declaration developing standard; nutrition labelling general principles
notes: Codex labelling = reference for national authorities; wine ingredient labelling debated for years - EU now requires (Dec 2023); Codex 146 originally exempted alcohol from ingredient declaration; trends towards full labelling

regulation_name: ISO 17025 + ISO 22000 — Wine Laboratory + Food Safety
enforcement_body: ISO + IAF (International Accreditation Forum) + national accreditation bodies
key_requirements: ISO 17025 = wine analytical labs accreditation standard; required for wine labs producing export certifications; covers SO2 analysis, ABV measurement, contaminant testing; ISO 22000 = food safety management system applicable to wineries; HACCP integrated; certification voluntary but commercially essential for export; recognized through IAF MLA mutual recognition;OIV-MA methods reference for wine analysis
notes: ISO 17025 mandatory accreditation for wine export labs (many countries); ISO 22000 voluntary but increasingly required by buyers; OIV reference methods used in wine labs; BRCGS Wine cert option for retail customers

regulation_name: ISO 11035 — Sensory Analysis for Wine Quality
key_requirements: ISO 11035 = sensory analysis methodology for foods including wine; standardized vocabulary; testing room design (ISO 8589); panellist training requirements; difference tests, descriptive analysis, hedonic tests; ISO 13301 thresholds; widely applied in wine quality control + product development + regulatory tasting; AOC/DOC panel tastings often reference ISO methods; voluntary but professional best practice
notes: ISO sensory standards = international wine industry reference; tasting panels for DOCG (Italy), AOC (France) use principles; quality control wine labs apply; consumer testing for new products use ISO 11035 framework

regulation_name: UNECE Geneva Standard 21 — Wine Trade Marketing + Quality
regulation_ref: UNECE Standard 21 (Wine) - Working Party on Agricultural Quality Standards
enforcement_body: UN Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE) - Geneva Standards
key_requirements: UNECE Standard 21 = international standard for wine marketing and commercial quality control; definitions; trade quality classes; labelling minimum requirements; producer/importer/seller information; sampling for inspection; reference for member states (UNECE = 56 member states including EU, US, CA, RU, JP); voluntary but used as reference; UNECE Working Party on Quality of Agricultural Produce updates
notes: UNECE standards = voluntary reference for international agricultural trade; wine standard 21 less binding than OIV but cited in some bilateral trade contexts; harmonization with EU + Codex generally

regulation_name: UNECE Recommendation on Wine Labelling — Trade Facilitation
regulation_ref: UNECE Recommendation 23 (Wine Trade) + UN/CEFACT documentation standards
key_requirements: UNECE Trade facilitation recommendations applicable to wine cross-border movement; standardized commercial documentation (commercial invoice, packing list); UN/EDIFACT electronic data interchange for wine; reference for VI-1 form replacement; ATA Carnet not typically used for wine; UNECE United Nations Layout Key for trade documents; e-Single Window initiatives
notes: UNECE trade facilitation primarily affects logistics not directly wine substance; UN/EDIFACT standards used in EMCS electronic systems; recommendation 23 supports paperless wine trade

regulation_name: WADA Prohibited Substances List — Alcohol monitoring
regulation_ref: WADA Prohibited List 2024 + Annex Alcohol Monitoring + International Standard
enforcement_body: World Anti-Doping Agency + International Federations + National Anti-Doping Organizations
key_requirements: WADA Prohibited List historically classified alcohol as prohibited in certain sports (aeronautics, archery, automobile, karate, motorcycling, powerboating); alcohol removed from prohibited list 2018 - now sport federations decide individually; alcohol still monitored in specific sports for safety; wine = source of alcohol = relevant for athlete consumption; sport-specific competition rules apply; out-of-competition no alcohol prohibition
notes: WADA removed alcohol from prohibited list 2018; FIA (motorsport) maintains its own alcohol limits; aviation has separate medical standards; wine consumption by athletes governed by sport rules + general health

regulation_name: WADA Athletes' Guide — Wine and Alcohol Education
regulation_ref: WADA Athletes Guide + International Standard for Education (ISE) + Anti-Doping Code
key_requirements: WADA athlete education materials cover alcohol/wine considerations: hydration, recovery, supplement contamination risks (wine + supplements); WADA-certified labs test for alcohol when applicable; some sports federations (FIA, FAI, WA Karate) maintain prohibition; cooking wine in food = no doping risk; wine vinegar = trace; education emphasises athlete awareness
notes: WADA athlete education emphasises responsibility; recreational alcohol use by athletes not doping but health/career considerations; team sponsorship sometimes affects wine endorsement compatibility

regulation_name: OECD Health Statistics — Alcohol Consumption Reporting
regulation_ref: OECD Health Statistics framework + OECD Alcohol Policy Database
enforcement_body: OECD Health Division + national statistics offices
key_requirements: OECD collects + publishes alcohol consumption data (including wine) from member countries; standardized measurement (litres pure alcohol per capita); informs alcohol policy debates; wine specifically tracked; trends analysis (Mediterranean countries declining, emerging markets growing); link to health outcomes published; OECD recommendations not binding but influential; alcohol affordability + minimum unit pricing studies
notes: OECD = statistical reference for global wine consumption trends; influences national policy debates; alcohol affordability research published 2021 + 2023 supporting MUP discussion; not regulatory but policy reference

regulation_name: OECD Guidelines on Multinational Enterprises — Wine Industry Application
regulation_ref: OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises 2023 update + National Contact Points
key_requirements: OECD Guidelines MNE 2023 update strengthens responsible business conduct expectations applicable to wine multinationals (LVMH, Pernod Ricard, Concha y Toro etc.); supply chain due diligence; environmental sustainability (water, climate impact); labour rights vineyard workers; community impact wine tourism; tax responsibility; lobbying transparency; non-binding but referenced by NCPs investigations
notes: OECD MNE Guidelines wine industry growing focus; vineyard worker conditions (especially migrant labour CA, FR, IT); water usage drought regions; sustainability reporting expected; NCPs hear complaints (cases against winery rare but possible)

enforcement_body: EC DG SANTE + national competent authorities + DG Health and Food Safety
key_requirements: Single comprehensive framework for official food/feed controls; risk-based inspection; TRACES NT mandatory for imports; Common Health Entry Document (CHED-P for animal products); approved BCPs (Border Control Posts) entry points; verification on slaughterhouses, cutting plants, cold stores; horizontal traceability requirements; documentary, identity, physical checks; sanctions harmonization
notes: Replaced multiple sectoral controls regulations; TRACES NT mandatory since 14/12/2019; CHED-P for fresh meat from third countries; BCPs upgraded with EU funding; veterinarian inspector role formalized

regulation_name: EU Animal Health Law — Beef + Pork specific
regulation_ref: Regulation (EU) 2016/429 (Animal Health Law) + Implementing Regulation (EU) 2020/692 (animal products imports)
enforcement_body: EC DG SANTE + national veterinary authorities
key_requirements: Animal Health Law framework; disease prevention/control for terrestrial animals (cattle, pigs, sheep); listed diseases (FMD, BSE, classical swine fever, ASF); approved zones for imports; movement documentation; identification + registration database (cattle ear tags TRACES-linked); 2020/692 = list of authorized non-EU countries + establishments for meat imports
notes: BSE classified controlled risk; ASF (African Swine Fever) major impact on pork trade; cattle passport mandatory; SANCO database official EU establishment list publicly available; export to EU requires listed-country status

regulation_name: Decret 2002-1465 + Information sur l'origine des viandes fraiches
regulation_ref: Decret n 2002-1465 du 17 decembre 2002 + Decret 2018-775 modifie (etiquetage origine viandes)
enforcement_body: DGCCRF + DGAL + Direction departementale de la protection des populations
key_requirements: France: etiquetage obligatoire origine viandes fraiches (bovins, porcs, volailles, ovins, caprins): 'Ne en', 'Eleve en', 'Abattu en'; depuis 2002 pour bovins (BSE) etendu autres especes 2018; pictogramme + mention claire requise; restauration collective + restaurants doivent indiquer origine viande servie depuis 2017 (Loi Egalim); contraventions DGCCRF
notes: France pionnier etiquetage origine viande post-BSE 1996; Loi Egalim 2018 etend restauration; 'Viandes de France' label volontaire (filieres tracees); fraudes etiquetage origine sanctionnees DGCCRF; conflits avec Cour Justice UE sur etendue

regulation_name: Reglement sanitaire EUROFROID + chaine du froid
regulation_ref: Code de la consommation art. R. 412-1 + Arrete 21 dec 2009 (temperatures denrees) + Code rural L231
enforcement_body: DGCCRF + DGAL + Brigade Nationale Enquetes Veterinaires + Sanitaires
key_requirements: Chaine du froid viandes fraiches France: viande rouge max 7C (4C ideal); viande hachee max 2C; abats max 3C; transport refrigere obligatoire (tunnel + frigorifique); tracabilite temperatures continue (data loggers); supermarche presentoir refrigere conforme; restaurants frigos calibres; controles DGCCRF aleatoires; controle visuel + thermometre infrarouge
notes: Chaine froid critique pour viandes fraiches; BSE + Listeria + Salmonella + STEC = pathogenes surveilles; DGCCRF retraits + rappels reguliers; HACCP obligatoire restauration commerciale + collective

regulation_ref: Lebensmittelhygiene-Verordnung (LMHV) vom 8. August 2007 (BGBl. I S. 1816) + Tierische Lebensmittel-Hygieneverordnung
enforcement_body: BMEL + Bundesamt fuer Verbraucherschutz und Lebensmittelsicherheit (BVL) + Laender (Landesveterinaeraemter)
key_requirements: German implementation of EU food hygiene + Tierische LM-Hygieneverordnung (Tier-LMHV) for animal products; mandatory HACCP; veterinary inspection slaughterhouses; cold chain documentation; food chain information (FCI) - farm-to-slaughter; carcass + cut traceability; specified risk material (SRM) removal mandatory for cattle (BSE); cleaning + disinfection requirements; Salmonella + STEC monitoring
notes: Germany strict implementation EU food hygiene; Schlachthof (slaughterhouse) approval EU + national level; BVL central federal authority; recent scandals (Toennies COVID outbreak 2020) led to reforms

enforcement_body: BVL + Federal Ministry of Food and Agriculture (BMEL) + State authorities
key_requirements: Germany Veterinary Medicinal Products Act 2022 implements EU Reg 2019/6; antibiotic use in food animals strict monitoring; antimicrobial sensitivity testing; reporting obligations farmers; antibiotic-free certification voluntary; residue limits in meat per Council Reg 470/2009 + MRL Regulation; slaughterhouse residue testing mandatory random sampling
notes: Germany high focus on AMR reduction; antibiotic use halved 2014-2022 in food animals; veterinary prescription mandatory; reserve antibiotics for human use restricted in livestock; Greenpeace + consumer NGOs pressure

regulation_ref: DM 31 gennaio 2002 (Anagrafe bovina) + Reg UE 1760/2000 + Reg UE 2016/429
enforcement_body: Ministero della Salute + Servizi Veterinari ASL + IZS (Istituti Zooprofilattici Sperimentali)
key_requirements: Italia Banca Dati Nazionale (BDN) bovini: identificazione individuale ogni bovino (orecchino + microchip opzionale); passaporto bovino; registrazione tutti movimenti azienda-mattatoio-cella; tracciabilita totale fino punto vendita; etichettatura obbligatoria origine carne bovina (Paese nascita, allevamento, macellazione, sezionamento); informazioni catena alimentare (FCI); controlli ASL + NAS Carabinieri
notes: Italia BDN modello EU per tracciabilita; etichettatura origine carne bovina pioniera (Italia + Francia 2002); NAS Carabinieri controlli anti-frode aggressivi; mozzarella di bufala campania DOP separate

regulation_name: Decreto del Made in Italy + Etichettatura origine carni
regulation_ref: Decreto MIPAAF 26 luglio 2017 (carni suine, ovine, caprine, pollame) + Reg UE 1169/2011 art. 26
enforcement_body: MIPAAF + Ministero della Salute + ICQRF + ASL
key_requirements: Italia obbligo origine carni suine + ovine + caprine + pollame da 2017: 'Allevato in [paese]' obbligatorio + 'Macellato in [paese]' se diverso; per pollame data macellazione (freshness); per suini origine 4 mesi minimo allevamento; etichetta italiana obbligatoria; controlli ICQRF + NAS; deroghe carne lavorata (salumi DOP/IGP regolati separatamente)
notes: Italia decreti origine carni post-2017 vasto impatto retail; Coop, Esselunga, Conad etichettano fortemente origine italiana; export pork EU pressure; salumi DOP (Prosciutto di Parma) regole separate cahier des charges

regulation_name: Real Decreto 1376/2003 — Comercio minorista carne fresca
regulation_ref: Real Decreto 1376/2003 + Real Decreto 1380/2002 (presentacion carnes) + Reglamentos UE
enforcement_body: AESAN (Agencia Espanola Seguridad Alimentaria) + CCAA + Veterinarios Inspectores
key_requirements: Espana comercio minorista carne fresca: requisitos sanitarios establecimientos venta; vitrinas refrigeradas independentes para carnes/pescados; trazabilidad detallada; etiquetado obligatorio especie animal + origen (pais + region en algunos casos); presentacion carcasas (cabeza, riñones, lengua) regulada por categoria; carnicero autorizado por CCAA; controles ITV-equivalent del establecimiento
notes: Espana red densa carniceros tradicionales (>15.000 establecimientos); CCAA autoridades sanitarias diferentes (Catalunya, Andalucia, Madrid mas estrictas); cordero lechal + ternera blanca + iberico (cerdo) categorias especiales; trazabilidad SIGGAN bovinos

regulation_ref: Real Decreto 728/2007 + Programa Nacional de control de Salmonella + Reg UE 2160/2003
key_requirements: Espana programa control Salmonella en gallinas ponedoras + pollos + cerdos + bovinos; testeo regular en granjas + mataderos; serotipos objetivos (Enteritidis, Typhimurium); sacrificio rebanos infectados; control STEC en carne picada/bovino; Programa Nacional aplicado por CCAA; resultados notificados a EFSA; reduccion progresiva prevalencia exigida UE
notes: Espana programas zoonosis activos; Salmonella prevalence menor que UE media; EHEC O104 brote 2011 (alemania) impacto regulatorio Espana; consumidores espanoles consumen mucho carne fresca

regulation_name: Dutch Levensmiddelenwet + Verordening hygiene van vlees
regulation_ref: Warenwet + Verordening (EU) 853/2004 (animal origin food hygiene) + Verordening (EU) 854/2004
enforcement_body: NVWA (Nederlandse Voedsel- en Warenautoriteit) + Veterinary Public Health
key_requirements: Nederland: NVWA inspections in slaughterhouses; ante-mortem + post-mortem veterinary inspection mandatory; specified risk material (SRM) removal cattle >30 months; hygiene controls cutting plants; HACCP system mandatory; cold chain 7C max for fresh meat; trace recording; food chain information (FCI); GMP certification voluntary on top
notes: Netherlands = major EU meat exporter (pork to Germany, China, Japan); large-scale slaughterhouses (Vion, Plukon); NVWA strict controls; Q-fever + dioxins crises shaped reform; antibiotic reduction targets met

regulation_ref: Voluntary industry schemes (IKB-CRA, KKM, GlobalGAP) + ZBC pre-conditions
key_requirements: Voluntary quality schemes mandatory in Dutch retail/export: IKB-CRA (Integraal Keten Beheer Centrale Registratie Aanvoer) for pork; KKM for veal/beef; cover farm + transport + slaughterhouse; antibiotic use registered; mortality monitoring; animal welfare scoring; supply chain integrated; certification audit annual
notes: Dutch retail (Albert Heijn, Jumbo) require IKB/KKM certification for fresh meat; export markets request; not legal requirement but commercial; Better Life ladder (Dierenbescherming) scoring widely adopted

regulation_name: UK Food Information Regulations 2014 (FIR) — Meat labelling
regulation_ref: Food Information Regulations 2014 (SI 2014/1855) + UK retained EU law
enforcement_body: Food Standards Agency (FSA) + Food Standards Scotland + Trading Standards
key_requirements: UK FIR 2014 implements EU FIC retained post-Brexit; mandatory country of origin for fresh, chilled, frozen beef, pork, poultry, sheep, goat meat; 'Origin'/'Country of provenance'; for ground meat (mince) specific rules; allergen labelling; nutrition declaration; date marking (use-by for fresh meat); ingredient labelling for preparations; species declaration; pictogram pregnancy not for meat
notes: UK retained FIC + amendments; Brexit no major change to meat labelling rules; FSA enforces; horse meat scandal 2013 reformed traceability; Red Tractor scheme major industry compliance

regulation_name: UK Veterinary Medicines Regulations 2013 — Antibiotic use
regulation_ref: Veterinary Medicines Regulations 2013 + RUMA Targets Task Force
enforcement_body: Veterinary Medicines Directorate (VMD) + DEFRA + Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons
key_requirements: UK Veterinary Medicines Regulations 2013: veterinary prescription required for all antibiotics food animals; restricted use of HP-CIAs (Highest Priority Critically Important Antimicrobials) - colistin, fluoroquinolones; mandatory recording on farm; withdrawal periods strict; RUMA Targets 2024 sector reduction targets; Red Tractor + Soil Association schemes additional standards; eMB-Pigs database monitoring
notes: UK antimicrobial use food animals reduced 53% 2014-2022 (RUMA report); leading globally; HP-CIA use further restricted 2024; colistin near-zero; cattle + pigs major reductions; Northern Ireland aligned with EU post-Brexit

regulation_name: Loi suisse sur les denrees alimentaires LDAl + ODAlOUs
regulation_ref: Loi federale sur les denrees alimentaires (LDAl, RS 817.0) + ODAlOUs (RS 817.02)
enforcement_body: OSAV (Office federal securite alimentaire) + chimistes cantonaux + veterinaires officiels
key_requirements: Suisse LDAl harmonisation avec UE alimentation; viandes fraiches: chaine du froid max 7C; tracabilite obligatoire; etiquetage trilingue (DE/FR/IT) selon canton vente; origine obligatoire pour viandes fraiches (Schweizer Fleisch / Viande Suisse); abattoirs agrees OSAV; veterinaires officiels controle ante/post-mortem; specifications races/elevage CH (Mutterkuhhaltung)
notes: Suisse marche carne autosuffisant 70% (importation Italie, Allemagne, Pologne); OSAV controles stricts; SRM removal applique; UE accord bilateral RM permet libre acces relativement; antibiotiques reduits 50% 2010-2022

regulation_name: Suisse Ordonnance sur la designation des viandes
