regulation_ref: Ordonnance sur la designation des viandes (RS 817.022.108) + Ordonnance Agriculture biologique RS 910.18
enforcement_body: OSAV + OFAG + Bio Suisse + IP-Suisse + canton autorites
key_requirements: Suisse ordonnance designation viandes precise mentions valorisantes: 'Schweizer Fleisch' = produit Suisse (animal ne, eleve, abattu, transformee CH); 'Viande Suisse' = idem en francais; 'IP-Suisse' = Integrated Production label; 'Bio Suisse' = bio CH; 'Mutterkuhhaltung' (vache allaitante); 'Naturabeef'; controles labels OSAV + organes certification; tracabilite blockchain pilote
notes: Suisse labels qualite carne forte penetration (>50% retail); IP-Suisse principal label production integree; Bio Suisse certification rigide; Naturabeef + Coop Natura-Beef positionne premium; suisse-allemand 'Schweizer Fleisch' fortement market

regulation_name: Norwegian Matloven (Food Act) + Forskrift om kjoett
regulation_ref: Matloven LOV-2003-12-19-124 + Forskrift om kjoettkontroll FOR-2008-09-04-1058
key_requirements: Norvege Matloven cadre alimentaire; Forskrift kjottkontroll: contrôle viandes; abattoirs agrees Mattilsynet; veterinaire officiel obligatoire; ante/post-mortem inspection; SRM retire cattle >12 mois (plus strict que UE); tracabilite NORWEGCATTLE base donnees; etiquetage origine norvegien obligatoire (norsk kjott); chaine froid 0-4C viandes fraiches; programmes salmonella zero (EU exemption status)
notes: Norvege exempt status Salmonella maintain EU status special; viande importee soumise tests salmonella stricts; production locale 90% besoin domestique; importation pas mais accord bilateral UE; rennes/sauvage chasse regule sapmi

regulation_ref: Forskrift om animalske biprodukter (FOR-2007-10-27-1254) + EU ABP Regulation 1069/2009 EEA
key_requirements: Norvege traitement sous-produits animaux (Animal By-Products); categories 1, 2, 3; SRM cattle ABM Category 1 (incineration only); offal/blood Cat 3 (rendering); pet food production permitted Cat 3; reception centers + biofuel plants + pet food plants registered; tracabilite ABP electronic; EEA agreement covers ABP rules
notes: Norvege ABP regulation aligned EU; Veksaltjenesten national ABP collection service; rendering plants approved; sea-based ABP disposal interdit; sustainability angle reuse

regulation_name: Russian Federal Law 88-FZ Technical Regulation on Milk + Meat Products
regulation_ref: TR TS 034/2013 (meat and meat products) + Federal Law 184-FZ Technical Regulation
key_requirements: Russia/EAEU TR TS 034/2013 technical regulation for meat and meat products; mandatory for all meat products; classification (chilled, frozen, ground); microbiological + physico-chemical standards; labelling Russian language mandatory; allergens; producer/importer details; manufacturing date + expiry; veterinary certificate (Form 2/3) for movement; Mercury system electronic veterinary certification mandatory
notes: Russia Mercury electronic veterinary certification mandatory since 2018 for all meat movements; integrated with Eurasian system; foreign imports require Rosselkhoznadzor inspection of foreign establishments; sanctions 2014+ closed EU meat market

regulation_ref: Federal Law 4979-1 Veterinary Service + Rosselkhoznadzor Order 81/2009 (foreign establishment list)
key_requirements: Foreign meat slaughterhouses + cutting plants + cold stores exporting to Russia must be approved by Rosselkhoznadzor; on-site inspection by RSN veterinarians; listed establishments published publicly; approval can be suspended/withdrawn (used as trade tool); EAEU member states reciprocal recognition; sanctions countries (EU, US, AU, NO, CA) banned 2014+; emerging markets (BR, AR, PY, BY) increased
notes: RSN approval = effective non-tariff barrier; political use sanctions on countries (Brazil pork suspension 2022, Belarus closer); supplier countries lobby for approval; reapproval after suspension via audit

regulation_name: Turkish Food Codex — Meat and Meat Products
regulation_ref: Turk Gida Kodeksi Et ve Et Urunleri Tebligi (Notification on Meat) No. 2018/52
enforcement_body: Tarim ve Orman Bakanligi + Gida ve Yem Kontrol Daire Baskanligi
key_requirements: Turkish Food Codex Meat Products Notification 2018/52; harmonized with EU framework; meat categories (red meat, poultry, processed meat); composition standards; mandatory labelling Turkish + optionally English; allergens (eggs, milk, gluten if used); halal certification voluntary but mandatory perception domestic market; nitrate/nitrite limits cured meats; lean meat content claims regulated
notes: Turkey Food Codex post-2018 EU-aligned; halal slaughter ritual obligatory practice >99% Turkish meat market; export to EU compliant slaughterhouses required (limited list); domestic market huge red meat consumption

regulation_name: Turkish Halal Slaughter + ESK Et ve Sut Kurumu Procurement
regulation_ref: Tarimda Hayvan Refahi Yonetmeligi + Religious Affairs Directorate (Diyanet) Halal Standards
enforcement_body: Tarim ve Orman Bakanligi + Diyanet (Religious Affairs) + ESK (state procurement)
key_requirements: Turkey halal slaughter mandatory for domestic market: tasmiya (Bismillah) recitation; cut throat with single sharp blade; conscious animal (no stunning required by traditional view but reversible stunning permitted by some authorities); blood drainage; halal certificate (HAK, GIMDES, others); ESK state procurement entity buys for strategic supply; price intervention; significant red meat imports under tariff quota due to domestic shortages
notes: Turkey halal slaughter universal domestic; export halal certified to MENA + ASEAN Muslim markets; ESK Et ve Sut Kurumu state buying program; Russia + Brazil imports significant; antibiotics + growth hormones banned (clenbuterol detection major issue)

regulation_name: Ukrainian Law on Veterinary Medicine + Animal Identification
regulation_ref: Zakon Ukrainy Pro veterynarnu medytsynu No. 2498-XII + Order 39/2013 (animal identification)
enforcement_body: Derzhprodspozyvsluzh (State Service for Food Safety and Consumer Protection) + Veterinary Service
key_requirements: Ukraine veterinary regulation; mandatory animal identification (cattle ear tags + database AKKO); livestock movement traceable; slaughterhouse veterinary inspection; export establishments approved by importing countries; halal slaughter facilities for MENA export market; African Swine Fever surveillance (major regional issue); ISO + HACCP for export facilities; EU + China + Saudi Arabia approval lists
notes: Ukraine major meat producer + exporter pre-2022; poultry leader (MHP exporter EU); pork hit hard by ASF + war; cattle modest; war 2022+ severely disrupts production + export logistics; Black Sea grain corridor critical for feed supply

regulation_name: Ukraine DCFTA Meat Quotas + EU Sanitary Certification
regulation_ref: Association Agreement UA-EU + DCFTA Annex tariff rate quotas + EU Implementing Regulation 2015/2447
enforcement_body: Ministry of Economic Development + Ministry of Agrarian Policy + EU DG TRADE
key_requirements: Ukraine-EU DCFTA tariff rate quotas for meat exports: beef + veal (12,000t), pork (40,000t), poultry (90,000t including 70,000t boneless + 20,000t additional); annual administered by EU; certificate of conformity mandatory; EU veterinary establishments approval; salmonella + STEC monitoring; war-emergency additional quotas suspended 2022+; sea blockade disrupts export logistics
notes: DCFTA TRQs significant Ukrainian poultry export route to EU; MHP largest Ukrainian poultry exporter; war disrupted but corridor + rail alternatives; Ukrainian export reorienting toward African + Middle East + China markets

regulation_name: EAEU TR TS 034/2013 + TR TS 021/2011 — Meat Safety Regulations
regulation_ref: TR TS 034/2013 Meat and meat products + TR TS 021/2011 Food safety + EEC implementing decisions
enforcement_body: Eurasian Economic Commission + Rosselkhoznadzor + Belarusian Veterinary + KazAgro
key_requirements: EAEU joint regulation: TR TS 021/2011 horizontal food safety (microbiology, contaminants); TR TS 034/2013 specific meat products (composition, classification, labelling); EAC mark mandatory; mutual recognition certifications; common veterinary certificate (Form 1); harmonized maximum residue limits (antibiotics, growth promoters banned); export-import within EAEU duty-free; Customs Tariff Code common
notes: EAEU meat regulations create internal market; TR TS 034 detailed product specifications; Belarus + Kazakhstan + Armenia + Kyrgyzstan + Russia all bound; foreign exports must comply with TR TS rules + obtain EAC certification or declaration

regulation_ref: EEC Decision 317 + national implementations + Mercury (Russia) integrated EAEU veterinary system
enforcement_body: Rosselkhoznadzor + EAEU national vet services + Eurasian Customs
key_requirements: EAEU electronic veterinary certification system: all meat movements within EAEU electronically certified; Mercury (Rosselkhoznadzor) integrated with Kazakhstan + Belarus + Armenia + Kyrgyzstan systems; veterinary certificate auto-generated; QR code + electronic signature; consumer verification mobile app; foreign imports get electronic certificate at first EAEU entry
notes: EAEU electronic veterinary cert = world's most extensive single-bloc system; Mercury alone processes 100M+ certificates/year (Russia); replacing paper completely; integration ongoing 2023-2025

regulation_name: FSIS Federal Meat Inspection Act (FMIA) + Poultry Products Inspection Act
regulation_ref: Federal Meat Inspection Act (21 USC 601-695) + Poultry Products Inspection Act (21 USC 451-472) + 9 CFR 300-590
enforcement_body: USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS) + Inspectors-in-Charge slaughter plants
key_requirements: FSIS mandatory inspection: every slaughtered animal (ante-mortem + post-mortem) by FSIS veterinarian; cattle, swine, sheep, goats, horses + poultry covered; HACCP mandatory; Pathogen Reduction (Salmonella, E. coli O157:H7 STEC, Listeria) sampling; mandatory inspection at slaughterhouse + processing plants; mark of inspection (USDA Inspected stamp); compulsory recall authority since 2008
notes: FSIS continuous inspection slaughterhouses (1 inspector per shift minimum); USDA mark required for interstate commerce; HACCP since 1996 (Pathogen Reduction); E. coli O157:H7 adulterant 1994; recently 6 STEC strains added; Salmonella declared adulterant in 2024

regulation_name: FSIS Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) — Beef + Lamb
regulation_ref: Agricultural Marketing Act 1946 amended 2008 + COOL Final Rule (74 FR 2657) + 7 CFR Parts 60-65
enforcement_body: USDA AMS (Agricultural Marketing Service) + FSIS implementing
key_requirements: US COOL: muscle cuts of beef, pork (note: pork COOL repealed 2015 post-WTO ruling), lamb, chicken, fish, peanuts, ginseng, fresh + frozen fruits + vegetables; for beef, lamb, chicken: 'Product of US' if born/raised/slaughtered US; mixed: 'Product of US and...' or specific; ground meat: list countries; exemptions food service + restaurants; voluntary 'Product of USA' label rule clarified by USDA-FSIS 2024 (only US-born and raised + slaughtered eligible)
notes: US COOL major political issue; WTO 2015 ruled COOL discriminated against Canadian + Mexican pork - US repealed pork/beef COOL retail; beef voluntary 'Product of USA' clarified Dec 2024 requires US-born; ranchers strongly support, packers oppose

regulation_name: California Proposition 12 — Farm Animal Confinement Standards
regulation_ref: California Health and Safety Code 25990-25994 (Prop 12 - approved 2018) + CDFA + USDA implementing
enforcement_body: California Department of Food and Agriculture + California Department of Public Health
key_requirements: California Prop 12 (2018) effective 2022: minimum space requirements for breeding pigs (24 sq ft/sow), veal calves (43 sq ft), egg-laying hens (cage-free); applies to meat + eggs sold in California regardless of origin state/country; certification system; affects national pork industry significantly (California = 13% of US pork demand); Supreme Court upheld 2023 (National Pork Producers v Ross); reciprocal scheme expanding
notes: Prop 12 most extensive farm animal welfare law in US; effective sows + veal Jan 2022, hens Jan 2024; pork industry sued + lost SCOTUS 2023; pork exports to California from non-compliant farms restricted; broad pork industry compliance forced; Massachusetts similar Question 3

regulation_ref: California AB 1437 (Animal Welfare) + 3 CCR 1320-1326 + CDFA enforcement
enforcement_body: California Department of Food and Agriculture
key_requirements: California AB 1437 (2008 expansion of confinement standards); animal cruelty under California PC 597 enhanced for production; veal crate ban since 2010; foie gras production + sale ban (litigation ongoing); animal cruelty whistleblower protections; ag-gag laws struck down by 9th Circuit; egg cage-free transition + traceability; CFA enforces; private prosecutions Animal Legal Defense Fund
notes: California leads US animal welfare regulation; foie gras ban repeatedly litigated; Mercy for Animals + ALDF active; Trader Joe's, Whole Foods sourcing aligned; conventional meat industry transition forced

regulation_name: New York Pet Source Disclosure + Meat Labeling Laws
regulation_ref: NY Agriculture and Markets Law Article 5-A + 1 NYCRR Part 351-352
enforcement_body: New York Department of Agriculture and Markets + Department of Health
key_requirements: NY meat labelling: complies with federal COOL; state-level mandatory disclosure for kosher + halal products (separate Kosher Law 1881, Halal Food Protection Act 2003); kosher certification disclosure required; halal certifier identification; pet meat (rendered) regulated separately; New York City additional health code requirements; restaurants menu labelling calorie counts (NYC Reg 81.50)
notes: NY Kosher Law historic; large kosher market NYC = strict supervision; Halal Food Protection Act unique state law requiring halal disclosure; restaurants menu calorie labels NYC pioneer

regulation_name: New York Food Safety Act + 21st Century Food Safety Modernization
regulation_ref: NY Agriculture and Markets Law + Public Health Law + 1 NYCRR Subchapter B
enforcement_body: NY State Department of Agriculture and Markets + Local Health Departments
key_requirements: NY State + Local food safety enforcement for retail butcher shops + restaurants; permits required for slaughtering + processing; sanitary food production rules; refrigeration standards; HACCP voluntary at retail but required at custom slaughter; mobile slaughter units regulated; deer + game meat custom processing rules; small farm slaughter exemption (under 1000 birds/year); NYS Grade label for state products
notes: NY mix federal + state inspection (some plants state-only); custom slaughter regulations specific; small farm exemption supports local agriculture; New York Grade A label marketing

regulation_name: Canada Safe Food for Canadians Regulations (SFCR) — Meat Products
regulation_ref: Safe Food for Canadians Regulations SOR/2018-108 + Meat Inspection Regulations (legacy parts)
enforcement_body: Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) + Health Canada + provincial authorities
key_requirements: Canada SFCR 2019 consolidated food regulations; preventive controls (HACCP-like) mandatory; meat establishment licence required (federal SFCR licence); animal slaughter under CFIA continuous inspection for federally registered establishments (interprovincial + export); provincial inspection for local-only; traceability + record-keeping; labelling in English + French; nutrition labelling exempt for unpackaged meat; allergen declaration
notes: Canada SFCR major modernization 2019; federal vs provincial inspection distinction maintained; SFCR licence mandatory; bilingual labelling EN+FR; CFIA primary federal authority

regulation_name: Canada Beef + Cattle Identification System (CCIA) + TRACE
regulation_ref: Health of Animals Act + CCIA Regulations + Canadian Cattle Identification Agency database
enforcement_body: CFIA + Canadian Cattle Identification Agency (CCIA) + provincial agriculture
key_requirements: Canada mandatory cattle identification: RFID ear tag (CCIA tag) at birth or before leaving herd of origin; identification number scanned at sale + slaughter; CCIA database tracks movement; mandatory at slaughter; bison + sheep similar systems; pork through PigTRACE since 2014; poultry voluntary; export certification dependent on traceability data; integrated with TRACE+ (provincial traceability)
notes: Canada CCIA = world's first national mandatory cattle ID system 2001; BSE response Canada-US; export markets (US, Japan, China, EU) require traceability; ear tag application mandatory by ranchers

regulation_ref: Norma Oficial Mexicana NOM-194-SSA1-2004 (productos carnicos crudos) + NOM-008-SAGARPA-2002 + NOM-009-ZOO-1994
key_requirements: Mexico NOM-194 productos carnicos: especificaciones sanitarias carnes frescas; NOM-008 inspeccion sanitaria mataderos (rastros TIF - Tipo Inspeccion Federal); NOM-009 transporte vehiculos refrigerados; HACCP recomendado obligatorio TIF; productos NoTIF venta local solamente; etiquetado origen espanol obligatorio; trazabilidad SINIIGA (Sistema Nacional de Identificacion Individual de Ganado)
notes: Mexico TIF mataderos = certificacion exportacion EE.UU. + Asia; Rastro Municipal (no-TIF) abasto local; 95% carne Mexico es Rastro Municipal; SINIIGA arete amarillo cattle ID; SENASICA controla TIF + epizootias

regulation_ref: Acuerdo SAGARPA + SINIIGA + Programa Nacional de Movilizacion del Ganado
key_requirements: SINIIGA Mexico identifies cattle individually; arete amarillo (yellow ear tag) RFID compatible; movilizacion ganado autorized via SIRPAC documentation; tracking from origin to slaughterhouse; foot-and-mouth disease zone classification; brucellosis + tuberculosis surveillance; export markets (US, Japan, Korea, EU) require traceability records; modern Mexico cattle industry largely compliant
notes: Mexico SINIIGA 70%+ cattle tagged; arete oblig todas movilizaciones; FMD-free status critical export; brucellosis erradicacion progresiva

regulation_name: Brazil RIISPOA - Regulamento Inspecao Industrial Sanitaria Produtos Origem Animal
regulation_ref: Decreto N 9.013/2017 (RIISPOA) + Lei 1.283/1950 + Instrucoes Normativas MAPA
enforcement_body: Ministerio da Agricultura, Pecuaria e Abastecimento (MAPA) + Servico de Inspecao Federal (SIF)
key_requirements: Brazil RIISPOA modernizado 2017; inspecao federal (SIF) frigorificos export + interestaduais; inspecao estadual (SIE) para venda dentro do estado; inspecao municipal (SIM) somente municipal; PSO (Programa de Autocontrole das Industrias) HACCP; inspecao continua veterinario federal; etiqueta SIF + carimbo SIF carcacas; trazabilidade SISBOV obrigatoria bovinos
notes: Brazil maior exportador carne bovina + frango mundial; SIF certificacao essential export; SISBOV bovinos 100% rastreaveis para mercados premium (EU, China); JBS, Marfrig, Minerva grandes produtores; recente escandalos (Carne Fraca 2017) reformaram controles

regulation_name: Brazil SISBOV Sistema Brasileiro de Identificacao e Certificacao de Bovinos
regulation_ref: Instrucao Normativa MAPA N 17/2006 + atualizacoes SISBOV + ERAS (lista de fazendas elegiveis)
enforcement_body: MAPA + SISBOV operadores certificados + frigorificos exportadores
key_requirements: SISBOV Brasil: identificacao individual bovino com brinco; tracabilidade nascimento ate abate; ERAS = registro fazendas habilitadas exportacao para mercados sensiveis (EU); auditorias terceira parte; vacinacao brucelose + tuberculose + aftosa registrada; mercado UE exige SISBOV completo; demais mercados (China, Russia, Egito) menos exigentes
notes: SISBOV essencial export bovino EU + Israel + Chile; 14M+ bovinos no SISBOV (de 220M total Brasil); FMD-free with vaccination status; Trofeu Brasil 2024 sustentabilidade certificacao adicional; deflorestacao Amazonia pressao internacional

regulation_name: SENASA Argentina - Sistema de Trazabilidad SARIIGA
regulation_ref: Resolucion SENASA 754/2006 + Resolucion 437/2018 + Codigo Alimentario Argentino
enforcement_body: Servicio Nacional de Sanidad y Calidad Agroalimentaria (SENASA)
key_requirements: Argentina SENASA cattle identification: caravana auricular + RFID; tracking from birth to slaughter; SARIIGA (Sistema Argentino de Identificacion Individual de Ganado de Carne); SENASA frigorificos certified Categoria A (export EU + sensitive) Categoria B (other export) Categoria C (domestic only); FMD vaccination zones; FMD-free without vaccination zones Patagonia; trichinella testing pork mandatory
notes: Argentina major exporter beef + EU Hilton quota beneficiary; Patagonia FMD-free without vaccination = premium markets eligibility; SENASA Categoria A frigorificos lista vital; bistecs Argentina exportados >100 paises

regulation_name: Codigo Alimentario Argentino — Capitulo VI Productos carnicos
regulation_ref: Codigo Alimentario Argentino (Ley 18.284) Capitulo VI + Resolucion Conjunta SAGyP+SC 21/95
enforcement_body: ANMAT + Secretaria de Agricultura, Ganaderia y Pesca + SENASA
key_requirements: CAA Capitulo VI Productos carnicos: clasificacion carne (fresca, refrigerada, congelada, picada); composicion (porcentaje grasa max picada); aditivos permitidos (nitratos, nitritos para curados solamente); etiquetado obligatorio espanol; lote, fecha vencimiento; importadores deben registrarse RNPA + RNE; ANMAT vigilancia post-mercado; PROFECO equivalente PROFECO controla informacion consumidor
notes: CAA marco general Argentina; carnes saladas (cecina, jamon crudo) regulados separadamente; embutidos categoria diferente; clenbuterol uso ilegal pero historicamente detectado en cattle imported; lab analyses certified

regulation_name: Chile SAG Reglamento de Carnes + Sistema de Identificacion Individual Bovina (SIIBO)
regulation_ref: Decreto Ley 3.557/1980 + DS 28/2007 (Sistema Identificacion Bovina) + Reglamento Sanitario Alimentos
enforcement_body: Servicio Agricola y Ganadero (SAG) + ISP (Instituto Salud Publica)
key_requirements: Chile SAG SIIBO: identificacion bovino individual; caravana SAG + microchip; trazabilidad nacimiento abate; FMD-free without vaccination (status premium); plantas faenadoras certificadas SAG; export markets (US, China, Korea, Japan, EU) reciben SIIBO information; HACCP obligatorio; ISP regulates labelling food safety; etiquetado obligatorio espanol; mariscos certificacion separada
notes: Chile FMD-free without vaccination 1981; status premium acceso mercados; SAG SIIBO modelo regional; Chile menor productor que Argentina + Brasil pero export significativo (>$2bn/ano); FTAs amplios (UE, US, China, India, Japon)

regulation_name: Chile Reglamento Sanitario Alimentos + Ley 20.606 etiquetado
regulation_ref: Reglamento Sanitario de los Alimentos (RSA) Decreto N 977/96 + Ley 20.606 (etiquetado nutricional) + DS 13/2015
enforcement_body: Ministerio Salud + ISP + SEREMI Salud regional
key_requirements: Chile RSA: composicion + clases carnes; etiquetado obligatorio castellano; Ley 20.606 sellos negros 'ALTO EN' (sodio, grasas saturadas, azucares, calorias) - aplicabilidad limitada a carnes con aditivos; carnes procesadas (embutidos, hamburguesas pre-cocinados) sujetas a sellos; carnes frescas exentas etiquetado calorico (no obligatorio); allergenos declaracion; importadores cumplimiento
notes: Chile sellos negros (warning labels) pioneer regional; aplicable productos procesados; carne fresca exenta; productos carnicos curados (embutidos) sujetos sellos sodio + grasa; modelo internacional copiado (Mexico, Peru)

regulation_name: Colombia INVIMA + ICA Resoluciones carnes frescas
regulation_ref: Decreto 1500/2007 (Sistema Oficial Inspeccion Vigilancia Carne) + Resoluciones INVIMA + ICA
enforcement_body: INVIMA + ICA (Instituto Colombiano Agropecuario) + Ministerio Salud
key_requirements: Colombia: Decreto 1500/2007 establece sistema oficial inspeccion carne; INVIMA controla plantas beneficio + procesadoras; ICA controla salud animal (FMD-free with vaccination zones); plantas Categoria Nacional vs Categoria Local; HACCP obligatorio Categoria Nacional; trazabilidad SINIGAN bovinos; etiquetado obligatorio espanol; importacion via INVIMA registro sanitario
notes: Colombia FMD-free with vaccination most zones; Categoria Nacional plantas certificacion exportacion; SINIGAN sistema identificacion bovinos creciendo; INVIMA + ICA coordinacion mejorada

enforcement_body: INVIMA + Ministerio Salud y Proteccion Social
key_requirements: Colombia BPM obligatorias industria alimentos; HACCP para riesgo medio-alto (carnes); Resolucion 2674/2013; capacitacion personal; control plagas; trazabilidad lotes; Resolucion 4506/2013 etiquetado nutricional aplicable a carnes procesadas (no frescas); Decreto 539/2014 carnes preparaciones (embutidos) reglamento especifico; sellos nutricionales 'Exceso de...' sodio/azucar/grasa saturada implementados 2022
notes: Colombia adopta sellos frontales 'Exceso de' 2022 modelo Chile-Mexico; aplicable productos procesados; carnes embutidas sodio elevado triggea sellos; INVIMA controla cumplimiento; sanciones progresivas

regulation_ref: Resolucion GMC N 40/97 (Identificacion productos pre-envasados) + N 80/96 (Carnes y Productos Carnicos) + MAS 200
enforcement_body: Grupo Mercado Comun + SENASA (AR) + MAPA (BR) + INAC (UY) + SENAVE (PY)
key_requirements: MERCOSUR harmonization carnes: definiciones comunes (carne, embutidos, salazones); inspeccion sanitaria intra-bloque reconocida; mercosulinos circulacion via certificado sanitario internacional; FMD-free + safety standards comunes; rastreabilidad mutua bovinos (SISBOV-BR, SARIIGA-AR, SIIBO-CL); etiquetado bilingue PT/ES facilitado; importacion terceros paises arancel externo comun
notes: MERCOSUR major beef-exporting bloc; AR + BR + UY top 5 mundial; PY emergiendo; FMD status mixed (Patagonia + UY FMD-free sin vacuna; resto vacunados); UE-MERCOSUR FTA 2024 cuota 99,000t beef en cuotas

regulation_name: MERCOSUR Animal Health Information System + Sanitary Certificates
regulation_ref: Resolucion GMC + ICZ (Information Comm Zoonosis) + OIE/WOAH integration
enforcement_body: Servicios Veterinarios Oficiales + Comite Permanente Sanidad Animal
key_requirements: MERCOSUR animal health coordination: shared FMD status reporting; ASF surveillance; BSE control; mutual recognition vet certificates; OIE/WOAH WAHIS integration; cross-border movement permits; bovine + porcine + ovine reciprocal recognition; sanitary corridors during outbreaks; vaccination programs harmonization (FMD)
notes: MERCOSUR FMD-free zone progressing 2025-2027 plan; UY first FMD-free without vaccination South America; AR Patagonia same; BR FMD-free with vaccination achieved 2023; Paraguay status uneven

regulation_name: China Food Safety Law + GB 2762/2763 contaminants
regulation_ref: Food Safety Law 2015 (revised 2021) + GB 2762-2022 (contaminants) + GB 2763-2021 (pesticide MRLs)
enforcement_body: SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) + GACC + Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Affairs
key_requirements: China Food Safety Law: stringent post-2015 reform; HACCP mandatory; mandatory tracebility; GB 2762 maximum residue limits for heavy metals + contaminants meat; GB 2763 pesticide MRLs (10,000+ MRLs); ASF zoning post-2018 outbreak; pork market disrupted; cattle imports surge; SAMR + GACC enforcement; foreign exporters require GACC facility registration (orders 248/249/2022)
notes: China = world's largest meat consumer + importer; ASF reduced pork production 30% 2018-2020; recovered 2023+; cattle import from BR, AR, AU, US, NZ surging; GACC facility registration mandatory; SAMR penalties severe

regulation_name: China GACC Customs Order 248/249 — Foreign meat exporter registration
regulation_ref: Customs Order 248 (Registration of overseas producers of imported food) + 249 (Administrative Measures food imports/exports)
enforcement_body: General Administration of Customs of China (GACC) + State Department
key_requirements: GACC 248/249 effective Jan 1 2022: foreign meat facilities exporting to China must register with GACC (CIFER system); facility-by-facility approval; product categories specific; renewal periodic; failure to register = automatic refusal at port; >40,000 meat facilities globally registered; China can suspend/withdraw facility approval as trade tool; halal meat separate certification (HALMUI)
notes: GACC 248/249 = major reform requiring all foreign food producers register; meat producers especially impacted; CIFER online registration system; political tool used by China (suspensions during trade disputes - lobster Australia, soybeans US)

regulation_name: Hong Kong Public Health and Municipal Services Ordinance — Meat
regulation_ref: Public Health and Municipal Services Ordinance Cap 132 + Slaughterhouses Regulation + Meat Safety Regulations
enforcement_body: Food and Environmental Hygiene Department (FEHD) + Centre for Food Safety + Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department
key_requirements: HK FEHD regulates meat: imported chilled + frozen meat permitted from approved countries only (US, AU, NZ, BR, AR, UY, CL, NL, FR, ES, DE etc.); live cattle/pig imports from Mainland China via Sheung Shui Slaughterhouse; only government-licensed slaughterhouses operate (Sheung Shui + Tsuen Wan); FEHD pre-import notification; veterinary certificate origin country; CFS market sampling; food alert system
notes: HK = 'food deficient' city imports 95% food; meat from approved sources only; Mainland China live cattle pause due ASF + COVID complications; SE Asia + Americas + Europe major suppliers; high quality + safety standards

regulation_name: Hong Kong Food Adulteration (Metallic Contamination) + Pesticide Residue
regulation_ref: Cap 132V (Metallic Contamination) + Cap 132CM (Pesticide Residue) + CFS recall procedures
enforcement_body: Centre for Food Safety + FEHD + Government Laboratory
key_requirements: HK Food Adulteration regulations: maximum metallic contaminants (Pb, Hg, As, Cd) in meat; Pesticide Residue regulation 2014 (effective fully 2018) sets MRLs; meat MRLs particularly for veterinary residues (HK aligned with Codex CAC/RCP 61-2005 + EU + Australia); CFS Government Lab does verification testing; non-compliant shipments refused at port + recalled if reached market
notes: HK Pesticide Residue Regulation 2014 = comprehensive food safety reform; meat veterinary residues monitored separately; clenbuterol + ractopamine zero tolerance; ractopamine permitted US/CA pork creates trade issue (some shipments refused)

regulation_name: Taiwan Act Governing Food Safety and Sanitation — Meat Inspection
regulation_ref: Act Governing Food Safety and Sanitation art. 22 + COA Slaughter Sanitary Inspection Standards
enforcement_body: Taiwan FDA (TFDA) + Council of Agriculture (COA) + Bureau of Animal and Plant Health Inspection (BAPHI)
key_requirements: Taiwan: meat inspection at slaughter (COA/BAPHI veterinary); HACCP mandatory; CAS (Certified Agricultural Standards) voluntary; FMD-free with vaccination status; ASF surveillance; Pork ractopamine policy: relaxed 2021 (allows US pork imports with labelling); domestic prefers ractopamine-free; mandatory origin labelling pork + beef + chicken; restaurants must disclose meat origin since 2021
notes: Taiwan ractopamine pork relaxation 2021 controversial domestically; opposition cited food safety; opposition rallied protests; US pork imports increased; labelling 'Contains ractopamine' mandatory; FMD-free without vaccination achieved 2020

regulation_name: Taiwan CAS Premium Labeling + Origin Disclosure
regulation_ref: Agriculture Standards Quality Act + CAS scheme regulations + Regulations Governing Origin Labeling for Beef + Pork
enforcement_body: Council of Agriculture (COA) + TFDA + local government inspectors
key_requirements: Taiwan: CAS (Certified Agricultural Standards) voluntary premium label for meat (domestic + select imports); HACCP + traceability + quality requirements; CAS Pork, CAS Beef, CAS Chicken sub-categories; Origin Disclosure Regulations 2021: restaurants + supermarkets must label pork + beef origin (country); covers fresh, processed, prepared meat; QR code permitted; enforcement strong; political sensitivity high
notes: Taiwan CAS = premium consumer signal; growing penetration; origin labelling restaurants/retail since 2021 reaction to ractopamine US imports; political tool + consumer protection; food safety perception critical

regulation_name: Japan Food Sanitation Act — Meat Inspection
regulation_ref: Food Sanitation Act + Slaughterhouse Act (1953) + Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare orders
enforcement_body: MHLW + Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries (MAFF) + Quarantine Stations
key_requirements: Japan meat inspection: domestic slaughter under MHLW Slaughterhouse Act; foreign imports require approved establishment list (MAFF for animal disease + MHLW for food safety); HACCP mandatory since 2020; BSE controls maintained (cattle SRM removal); positive list system food additives; pesticide residue Positive List (2006) covers veterinary drugs; ractopamine + clenbuterol prohibited (zero tolerance); only US, AU, NZ, CA cattle eligible during BSE era expanded post-2018
notes: Japan post-BSE rules strict; aged cattle (>30 months) US restored 2005; BSE risk countries reapproved with conditions; ractopamine zero tolerance affects US pork imports; halal/kosher minor markets but growing; ASEAN beef imports increasing

regulation_ref: Law concerning Special Measures on Beef Traceability (2003) + Implementing Regulations
enforcement_body: MAFF + Center for Beef Traceability + prefectural governments
key_requirements: Japan Beef Cattle Traceability Law 2003: post-BSE response; individual cattle ID number; tracking birth to slaughter to retail; consumer can lookup individual cattle origin by ID number; mandatory bovine identification cards; integrated database; mandatory traceability extended to processing/retail; restaurants menu encouraged disclosure; imported beef requires equivalent traceability certification
notes: Japan beef traceability = world's most comprehensive consumer-facing; 11-digit individual ID number; consumer can verify on website; rebuild consumer confidence post-BSE 2001 outbreak

regulation_name: Korea Livestock Products Sanitary Control Act
regulation_ref: Livestock Products Sanitary Control Act + Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS) regulations + Animal and Plant Quarantine Agency (APQA)
enforcement_body: MFDS + APQA + Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs
key_requirements: Korea livestock products comprehensive safety regulation; HACCP mandatory; mandatory traceability cattle + pork + chicken (Beef Traceability Act 2008, Pork 2014); imported meat through APQA quarantine inspection + MFDS food safety; KFDA pre-import notification; ractopamine zero tolerance; growth hormone monitoring; FMD-free with vaccination cattle/pig + FMD-free without vaccination poultry; mad cow concerns historically blocked US beef
notes: Korea beef + pork traceability comprehensive; 12-digit ID; consumer mobile app verification; ractopamine zero tolerance limits US pork; FTA US (KORUS) beef imports increased; Hanwoo (Korean beef breed) premium domestic

regulation_name: Korea Country of Origin Labeling for Restaurants + Retail
regulation_ref: Country of Origin Labeling Act + Implementing Regulations 2008 + amendments
enforcement_body: Korea Customs Service + Korea Agro-Fisheries & Food Trade Corporation + Local Governments
key_requirements: Korea: mandatory country of origin labelling for restaurants + retail since 2008 (expanded over years); covers beef, pork, chicken, rice, fish; restaurants must display origin on menu + on premise; supermarket retail clear origin; cuisine made with multiple origins must disclose each; mandatory penalties for misrepresentation up to KRW 10M; consumer can report violations; mystery shopping audits
notes: Korea origin labelling restaurants pioneering global; consumer awareness very high; Hanwoo restaurant premium 3-5x imported; mandatory disclosure prevents fraud; sales of Hanwoo restaurants verified

regulation_name: Singapore Wholesome Meat and Fish Act + Standards
regulation_ref: Wholesome Meat and Fish Act (Cap 349A) + Sale of Food Act + SFA regulations
key_requirements: Singapore SFA imports meat from approved sources only (US, AU, NZ, BR, AR, JP, NL, DE, FR, ES, UK etc.); facility-by-facility approval; veterinary certificate required; SFA pre-import inspection; HACCP equivalent required at source; halal meat from JAKIM-approved facilities (for halal market); cold chain documentation; English labelling; allergen declaration; SFA random sampling at port + market
notes: Singapore 90%+ meat imported; SFA approved source list managed strictly; diverse origin (US beef, AU lamb, BR poultry, JP wagyu); halal market significant; SFA quick response to outbreaks (refusals during ASF, BSE)

regulation_name: Singapore '30 by 30' Food Security + Alternative Meat Regulations
regulation_ref: Sale of Food (Novel Food Sources) Regulations + SFA Novel Food framework + Cultivated Meat Approval (2020)
key_requirements: Singapore '30 by 30' strategy: 30% nutritional needs from local production by 2030; alternative meat approvals (cultivated meat first global approval Eat Just 2020); plant-based meat permitted (Beyond Meat, Impossible); insect protein authorized 2024; novel food regulatory framework; safety assessment for cell-cultured meat; transparent labelling required
notes: Singapore = first country approving cultivated meat (Dec 2020); regulatory innovation; cell-based chicken Eat Just/GOOD Meat available restaurants; insect protein 2024; food security driver

regulation_name: Malaysia Food Act 1983 + Food Regulations 1985 — Meat
regulation_ref: Food Act 1983 + Food Regulations 1985 Reg 360-365 + Department of Veterinary Services standards
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health Malaysia + Department of Veterinary Services + Malaysian Quarantine and Inspection Services (MAQIS)
key_requirements: Malaysia meat regulation; importation through MAQIS only; halal certification JAKIM mandatory for halal-claiming products; abattoirs approved Department of Veterinary Services; HACCP + GMP mandatory; non-halal meat (pork) handled separately + clearly labelled; mandatory labelling Bahasa Malaysia + English; allergen declaration; certain country approvals (US beef, BR halal poultry, AU lamb, NZ); FMD + ASF surveillance strict
notes: Malaysia halal certification JAKIM dominant in meat trade; foreign abattoirs halal-certified by JAKIM-approved bodies; non-halal section separate physical facility supermarket; pork market non-Muslim only

regulation_name: Malaysia JAKIM Halal Certification + MS 1500:2019 Halal Slaughter
regulation_ref: MS 1500:2019 Halal Food General Requirements + JAKIM Halal Manual + Trade Descriptions Act 2011
enforcement_body: Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (JAKIM) + Department of Standards Malaysia (DSM)
key_requirements: MS 1500:2019 = comprehensive halal food standard; covers slaughter, processing, packaging, storage, transport, retail; trained Muslim slaughtermen (Bismillah recitation); stunning permitted only reversible (mechanical, electric not penetrating); equipment dedicated halal; cross-contamination prevention; halal logo display mandatory; JAKIM approves foreign abattoirs; halal certification audit annual; Malaysia exports halal globally
notes: Malaysia JAKIM halal cert = gold standard globally; foreign meat plants pursue JAKIM certification for export to Malaysia + global halal markets; MS 1500 referenced internationally; Selangor halal hub

regulation_ref: Animal Slaughterhouses + Meat Product Distribution Act B.E. 2559 (2016) + Food Act B.E. 2522
enforcement_body: Thai Food and Drug Administration + Department of Livestock Development + Ministry of Public Health
key_requirements: Thailand 2016 slaughterhouse + meat distribution act modernization; standardized slaughter facilities; Department of Livestock Development inspection; HACCP increasingly mandatory exports; halal certification CICOT (Central Islamic Council of Thailand) for halal meat; FDA food safety; pork + chicken + beef + buffalo all covered; mandatory traceability for exports; FMD/ASF surveillance strong; antibiotics policy reducing AMR
notes: Thailand major chicken exporter (CPF major; tariff-quota agreements EU + Japan); pork production growing; halal certified plants for Middle East export market; FMD-free with vaccination some zones

regulation_name: Thai Food Safety Standards + Pesticide MRL Regulations
regulation_ref: TFDA Notifications 386 + 387 (food labels) + Ministerial Notification on Pesticide MRLs + Antimicrobial residues
enforcement_body: Thai FDA + Department of Livestock Development + ACFS (Bureau of Animal Standards)
key_requirements: Thailand TFDA labelling: Thai language mandatory; product name, ingredients, manufacturer/importer, origin, weight, manufacture/expiry; nutritional labelling for some prepared meat products; pesticide MRLs reformed 2020 (10,000+ Codex aligned); veterinary residue limits (chloramphenicol prohibited, ractopamine prohibited 2017); ACFS Bureau of Animal Standards for export certification
notes: Thailand ractopamine ban 2017 = trade impact; chicken exports require strict residue monitoring; Codex MRL alignment progressing; TQB (Thai Quality Beef) premium domestic; halal meat dedicated facilities

regulation_ref: Food Safety Law No. 55/2010/QH12 + Decree 15/2018/ND-CP + Circular 24/2019
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health + Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development + Vietnam Food Administration
key_requirements: Vietnam Food Safety Law comprehensive framework; product self-declaration system (Decree 15/2018 replaced pre-approval for most products); risk-based control; mandatory traceability (lo + mo); MARD + MOH joint oversight meat; abattoirs licensed; HACCP encouraged; veterinary residue limits (ractopamine prohibited 2018); FMD widespread + ASF outbreak 2019+ disrupted pork; chicken exports approved EU certain facilities
notes: Vietnam ASF outbreak 2019-2020 = 6M pigs destroyed; pork imports surged; recovery ongoing 2024; CPTPP + EVFTA reduce import duties; cattle imports from Australia + India growing; halal market for Muslim communities + Middle East export

regulation_name: Vietnam Veterinary Drug + Antimicrobial Residue Standards
enforcement_body: Department of Animal Health + Department of Plant Protection + MARD
key_requirements: Vietnam: veterinary drug regulation + antimicrobial residue monitoring; ractopamine prohibited 2018; growth hormones prohibited; clenbuterol zero tolerance; antibiotic withdrawal periods enforced; AMR National Action Plan 2017-2020 + extended; importers must declare residue compliance; FMD vaccination zones; pig blood serology FMD + classical swine fever monitoring
notes: Vietnam AMR efforts increasing; chicken raw export to EU/JP requires zero antibiotics during last 14 days; ractopamine ban affects US pork imports modestly; growth promoter ban universal

regulation_name: Indonesia BPOM Food Safety + Halal Mandatory Law
regulation_ref: Halal Product Assurance Law No. 33/2014 + Government Regulation 31/2019 + BPOM Regulation 31/2018 + Law 18/2012
enforcement_body: BPJPH (Halal Product Assurance Implementation Agency) + LPPOM-MUI + BPOM + Ministry of Agriculture
key_requirements: Indonesia Halal Product Assurance Law: mandatory halal certification ALL food products gradually rolled out (2024 deadline mandatory for meat); BPJPH government agency since 2017 replacing LPPOM-MUI exclusive; halal label mandatory on packaging; haram products explicit non-halal label; pork separated; foreign abattoirs LPPOM-MUI/BPJPH-recognized certifier required; significant export market for halal-certified
notes: Indonesia mandatory halal certification ALL meat + processed food + cosmetics deadline 2024-2026; BPJPH controls; foreign suppliers require recognized certifier (LPPOM-MUI, JAKIM, MUIS-SG, ISWA-AU etc.); halal export market opportunity for compliant producers

regulation_name: Indonesia Animal Health Law + Meat Import Regulations
regulation_ref: Law 18/2009 (Animal Husbandry and Animal Health) + Ministerial Regulations 14/2018 (meat imports)
enforcement_body: Ministry of Agriculture + Indonesian Quarantine Agency (IAQA) + Ministry of Trade
key_requirements: Indonesia meat import strict regulation; approved exporting country list narrow (Australia primarily, also NZ, US for non-halal beef, BR limited); facility-by-facility approval; quotas for beef (limited annually); halal certification mandatory; HALOM Halal slaughter audit; PPL Letter (Letter of Approval) per shipment; Bulog state procurement; ASF + FMD zoning strict; live cattle import dominant from AU
notes: Indonesia 3rd largest beef importer Asia; AU dominant supplier; live cattle for local slaughter (halal) major; PPL system controls volume + origin; halal mandatory affects all approved facilities; price controls under Bulog

regulation_name: Philippines FDA + Bureau of Animal Industry Meat Regulations
regulation_ref: Republic Act 9296 (Meat Inspection Code 2004) + RA 10611 (Food Safety Act 2013) + DA-BAI orders
enforcement_body: Department of Agriculture - Bureau of Animal Industry (BAI) + National Meat Inspection Service (NMIS) + FDA
key_requirements: Philippines RA 9296 Meat Inspection Code: NMIS inspects domestic meat establishments; AAA + AA + A + B + C classification slaughterhouses (Triple-A export, BBB local); HACCP mandatory AAA; halal certification voluntary growing for Mindanao + export; imported meat through BAI Veterinary Quarantine + FDA; BSE-free countries approved; FMD zoning; ASF response (2019+ ASF outbreak affected domestic pork); pork import surge
notes: Philippines ASF outbreak 2019+ devastated domestic pork; price spikes; pork imports increased dramatically (Spain, US, Canada, Brazil); halal certification CDA + IDCP regulated; export beef limited (mostly carabao + cattle)

regulation_ref: Republic Act 10817 (Philippine Halal Export Development and Promotion Act 2016) + DA Administrative Order on Halal Meat
enforcement_body: Department of Agriculture (DA) + Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) + Halal Affairs - Philippines
key_requirements: Philippines Halal Bill 2016: develop halal industry; National Commission on Muslim Filipinos coordinates; halal certification bodies accredited; halal meat domestic production + export market; Mindanao Halal hub; Bangsamoro Autonomous Region Muslim Mindanao; SAHC accreditation; export to OIC countries opportunity; mandatory disclosure of halal/non-halal in some retail settings
notes: Philippines halal market 5-6M Muslims; growing; export potential to ASEAN + Middle East; halal certification framework developing; mainland mostly non-halal but options expanding

regulation_name: India FSSAI Regulations on Meat — Compositional + Hygiene Standards
regulation_ref: Food Safety and Standards (Food Products Standards and Food Additives) Regulations 2011 + FSSR Meat amendments
enforcement_body: FSSAI (Food Safety and Standards Authority of India) + state food authorities + Department of Animal Husbandry
key_requirements: FSSAI Meat Regulations: definitions (fresh, frozen, processed, organ meats); composition standards; mandatory labelling English + Hindi/regional language; halal certification voluntary but commercially mandatory Muslim market; HACCP increasingly required; veterinary residue limits; export establishments BSE-free certification; Carabao buffalo meat major export (water buffalo from India significant global beef substitute); cow slaughter banned in most states (Hindu sentiment)
notes: India = world's largest buffalo meat (carabao) exporter; cow slaughter prohibited 24 of 28 states (Hindu religious); cattle vs buffalo distinction critical; vegetarian state populations significant; halal market for Muslims (15% population)

regulation_name: Indian State Cattle Slaughter Prohibition Laws
regulation_ref: State Cattle Preservation Acts (Maharashtra, Gujarat, UP, MP, Jharkhand, Karnataka, Bihar, Haryana, Punjab etc.)
enforcement_body: State governments + State Pollution Control Boards + Police
key_requirements: India state cattle slaughter laws: 24 states prohibit cow + bull slaughter (some include calves); buffalo slaughter permitted nationally; Gujarat life imprisonment for cow slaughter; UP + Haryana strict; Maharashtra extended ban; Karnataka 2020 enacted; significant impact on beef industry; meat traders harassed in some areas; vigilante violence against beef transport (lynchings reported); legal interstate transport challenging
notes: India cow slaughter prohibition major political + cultural issue; BJP-led states stricter; Kerala + West Bengal + NE states permit; meat industry adapted to buffalo-only model; significant legal + social tension; export buffalo meat = 'Indian beef' globally

regulation_ref: Export Control Act 2020 + Australian Export Meat Inspection System (AEMIS) + DAFF orders
enforcement_body: Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry (DAFF) + AQIS legacy + state authorities
key_requirements: Australia AEMIS: rigorous export meat inspection system; AQIS veterinarians + government meat inspectors at approved export establishments; HACCP mandatory; species-specific traceability (cattle NLIS, sheep NLIS-Sheep, pork PigPass); food safety standards Codex aligned + Country-specific; halal certification AHCS Australia + JAKIM + MUIS + others; major exports to China, US, Japan, Korea, EU, MENA, ASEAN
notes: Australia world's largest meat exporter per capita; AEMIS = export inspection backbone; NLIS cattle traceability since 1999 mandatory; halal-certified plants major for MENA + SE Asia exports; recent issues China suspensions trade tool

regulation_name: Australia LPA + NLIS Cattle Traceability System
regulation_ref: Livestock Production Assurance (LPA) program + National Livestock Identification System (NLIS) + Cattle Council of Australia rules
enforcement_body: Meat & Livestock Australia (MLA) + Cattle Council + Animal Health Australia + state agriculture
key_requirements: Australia LPA program voluntary but commercially essential; NLIS Cattle = mandatory radio frequency ID tags individual cattle since 1999; sheep + goat NLIS expanded; tracking birth to slaughter; livestock movement transfers electronically; vendor declarations + waybills; ASEL transport welfare; export markets (EU, China, US, JP, KR) require NLIS records; trace-back capability <24h for outbreaks
notes: Australia NLIS = world's most rigorous national cattle traceability; competitive advantage premium markets; biosecurity foundation for FMD-free status; PIC (Property Identification Code) on tags; LPA NVD covers food safety, biosecurity, animal welfare

regulation_name: New Zealand Animal Products Act + Risk Management Programmes
regulation_ref: Animal Products Act 1999 + Animal Products (Specifications) Notice + RMP requirements
enforcement_body: Ministry for Primary Industries (MPI) + MPI Verification Services
key_requirements: NZ Animal Products Act 1999 risk-based; mandatory Risk Management Programme (RMP) for export meat operators; HACCP equivalent + traceability; MPI Verification Services on-site inspectors; New Zealand Standard 4910 traceability; halal certified plants (Asure Quality, FIANZ certifier); export to US, China, UK, Korea, MENA, ASEAN, EU; FMD-free without vaccination = premium markets eligibility
notes: NZ = 4th largest red meat exporter (after AU, US, BR); FMD-free without vaccination critical; ANZ Beef + Lamb sustainability initiatives; >90% red meat exported; OIE/WOAH negligible BSE risk status

regulation_name: NZ National Animal Identification and Tracing (NAIT)
regulation_ref: NAIT Act 2012 + NAIT Regulations + OSPRI New Zealand
enforcement_body: OSPRI New Zealand (Operational Solutions for Primary Industries) + MPI + farmers
key_requirements: NZ NAIT mandatory cattle + deer individual identification since 2012; RFID ear tags; movement reporting OSPRI online + app; biosecurity foundation; M. bovis outbreak 2017-2018 demonstrated value; failure to NAIT can be prosecuted; integrated with national disease control; ENZA (Earthworm New Zealand Agriculture) and other programs
notes: NZ NAIT critical biosecurity tool; M. bovis (Mycoplasma bovis) eradication campaign 2017-2024 succeeded via NAIT tracking; world-first eradication of M. bovis at national level; cost NZD 880M but FMD prevention worth multiples

regulation_name: ASEAN Framework on Animal Health + Biosecurity Cooperation
regulation_ref: ASEAN Animal Health Strategic Framework + ASEAN Cooperation on Livestock + ASEAN Sectoral Working Groups
enforcement_body: ASEAN Secretariat + ASEAN Sectoral Working Group on Livestock + national veterinary services
key_requirements: ASEAN framework animal health coordination: FMD vaccination program; ASF coordination 2019+ outbreak; HPAI (avian influenza) surveillance; transboundary disease management; mutual recognition certifications evolving; ATIGA reduces intra-ASEAN tariffs meat; halal trade facilitation for Indonesia + Malaysia + Brunei + Southern Thailand + Southern Philippines markets; ASEAN Single Window food trade
notes: ASEAN Bandung Action Plan 2015 + Vientiane 2020 livestock cooperation; ASF 2019+ devastated regional pork (VN, PH, ID, MY); FMD eradication strategy Mekong region; ASEAN halal cooperation framework strengthening trade

regulation_name: ASEAN Harmonized Tariff Nomenclature (AHTN) for Meat
regulation_ref: AHTN 2022 + ASEAN Single Window + national customs
key_requirements: AHTN harmonizes HS codes intra-ASEAN at 8-digit level for meat: 0201 (beef fresh/chilled), 0202 (beef frozen), 0203 (pork), 0204 (sheep/goat), 0207 (poultry); preferential tariffs Form D under ATIGA; ASEAN cumulation rules; e-Form D operational; halal meat HS coding informal; trade facilitation electronic documentation
notes: AHTN simplifies regional meat trade; intra-ASEAN meat trade growing (TH, VN, MY exporting; ID, PH, SG importing); halal supply chain ASEAN-Middle East-Africa expanding

regulation_name: GCC GSO Halal Slaughter Standard + Mandatory Halal Certification
regulation_ref: GSO 993:2015 Halal slaughter + GSO 2055 Halal Food General Requirements + GSO 9 Labelling
enforcement_body: GCC Standardization Organization (GSO) + national authorities (ESMA UAE, SFDA Saudi, SASO etc.)
key_requirements: GCC halal slaughter mandatory: GSO 993:2015 specifies halal slaughter requirements; Muslim slaughterman; tasmiya (Bismillah ar-Rahman ar-Rahim); sharp knife; throat cut single stroke; complete bleeding; conscious animal preferred (stunning electric + non-penetrating mechanical acceptable to some authorities); GSO 2055 halal food general requirements; halal certifier accredited (HACS); foreign abattoirs registered with GCC authorities
notes: GCC = critical halal market; foreign abattoirs (BR, AU, NZ, US, IN) must be halal-certified by GCC-recognized bodies; HACS scheme harmonizes accreditation; significant export opportunity

regulation_name: Saudi Arabia SFDA Food Import Requirements + Halal Authentication
regulation_ref: SFDA Imports Regulations + ISO 22000 GFSI recognition + SFDA Food Import Track and Trace
key_requirements: Saudi Arabia (largest GCC market) SFDA mandatory pre-import notification; halal certification required (SLCC accredited certifier); facility-by-facility approval; SFDA inspection foreign facilities periodically; e-Tracking system; Saudi-specific requirements; SASO conformity certificate (SABER platform 2019+); reformed under Vision 2030 modernization; Madinah + Mecca pilgrim food critical attention
notes: Saudi SFDA = largest GCC food authority; SABER platform 2019 streamlined imports; SLCC accreditation tightened halal certification; Vision 2030 food security driving local production growth

regulation_name: Israel Kashrut Laws + Beef Importation Rabbinical Authority
regulation_ref: Kashrut Fraud Prevention Law 1983 + Chief Rabbinate of Israel kashrut regulations + Beef Importation Reform 2008
enforcement_body: Chief Rabbinate of Israel + Ministry of Agriculture + Ministry of Health
key_requirements: Israel kosher certification commercially essential >95% market; Chief Rabbinate dominant authority; private OU + OK + Star-K acceptable for export-import; kosher slaughter (shechita) by certified shochet; specific salting + soaking process; hindquarters generally not kosher in Israel (foreign suppliers handle separately); 2008 reform opened beef imports beyond traditional sources; major suppliers South America, Eastern Europe, Australia
notes: Israel kosher meat industry sophisticated; shechita banned several European countries (BE, DK, SE) impacts supply; South American kosher facilities major suppliers (Marfrig, JBS, AdeCoAgro); Glatt kosher premium; Halal demand growing minor Israeli Arab + tourist market

regulation_name: Israel Import Permits + Tnuva Cooperative Reforms
regulation_ref: Plant Protection and Animal Diseases Imports Act + 2014 Antitrust Reform of Tnuva monopoly
enforcement_body: Israel Veterinary Services + Ministry of Health + Antitrust Authority
key_requirements: Israel meat import permits via Veterinary Services; pre-import inspection of foreign facilities; FMD-free + BSE-free source countries; ractopamine prohibited; specific halacha (kosher) compliant supply chain; previously Tnuva controlled 70% market - antitrust reform 2014 increased competition; imported beef share grew; price controls some items; Yom Kippur shutdowns full sector
notes: Israel Tnuva reform increased import competition; consumer prices reduced; multiple importers (Soglovek, Tnuva, Zoglovek, Strauss); religious observance affects seasonal demand

regulation_name: Egypt General Authority for Veterinary Services + Halal Slaughter
regulation_ref: Decree 88/1991 + Egyptian Veterinary Services regulations + Al-Azhar Halal Standards
enforcement_body: GOEIC (General Organization for Export Import Control) + Egyptian Veterinary Services + Al-Azhar
key_requirements: Egypt meat imports through GOEIC; veterinary certificate origin country; halal certification mandatory (Al-Azhar or recognized); FMD + BSE status critical; reformed import processes 2019+; live cattle imports major from Sudan, Ethiopia, Brazil; major frozen beef imports from Brazil, Argentina, India (buffalo), Sudan; HACCP increasingly required; halal hub for Africa
notes: Egypt = major meat importer; live cattle from East Africa + Brazil; frozen beef Brazil + Argentina + India buffalo; halal certification central; price subsidies social bread/meat program; Ramadan demand spike

regulation_name: Egypt Food Safety Authority (NFSA) + Halal Certification Reform
regulation_ref: Law 1/2017 (NFSA establishment) + NFSA implementing regulations + Egyptian Halal Certification Body
enforcement_body: National Food Safety Authority (NFSA) + Egyptian Standards + Al-Azhar al-Sharif
key_requirements: Egypt NFSA established 2017 modernizing food safety; consolidated previously fragmented oversight; HACCP requirements rolling out; halal certification reform increasing scrutiny; Egyptian Halal Certification Center growing recognition; Egyptian standards aligning Codex/EU; imports inspection at port; refrigerated transport regulations; consumer protection meat origin disclosure
notes: Egypt NFSA = post-2017 institutional reform; consolidating food safety; halal certification commercial advantage Africa + Middle East; CICOT-like Egyptian body emerging

regulation_ref: Loi 28-07 (Securite Sanitaire Produits Alimentaires) + Decret 2-10-473 + Arretes ONSSA
enforcement_body: Office National de Securite Sanitaire des Produits Alimentaires (ONSSA) + ministere agriculture
key_requirements: Maroc ONSSA controle viandes; abattoirs agrees ONSSA; abattage halal obligatoire conforme rite malekite + procedures HACCP; chaine froid 4C; tracabilite obligatoire bovins (DNTABov - Dispositif National Traceabilite Bovins); etiquetage francais + arabe obligatoire; importation via ONSSA agrement; FTV exemption EU-Maroc accord agricole; production bovins atlasiens cattle race autochtone
notes: Maroc ONSSA modernise 2010 controle alimentaire; abattage halal universel; importation vers UE certain abattoirs agrees; ovins de l'Aid el-Kebir importation massive annuelle 5M+ moutons; consumption rouge viande croissant

regulation_name: Maroc Loi 49-99 et Programme National Identification Bovine
regulation_ref: Loi 49-99 (sante animale + identification) + Decret 2-04-684 + Programme DNTABov
enforcement_body: ONSSA + Ministere Agriculture + Federation Interprofessionnelle Bovine
key_requirements: Maroc Loi 49-99: identification + tracabilite bovins; boucle auriculaire + microchip optional; base DNTABov; surveillance epizooties (PPR, FMD, BSE); vaccination FMD obligatoire; export limite a UE + certains pays africains; race autochtone Sardi mouton + Atlas bovin labellisees AOC marocaines
notes: Maroc DNTABov modernisation tracabilite bovins 2010+; AOC viande Sardi + Beni-Hsen reconnues; FMD vaccination universelle; PPR (peste petits ruminants) surveillance africaine

regulation_name: South Africa Meat Safety Act + Independent Meat Inspector Programme
regulation_ref: Meat Safety Act 40 of 2000 + Regulations + Independent Meat Inspector framework
enforcement_body: Department of Agriculture, Land Reform and Rural Development (DALRRD) + Provincial Authorities + ARC
key_requirements: South Africa MSA 2000 modernized meat sector; HACCP mandatory abattoirs; classification: A (export + national), B (provincial), C (local), D (small-scale); Independent Meat Inspector (IMI) system; halal certification SANHA + NIHT + MJC accredited; export-approved facilities to EU, Middle East, Asia; FMD-free with vaccination some zones; recent FMD outbreaks (2019, 2022) suspended exports temporarily; BSE-free
notes: SA major beef + lamb exporter Africa + Middle East; FMD outbreaks 2019 + 2022 suspended exports China + Middle East; SANHA halal cert major; Karoo Lamb GI registered 2014 first SA meat GI

regulation_ref: SAMIC (South African Meat Industry Company) standards + Karoo Meat of Origin scheme + Free-Range schemes
key_requirements: SAMIC = industry quality body; sets grading standards (A, AB, B, C; Super A, A, AB, B); Karoo Meat of Origin GI scheme protected geographical indication; Free-Range Beef scheme; pastured pork; export quality marks; consumer guidance; quality assurance audits voluntary but commercially essential
notes: SA Karoo Lamb GI = first SA meat origin-protected; Karoo Meat of Origin scheme growing; free-range market premium growing; ostrich meat SA significant export niche

regulation_name: Nigeria NAFDAC Meat Regulations + Veterinary Council Inspection
regulation_ref: NAFDAC Act + Pre-packaged Food Labelling Regulations + Nigeria Veterinary Council inspection
enforcement_body: NAFDAC + Veterinary Council of Nigeria + Federal Department of Animal Husbandry Services
key_requirements: Nigeria meat regulation: NAFDAC controls processed meat + retail; veterinary inspection at abattoirs (federal + state); NIVET (Nigerian Institute of Veterinary Technology); halal certification voluntary but commercially essential 50%+ Muslim population (NSCIA = Nigerian Supreme Council for Islamic Affairs); poultry industry growing; cattle pastoralist Fulani + crisis affects supply; ASF outbreaks intermittent; FMD endemic
notes: Nigeria largest African beef consumer; pastoralist cattle production dominates (>70% cattle); abattoir capacity limited; cold chain weak rural; informal sector dominant; modernization slow

regulation_name: Nigeria ECOWAS Livestock Trade Protocol + Halal Standards
regulation_ref: ECOWAS Treaty + Livestock Trade Protocol + NSCIA Halal Standards
key_requirements: ECOWAS livestock trade protocol facilitates Nigeria's role as major regional importer; live cattle imports from Niger, Chad, Burkina Faso, Mali significant; NSCIA halal standards developing; informal cross-border trade dominant; FMD + RVF + ASF crossover risk; Sambisa + Lake Chad insecurity disrupts cattle routes; ranches modernization slow
notes: Nigeria pastoralist cattle = 95% supply; insecurity in Middle Belt cattle-belt + Sahel; informal trade evades regulations; modernization via private sector (Niger Foods, Olam, Dangote Group)

regulation_name: Kenya Meat Control Act + KEBS Standards
regulation_ref: Meat Control Act Cap 356 + KS 2479 (Hygiene Meat Products) + Department of Veterinary Services
enforcement_body: Department of Veterinary Services + KEBS + County Governments
key_requirements: Kenya Meat Control Act + KS 2479; abattoir registration + inspection; HACCP mandatory export facilities; halal certification (Kenya Halal Bureau, SUPKEM); county-level retail butcher licensing; cold chain limited rural; pastoralist cattle (Masai, Turkana, Somali) major supply; ASF + FMD + RVF + ECF cattle diseases endemic; export to UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, growing China
notes: Kenya = major East African beef exporter; pastoralist supply chain modernizing; Naivasha + Athi River abattoirs major; Kenyan camel meat growing niche export to Gulf; mutton + chevon (goat) significant

regulation_name: Kenya Bureau of Standards + East African Community Harmonization
regulation_ref: EAC Standards EAS 16 (Pre-packaged Foods Labelling) + KS 1234 + KS 2479
key_requirements: Kenya KEBS + EAC harmonized standards meat: EAS 16 labelling; EAS 39 meat hygiene; mutual recognition Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, Burundi, S. Sudan; reduces duplication; EAC Customs Union zero internal tariff; live cattle movement controlled foot-and-mouth zones; abattoir Bovine ear-tag traceability piloting; export quality marks shared
notes: Kenya EAC trade integration progressing; cattle movement EAC zones controlled; KEBS market surveillance; mobile market controls; counterfeit cuts emerging issue

regulation_name: Codex Code of Hygienic Practice for Meat + CXC 58
regulation_ref: Codex CXC 58-2005 (Code of Hygienic Practice for Meat) + CXG 79 (Microbiological Risk Assessment) + CXS 192 (food additives)
key_requirements: Codex CXC 58 = international reference for meat hygiene from primary production to retail; risk-based approach; ante/post-mortem inspection requirements; HACCP application; allergen control; cold chain; traceability; basis for many national regulations; sanitary/phytosanitary measures under WTO must comply with Codex (presumption of compliance under SPS Agreement); maximum residue limits (CXM)
notes: Codex CXC 58 = global meat hygiene reference; WTO SPS Agreement gives Codex strong influence; national authorities cite Codex for justification; OIE/WOAH animal health complementary; veterinary residue MRLs via JECFA evaluations

regulation_name: Codex Maximum Residue Limits (CXM) for Veterinary Drugs
regulation_ref: CXM-7 + JECFA evaluations + Codex Committee on Residues of Veterinary Drugs in Foods (CCRVDF)
enforcement_body: Codex Alimentarius Commission + JECFA + national authorities
key_requirements: Codex maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs in food animal products; CCRVDF reviews JECFA evaluations; antimicrobial MRLs (chloramphenicol prohibited, oxytetracycline limits, etc.); growth promoters (ractopamine controversial - permitted by Codex but banned EU/CN/RU/TW); BST (bovine somatotropin), beta-agonists, hormones; international harmonization basis; WTO trade dispute reference (US-EU hormones)
notes: Codex MRLs binding for international trade SPS reference; ractopamine politically divisive (Codex MRL 2012 contested EU/CN/RU); hormone treated beef US-EU long-standing dispute; meat exporters monitor Codex updates

regulation_name: ISO 22005 — Traceability in feed and food chain
key_requirements: ISO 22005 = principles + requirements for design + implementation of feed/food traceability systems; applicable across supply chain; product tracking + tracing; documentation; verification + auditing; supports ISO 22000 food safety + ISO 9001 quality; voluntary but commercially essential meat industry export; many national meat traceability systems align with ISO 22005
notes: ISO 22005 = framework standard widely adopted; meat industry traceability systems (Australia NLIS, Canada CCIA, Brazil SISBOV, EU TRACES) reference ISO principles; export markets expect compliance

regulation_name: ISO 17604 + ISO 17410 — Microbiological Methods for Meat
regulation_ref: ISO 17604:2015 (carcass sampling) + ISO 17410:2019 + ISO 6579 (Salmonella) + ISO 16649 (E. coli)
enforcement_body: ISO TC 34 SC 9 (microbiology of food chain)
key_requirements: ISO microbiological method standards for meat: ISO 17604 carcass sampling for E. coli + total viable count; ISO 6579 Salmonella detection; ISO 11290 Listeria monocytogenes; ISO 21528 Enterobacteriaceae; ISO 4833 aerobic colony count; methods harmonized internationally; regulatory authorities + commercial labs use; AOAC alternatives in US;PCR-based ISO methods emerging
notes: ISO microbiological methods backbone meat safety testing; lab accreditation (ISO 17025) verifies competence; new pathogen methods (STEC molecular) standardized; commercial rapid methods AOAC-PAS or ISO-validated

regulation_name: UNECE Beef Carcasses and Cuts Standard
regulation_ref: UNECE Standard for Bovine Meat (Carcasses and Cuts) + UN/CEFACT trade documentation
enforcement_body: UNECE Working Party on Agricultural Quality Standards
key_requirements: UNECE standardized definitions + classification for bovine + porcine + ovine + caprine + camelid + chicken carcasses + cuts; trade descriptions; quality classes; presentation requirements; sampling; supports international meat trade harmonization; widely used in commercial contracts; member states 56 including major meat exporters/importers; voluntary but influential trade reference
notes: UNECE meat standards = international trade reference; commercial sales contracts reference UNECE codes; coding system enables clear cross-border commercial specifications; updates ongoing for novel cuts

regulation_name: UNECE Geneva Convention on Carriage of Perishable Foodstuffs (ATP)
regulation_ref: Agreement on the International Carriage of Perishable Foodstuffs and on the Special Equipment to be Used for such Carriage (ATP) 1970
enforcement_body: UNECE Inland Transport Committee + national authorities
key_requirements: UNECE ATP Agreement: international transport of perishable foodstuffs including meat; equipment classification (insulated, refrigerated, mechanically refrigerated, heated); type approval transport equipment; testing standards for thermal performance; meat: max -18C frozen, -12C 'frigorific'; certificate ATP renewal 6 years; mandatory for cross-border refrigerated transport in 50+ contracting states
notes: UNECE ATP = international refrigerated transport framework; meat trucks/containers ATP-certified; testing stations across signatory countries; failed ATP can prevent border crossing meat shipments

regulation_name: WADA Prohibited Substances + Meat Contamination Risks

notes: Battery Regulation 2023/1542 game-changer; removable batteries 2027 transformative for smartphone design; battery passport mandatory; recycled content lithium/cobalt minimum %

regulation_name: EU MDR 2017/745 Class I Self-Certification + Vigilance
enforcement_body: EC DG SANTE + Notified Bodies + Member State competent authorities
key_requirements: EU MDR: Class I medical devices self-certification; manufacturer declaration of conformity + technical documentation; CE marking; UDI Unique Device Identification mandatory; EUDAMED database registration; post-market surveillance + Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR); vigilance reporting serious incidents within 15 days
notes: MDR 2017/745 effective 26 May 2021; transition extended for some Class I sterile/measuring; adhesive bandages Class I (sterile = Class Is); UDI mandatory all classes

regulation_name: EU MDR Annex I + ISO 10993 Biocompatibility + EN ISO 11607 Sterilization
regulation_ref: MDR 2017/745 Annex I (General Safety and Performance Requirements) + ISO 10993 + EN ISO 11607

notes: MDR Annex I rigorous safety + performance demonstration; ISO 10993 biocompatibility testing extensive; sterilization validation per type

regulation_ref: Regulation (EC) 648/2004 + Annex II surfactants biodegradability + OECD 301-310
enforcement_body: EC DG GROW + Member State authorities
key_requirements: EU Detergent Regulation 648/2004: surfactants must be ultimately biodegradable (>60% per OECD 301 tests); ingredient labelling mandatory; perfumes/preservatives allergens declaration; concentrate restrictions; manufacturer responsibility; SDS available; recent reform proposal 2023+ for new substances + sustainability
notes: Detergent Reg 648/2004 cornerstone; surfactant biodegradability mandatory; ongoing reform 2023+ Commission proposal

regulation_name: EU Phosphate Restriction + Liquid Pod Child-Resistant Packaging
regulation_ref: Regulation (EU) 259/2012 (phosphate ban in laundry detergents) + Regulation (EU) 2017/1273 (liquid laundry detergent capsules)
enforcement_body: EC DG GROW + Member State market surveillance
key_requirements: EU phosphate ban in consumer laundry detergents (effective 30 June 2013) + dishwasher detergents (1 Jan 2017); Regulation 2017/1273 mandatory child-resistant packaging for liquid laundry detergent capsules (pods): opaque packaging, child-resistant fastening, repulsive aversive agent on capsule, warnings; response to pediatric poisoning incidents
notes: Phosphate ban + pod safety regs landmark consumer protection; pediatric ingestion incidents drove pod regulation 2017

regulation_name: Code de l'Environnement Detergents Capsules France
regulation_ref: Code Environnement L541-10-9 (REP) + Decret 2017-XX detergents + Loi AGEC
key_requirements: France REP (responsabilite elargie producteur) detergents emballages; eco-organisme Citeo recycle emballages capsules lessive; eco-modulation tarifaire (penalise PVOH film non-recycle); Loi AGEC interdit micro-plastiques rincage (effets brosse a dents) - capsules PVOH soumises pression reglementaire alternatives; consumer information
notes: France Citeo REP emballages; capsules PVOH film polyvinyl alcohol = nano-plastique en discussion; alternatives en developpement

regulation_name: Code Sante Publique Capsules Lessive Securite Enfants France
regulation_ref: Code Sante Publique + Reglement UE 2017/1273 + Recommandations DGS
key_requirements: France implementation Reg UE 2017/1273 capsules lessive: emballage opaque, ferme securite enfants, agent amer aversif (Bitrex) sur capsule, avertissement; pediatrics emergencies post-2010 inquietants; Centres antipoison surveillance; Sanofi/Reckitt/P&G compliance; sanctions DGCCRF rappel
notes: France securite capsules enfants implementee strictement; Centres antipoison < 50 cas/an post-reglement; rappels rares
